revision.comment: 新しいページ：甲塚古墳（京都府京都市にある古墳）。
revision.text:
{{日本の古墳 |名称 = 甲塚古墳 |画像 = [[File:Kabutozuka Kofun (Kyoto), entrance.jpg|280px]]
石室開口部 |別名 = 冑塚/兜塚/大塚{{Sfn|冑塚（平凡社）|1979}} |所属 = |所在地 = [[京都府]][[京都市]][[右京区]]嵯峨甲塚町 |緯度度 = 35|緯度分 = 1|緯度秒 = 10.80 |経度度 = 135|経度分 = 41|経度秒 = 24.85 |ISO = JP-26 |形状 = [[円墳]] |規模 = 直径38m以上
高さ5.5m以上 |埋葬施設 = 両袖式[[横穴式石室]] |出土品 = |陪塚 = |築造時期 = [[7世紀]]前半 |被葬者 = （推定）[[秦氏]]一族 |陵墓 = |史跡 = なし |有形文化財 = |特記事項 = |地図 = Japan Kyoto city |アイコン = 円墳 |ラベル = 甲塚古墳 |ラベル位置 = top }} '''甲塚古墳'''（かぶとづかこふん）は、[[京都府]][[京都市]][[右京区]]嵯峨甲塚町にある[[古墳]]。形状は[[円墳]]。史跡指定はされていない。 == 概要 ==
{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+太秦・嵯峨野地域の主な古墳{{Sfn|広瀬和雄|2013}} !期!!古墳名!!形状!!規模!!石室全長!!築造時期!!史跡指定 |- | ||[[垂箕山古墳]]||[[前方後円墳]]||65m|| ||5c末-6c初頭||[[宮内庁]]治定墓 |- |rowspan=2|3||rowspan=2|[[天塚古墳]]||rowspan=2|前方後円墳||rowspan=2|73m||(8.1m)||rowspan=2|6c前半||rowspan=2|国の[[史跡]] |- |(7.7m) |- |rowspan=4|4
・
5||[[清水山古墳]]||前方後円墳||57m|| ||rowspan=2|6c中葉-後半||（消滅） |- |段ノ山古墳||前方後円墳|| || ||（消滅） |- |双ヶ岡1号墳||[[円墳]]||45m||14.6m||rowspan=2|6世紀後半||なし |- |衣笠山1号墳||円墳||26m||(6.0m)||なし |- |rowspan=6|6||[[蛇塚古墳]]||前方後円墳||75m||17.8m||rowspan=6|6c末-7c初頭||国の史跡 |- |円山古墳
（大覚寺1号墳）||円墳||50m||14.7m||宮内庁[[陵墓参考地]] |- |狐塚古墳
（大覚寺4号墳）||方墳||28m||(12.8m)||なし |- |入道塚古墳
（大覚寺2号墳）||[[方墳]]||30m|||11.2m||宮内庁陵墓参考地 |- |南天塚古墳
（大覚寺3号墳）||円墳||25-30m||8.1m||（消滅） |- |御堂ヶ池1号墳||円墳||30m||(8.3m)||京都市登録史跡 |- |rowspan=2|7||'''甲塚古墳'''||円墳||38m||14.4m||rowspan=2|7c前半||なし |- |広沢古墳||円墳||30m||12.0m||なし |}
[[京都盆地]]（山城盆地）西部、嵯峨野台地上に築造された単独の大型円墳である。古墳名は冑の形をした墳丘外観に由来し、現在は石室奥に祠が祀られている{{Sfn|冑塚（平凡社）|1979}}。これまでに盗掘に遭っているほか、発掘調査は実施されていない。 墳形は円形で、直径38メートル以上・高さ5.5メートル以上を測る{{Sfn|広瀬和雄|2013}}。埋葬施設は両袖式の[[横穴式石室]]で、南方向に開口する。石室全長14.4メートルを測る大型石室であり、石材には巨石が使用される。副葬品は詳らかでない{{Sfn|冑塚（平凡社）|1979}}。 築造時期は、[[古墳時代]][[終末期古墳|終末期]]の[[7世紀]]前半頃と推定される{{Sfn|広瀬和雄|2013}}。太秦・嵯峨野地域の古墳のうちでは、[[蛇塚古墳]]・円山古墳に後続する最後の首長墓に位置づけられ{{Sfn|広瀬和雄|2013}}、嵯峨野一帯を開発した渡来系氏族の[[秦氏]]との関連を示唆する古墳として注目される。 == 埋葬施設 == 埋葬施設としては両袖式[[横穴式石室]]が構築されており、南方向に開口する。石室の規模は次の通り{{Sfn|広瀬和雄|2013}}。 * 石室全長：14.4メートル * 玄室：長さ5.2メートル、幅2.9メートル、現在高さ3.4メートル * 羨道：長さ9.2メートル、幅1.7メートル、現在高さ1.8メートル 玄室の奥壁は2段積みと推定され（祠が存在するため明確としない）、側壁は2石の2段積み、前壁は1段積みによって構築される。玄門部は1石、羨道の側壁は2段積みである。天井石は、玄室では3石、羨道では4石{{Sfn|広瀬和雄|2013}}。 File:Kabutozuka Kofun (Kyoto), 3D-1.jpg|石室俯瞰図
{{small|左から、羨道・玄室。}} File:Kabutozuka Kofun (Kyoto), 3D-2.jpg|石室展開図 File:Kabutozuka Kofun (Kyoto), genshitsu-1.jpg|玄室（奥壁方向） File:Kabutozuka Kofun (Kyoto), genshitsu-2.jpg|玄室（開口部方向） File:Kabutozuka Kofun (Kyoto), sendou-2.jpg|羨道（開口部方向） File:Kabutozuka Kofun (Kyoto), sendou-1.jpg|羨道（玄室方向） File:Kabutozuka Kofun (Kyoto), funkyu.jpg|墳丘 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == 参考文献 == {{small|（記事執筆に使用した文献）}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1979|chapter=冑塚|title=[[日本歴史地名大系]] 27 京都市の地名|publisher=[[平凡社]]|isbn=978-4-582-49027-5|ref={{Harvid|冑塚（平凡社）|1979}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=京都市|author=|year=1983|chapter=甲塚古墳|title=史料京都の歴史 第2巻 考古|publisher=[[平凡社]]|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|史料京都の歴史 第2巻 考古|1983}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|author=[[広瀬和雄]]|year=2013|chapter=山城・蛇塚古墳をめぐる二,三の問題|title=国立歴史民俗博物館研究報告 第178集|publisher=[[国立歴史民俗博物館]]|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|広瀬和雄|2013}}}} == 関連文献 == {{small|（記事執筆に使用していない関連文献）}} * {{Cite journal|和書|author=|title=右京区甲塚町兜塚古墳実測図付記|date=1964|journal=とれんち|publisher=京都大学考古学研究会|volume=|number=11|pages=|url=|naid=|doi=|ref=}} == 外部リンク == * {{Commonscat-inline|Kabutozuka Kofun (Kyoto)}} {{DEFAULTSORT:かふとつかこふん きようとし}} [[Category:京都府の古墳]] [[Category:右京区の歴史]] [[Category:円墳]] [[Category:秦氏|墓かふとつかこふん]] [[Category:太秦]]

[[京都盆地]]（山城盆地）西部、嵯峨野台地上に築造された単独の大型円墳である。古墳名は冑の形をした墳丘外観に由来し、現在は石室奥に祠が祀られている{{Sfn|冑塚（平凡社）|1979}}。これまでに盗掘に遭っているほか、発掘調査は実施されていない。 墳形は円形で、直径38メートル以上・高さ5.5メートル以上を測る{{Sfn|広瀬和雄|2013}}。埋葬施設は両袖式の[[横穴式石室]]で、南方向に開口する。石室全長14.4メートルを測る大型石室であり、石材には巨石が使用される。副葬品は詳らかでない{{Sfn|冑塚（平凡社）|1979}}。 築造時期は、[[古墳時代]][[終末期古墳|終末期]]の[[7世紀]]前半頃と推定される{{Sfn|広瀬和雄|2013}}。太秦・嵯峨野地域の古墳のうちでは、[[蛇塚古墳]]・円山古墳に後続する最後の首長墓に位置づけられ{{Sfn|広瀬和雄|2013}}、嵯峨野一帯を開発した渡来系氏族の[[秦氏]]との関連を示唆する古墳として注目される。 == 埋葬施設 == 埋葬施設としては両袖式[[横穴式石室]]が構築されており、南方向に開口する。石室の規模は次の通り{{Sfn|広瀬和雄|2013}}。 * 石室全長：14.4メートル * 玄室：長さ5.2メートル、幅2.9メートル、現在高さ3.4メートル * 羨道：長さ9.2メートル、幅1.7メートル、現在高さ1.8メートル 玄室の奥壁は2段積みと推定され（祠が存在するため明確としない）、側壁は2石の2段積み、前壁は1段積みによって構築される。玄門部は1石、羨道の側壁は2段積みである。天井石は、玄室では3石、羨道では4石{{Sfn|広瀬和雄|2013}}。 File:Kabutozuka Kofun (Kyoto), 3D-1.jpg|石室俯瞰図

[[京都盆地]]（山城盆地）西部、嵯峨野台地上に築造された単独の大型円墳である。古墳名は冑の形をした墳丘外観に由来し、現在は石室奥に祠が祀られている{{Sfn|冑塚（平凡社）|1979}}。これまでに盗掘に遭っているほか、発掘調査は実施されていない。 墳形は円形で、直径38メートル以上・高さ5.5メートル以上を測る{{Sfn|広瀬和雄|2013}}。埋葬施設は両袖式の[[横穴式石室]]で、南方向に開口する。石室全長14.4メートルを測る大型石室であり、石材には巨石が使用される。副葬品は詳らかでない{{Sfn|冑塚（平凡社）|1979}}。 築造時期は、[[古墳時代]][[終末期古墳|終末期]]の[[7世紀]]前半頃と推定される{{Sfn|広瀬和雄|2013}}。太秦・嵯峨野地域の古墳のうちでは、[[蛇塚古墳]]・円山古墳に後続する最後の首長墓に位置づけられ{{Sfn|広瀬和雄|2013}}、嵯峨野一帯を開発した渡来系氏族の[[秦氏]]との関連を示唆する古墳として注目される。 古墳域は、現在では個人敷地内で保存されている。 == 埋葬施設 == 埋葬施設としては両袖式[[横穴式石室]]が構築されており、南方向に開口する。石室の規模は次の通り{{Sfn|広瀬和雄|2013}}。 * 石室全長：14.4メートル * 玄室：長さ5.2メートル、幅2.9メートル、現在高さ3.4メートル * 羨道：長さ9.2メートル、幅1.7メートル、現在高さ1.8メートル 玄室の奥壁は2段積みと推定され（祠が存在するため明確としない）、側壁は2石の2段積み、前壁は1段積みによって構築される。玄門部は1石、羨道の側壁は2段積みである。天井石は、玄室では3石、羨道では4石{{Sfn|広瀬和雄|2013}}。 File:Kabutozuka Kofun (Kyoto), 3D-1.jpg|石室俯瞰図

[[京都盆地]]（山城盆地）西部、嵯峨野台地上に築造された単独の大型円墳である。古墳名は冑の形をした墳丘外観に由来し、現在は石室奥に祠が祀られている{{Sfn|冑塚（平凡社）|1979}}。これまでに盗掘に遭っているほか、発掘調査は実施されていない。 墳形は円形で、直径38メートル以上・高さ5.5メートル以上を測る{{Sfn|広瀬和雄|2013}}。埋葬施設は両袖式の[[横穴式石室]]で、南方向に開口する。石室全長14.4メートルを測る大型石室であり、石材には巨石が使用される。副葬品は詳らかでない{{Sfn|冑塚（平凡社）|1979}}。 築造時期は、[[古墳時代]][[終末期古墳|終末期]]の[[7世紀]]前半頃と推定される{{Sfn|広瀬和雄|2013}}。太秦・嵯峨野地域の古墳のうちでは、[[蛇塚古墳]]・円山古墳に後続する最後の首長墓に位置づけられ{{Sfn|広瀬和雄|2013}}、嵯峨野一帯を開発した渡来系氏族の[[秦氏]]との関連を示唆する古墳として注目される。 古墳域は、現在では個人敷地内で保存されている。 == 埋葬施設 == 埋葬施設としては両袖式[[横穴式石室]]が構築されており、南方向に開口する。石室の規模は次の通り{{Sfn|広瀬和雄|2013}}。 * 石室全長：14.4メートル * 玄室：長さ5.2メートル、幅2.9メートル、現在高さ3.4メートル * 羨道：長さ9.2メートル、幅1.7メートル、現在高さ1.8メートル 玄室の奥壁は2段積みと推定され（祠が存在するため明確としない）、側壁は2石の2段積み、前壁は1段積みによって構築される。玄門部は1石、羨道の側壁は2段積みである。天井石は、玄室では3石、羨道では4石{{Sfn|広瀬和雄|2013}}。 File:Kabutozuka Kofun (Kyoto), 3D-1.jpg|石室俯瞰図

[[京都盆地]]（山城盆地）西部、嵯峨野台地上に築造された単独の大型円墳である。古墳名は冑の形をした墳丘外観に由来し、現在は石室奥に祠が祀られている{{Sfn|冑塚（平凡社）|1979}}。これまでに盗掘に遭っているほか、発掘調査は実施されていない。 墳形は円形で、直径38メートル以上・高さ5.5メートル以上を測る{{Sfn|広瀬和雄|2013}}。埋葬施設は両袖式の[[横穴式石室]]で、南方向に開口する。石室全長14.4メートルを測る大型石室であり、石材には巨石が使用される。副葬品は詳らかでない{{Sfn|冑塚（平凡社）|1979}}。 築造時期は、[[古墳時代]][[終末期古墳|終末期]]の[[7世紀]]前半頃と推定される{{Sfn|広瀬和雄|2013}}。太秦・嵯峨野地域の古墳のうちでは、[[蛇塚古墳]]・円山古墳に後続する最後の首長墓に位置づけられ{{Sfn|広瀬和雄|2013}}、嵯峨野一帯を開発した渡来系氏族の[[秦氏]]との関連を示唆する古墳として注目される。 古墳域は、現在では個人敷地内で保存されている。 == 埋葬施設 == 埋葬施設としては両袖式[[横穴式石室]]が構築されており、南方向に開口する。石室の規模は次の通り{{Sfn|広瀬和雄|2013}}。 * 石室全長：14.4メートル * 玄室：長さ5.2メートル、幅2.9メートル、現在高さ3.4メートル * 羨道：長さ9.2メートル、幅1.7メートル、現在高さ1.8メートル 玄室の奥壁は2段積みと推定され（祠が存在するため明確としない）、側壁は2石の2段積み、前壁は1段積みによって構築される。玄門部は1石、羨道の側壁は2段積みである。天井石は、玄室では3石、羨道では4石{{Sfn|広瀬和雄|2013}}。 File:Kabutozuka Kofun (Kyoto), 3D-1.jpg|石室俯瞰図

[[京都盆地]]（山城盆地）西部、嵯峨野台地上に築造された単独の大型円墳である。古墳名は冑の形をした墳丘外観に由来し、現在は石室奥に祠が祀られている{{Sfn|冑塚（平凡社）|1979}}。これまでに盗掘に遭っているほか、発掘調査は実施されていない。 墳形は円形で、直径38メートル以上・高さ5.5メートル以上を測る{{Sfn|広瀬和雄|2013}}。埋葬施設は両袖式の[[横穴式石室]]で、南方向に開口する。石室全長14.4メートルを測る大型石室であり、石材には巨石が使用される。副葬品は詳らかでない{{Sfn|冑塚（平凡社）|1979}}。 築造時期は、[[古墳時代]][[終末期古墳|終末期]]の[[7世紀]]前半頃と推定される{{Sfn|広瀬和雄|2013}}。太秦・嵯峨野地域の古墳のうちでは、[[蛇塚古墳]]・円山古墳に後続する最後の首長墓に位置づけられ{{Sfn|広瀬和雄|2013}}、嵯峨野一帯を開発した渡来系氏族の[[秦氏]]との関連を示唆する古墳として注目される。 古墳域は、現在では個人敷地内で保存されている。 == 埋葬施設 == 埋葬施設としては両袖式[[横穴式石室]]が構築されており、南方向に開口する。石室の規模は次の通り{{Sfn|広瀬和雄|2013}}。 * 石室全長：14.4メートル * 玄室：長さ5.2メートル、幅2.9メートル、現在高さ3.4メートル * 羨道：長さ9.2メートル、幅1.7メートル、現在高さ1.8メートル 玄室の奥壁は2段積みと推定され（祠が存在するため明確としない）、側壁は2石の2段積み、前壁は1段積みによって構築される。玄門部は1石、羨道の側壁は2段積みである。天井石は、玄室では3石、羨道では4石{{Sfn|広瀬和雄|2013}}。 File:Kabutozuka Kofun (Kyoto), 3D-1.jpg|石室俯瞰図

高さ5.5m以上 |埋葬施設 = 両袖式[[横穴式石室]] |出土品 = |陪塚 = |築造時期 = [[7世紀]]前半 |被葬者 = （推定）[[秦氏]]一族 |陵墓 = |史跡 = なし |有形文化財 = |特記事項 = |地図 = Japan Kyoto city |アイコン = 円墳 |ラベル = 甲塚古墳 |ラベル位置 = bottom }} {{OSM Location map | coord = {{Coord|35|0|50|N|135|41|50|E}} | zoom = 12 | float = right | width = 250| height =200 | caption = {{center|太秦・嵯峨野地域の古墳分布図}} | scalemark = 60 | mark-coord1= {{Coord|35|0|59.81|N|135|41|59.30|E}} | label1=垂箕山 | label-pos1=top | label-color1=black | mark1=前方後円墳-東南東.png | mark-size1=20 | mark-coord2= {{Coord|35|0|25.57|N|135|42|43.45|E}} | label2=天塚 | label-pos2=right | label-color2=black | mark2=前方後円墳-南南東.png | mark-size2=20 | mark-coord3= {{Coord|35|0|30.51|N|135|42|44.42|E}} | label3=清水山 | label-pos3=top | label-color3=black | mark3=前方後円墳-南南東.png | mark-size3=20 | mark-coord4= {{Coord|35|0|14.80|N|135|42|24.37|E}} | label4=段ノ山 | label-pos4=bottom | label-color4=black | mark4=前方後円墳-南.png | mark-size4=20 | mark-coord5= {{Coord|35|1|26.80|N|135|42|47.87|E}} | label5=双ヶ岡1号 | label-pos5=top | label-color5=black | mark5=円墳.png | mark-size5=20 | mark-coord6= {{Coord|35|0|42.83|N|135|41|59.88|E}} | label6=蛇塚 | label-pos6=left | label-color6=black | mark6=前方後円墳-南西.png | mark-size6=20 | mark-coord7= {{Coord|35|1|33.45|N|135|40|46.70|E}} | label7=円山 | label-pos7=left | label-color7=black | mark7=円墳.png | mark-size7=20 | mark-coord8= {{Coord|35|1|10.80|N|135|41|24.85|E}} | label8=甲塚 | label-pos8=left | label-color8=black | mark8=円墳.png | mark-size8=20 }} '''甲塚古墳'''（かぶとづかこふん）は、[[京都府]][[京都市]][[右京区]]嵯峨甲塚町にある[[古墳]]。形状は[[円墳]]。史跡指定はされていない。 == 概要 ==
{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+太秦・嵯峨野地域の主な古墳{{Sfn|広瀬和雄|2013}} !期!!古墳名!!形状!!規模!!石室全長!!築造時期!!陵墓・史跡 |- | ||[[垂箕山古墳]]||[[前方後円墳]]||65m|| ||5c末-6c初頭||[[宮内庁]]治定墓 |- |rowspan=2|3||rowspan=2|[[天塚古墳]]||rowspan=2|前方後円墳||rowspan=2|73m||(8.1m)||rowspan=2|6c前半||rowspan=2|国の[[史跡]] |- |(7.7m) |- |rowspan=4|4
・
5||[[清水山古墳]]||前方後円墳||57m|| ||rowspan=2|6c中葉-後半||（消滅） |- |段ノ山古墳||前方後円墳|| || ||（消滅） |- |[[双ヶ岡1号墳]]||[[円墳]]||44m||15.8m||rowspan=2|6世紀後半||なし |- |衣笠山1号墳||円墳||26m||(6.0m)||なし |- |rowspan=6|6||[[蛇塚古墳]]||前方後円墳||75m||17.8m||rowspan=6|6c末-7c初頭||国の史跡 |- |[[円山古墳 (京都市)|円山古墳
（大覚寺1号墳）]]||円墳||50m||14.7m||[[陵墓参考地]] |- |[[狐塚古墳 (京都市)|狐塚古墳
（大覚寺4号墳）]]||円墳||28m||(12.8m)||なし |- |[[入道塚古墳|入道塚古墳
（大覚寺2号墳）]]||[[方墳]]||30m|||11.2m||陵墓参考地 |- |[[大覚寺古墳群|南天塚古墳
（大覚寺3号墳）]]||円墳||25-30m||8.1m||（埋没） |- |御堂ヶ池1号墳||円墳||30m||(8.3m)||京都市登録史跡 |- |rowspan=2|7||'''甲塚古墳'''||円墳||38m||14.4m||rowspan=2|7c前半||なし |- |広沢古墳||円墳||30m||12.0m||なし |}
[[京都盆地]]（山城盆地）西部、嵯峨野台地上に築造された単独の大型円墳である。古墳名は冑の形をした墳丘外観に由来し、現在は石室奥に祠が祀られている{{Sfn|冑塚（平凡社）|1979}}。これまでに盗掘に遭っているほか、発掘調査は実施されていない。 墳形は円形で、直径38メートル以上・高さ5.5メートル以上を測る{{Sfn|広瀬和雄|2013}}。埋葬施設は両袖式の[[横穴式石室]]で、南方向に開口する。石室全長14.4メートルを測る大型石室であり、石材には巨石が使用される。副葬品は詳らかでない{{Sfn|冑塚（平凡社）|1979}}。 築造時期は、[[古墳時代]][[終末期古墳|終末期]]の[[7世紀]]前半頃と推定される{{Sfn|広瀬和雄|2013}}。太秦・嵯峨野地域の古墳のうちでは、[[蛇塚古墳]]・円山古墳に後続する最後の首長墓に位置づけられ{{Sfn|広瀬和雄|2013}}、嵯峨野一帯を開発した渡来系氏族の[[秦氏]]との関連を示唆する古墳として注目される。 古墳域は、現在では個人敷地内で保存されている。 == 埋葬施設 == 埋葬施設としては両袖式[[横穴式石室]]が構築されており、南方向に開口する。石室の規模は次の通り{{Sfn|広瀬和雄|2013}}。 * 石室全長：14.4メートル * 玄室：長さ5.2メートル、幅2.9メートル、現在高さ3.4メートル * 羨道：長さ9.2メートル、幅1.7メートル、現在高さ1.8メートル 玄室の奥壁は2段積みと推定され（祠が存在するため明確としない）、側壁は2石の2段積み、前壁は1段積みによって構築される。玄門部は1石、羨道の側壁は2段積みである。天井石は、玄室では3石、羨道では4石{{Sfn|広瀬和雄|2013}}。 File:Kabutozuka Kofun (Kyoto), 3D-1.jpg|石室俯瞰図
{{small|左から、羨道・玄室。}} File:Kabutozuka Kofun (Kyoto), 3D-2.jpg|石室展開図 File:Kabutozuka Kofun (Kyoto), genshitsu-1.jpg|玄室（奥壁方向） File:Kabutozuka Kofun (Kyoto), genshitsu-2.jpg|玄室（開口部方向） File:Kabutozuka Kofun (Kyoto), sendou-2.jpg|羨道（開口部方向） File:Kabutozuka Kofun (Kyoto), sendou-1.jpg|羨道（玄室方向） File:Kabutozuka Kofun (Kyoto), funkyu.jpg|墳丘 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == 参考文献 == {{small|（記事執筆に使用した文献）}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1979|chapter=冑塚|title=[[日本歴史地名大系]] 27 京都市の地名|publisher=[[平凡社]]|isbn=978-4-582-49027-5|ref={{Harvid|冑塚（平凡社）|1979}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|author=[[広瀬和雄]]|year=2013|chapter=山城・蛇塚古墳をめぐる二,三の問題|title=国立歴史民俗博物館研究報告 第178集|publisher=[[国立歴史民俗博物館]]|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|広瀬和雄|2013}}}} == 関連文献 == {{small|（記事執筆に使用していない関連文献）}} * {{Cite journal|和書|author=|title=右京区甲塚町兜塚古墳実測図付記|date=1964|journal=とれんち|publisher=京都大学考古学研究会|volume=|number=11|pages=|url=|naid=|doi=|ref=}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=[[京都大学]]考古学研究会|author=|year=1971|chapter=各古墳の概要 > 甲塚古墳|title=嵯峨野の古墳時代 -御堂ヶ池群集墳発掘調査報告-|publisher=京大考古学研究会出版事務局|isbn=|ref=}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=京都市|author=|year=1983|chapter=古墳時代 > 右京区|title=史料京都の歴史 第2巻 考古|publisher=[[平凡社]]|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|史料京都の歴史 第2巻 考古|1983}}}} == 外部リンク == * {{Commonscat-inline|Kabutozuka Kofun (Kyoto)}} {{DEFAULTSORT:かふとつかこふん きようとし}} [[Category:太秦・嵯峨野古墳群]] [[Category:京都府の古墳]] [[Category:右京区の歴史]] [[Category:円墳]] [[Category:秦氏|墓かふとつかこふん]] [[Category:太秦]]

[[京都盆地]]（山城盆地）西部、嵯峨野台地上に築造された単独の大型円墳である。古墳名は冑の形をした墳丘外観に由来し、現在は石室奥に祠が祀られている{{Sfn|冑塚（平凡社）|1979}}。これまでに盗掘に遭っているほか、発掘調査は実施されていない。 墳形は円形で、直径38メートル以上・高さ5.5メートル以上を測る{{Sfn|広瀬和雄|2013}}。埋葬施設は両袖式の[[横穴式石室]]で、南方向に開口する。石室全長14.4メートルを測る大型石室であり、石材には巨石が使用される。副葬品は詳らかでない{{Sfn|冑塚（平凡社）|1979}}。 築造時期は、[[古墳時代]][[終末期古墳|終末期]]の[[7世紀]]前半頃と推定される{{Sfn|広瀬和雄|2013}}。太秦・嵯峨野地域の古墳のうちでは、[[蛇塚古墳]]・円山古墳に後続する最後の首長墓に位置づけられ{{Sfn|広瀬和雄|2013}}、嵯峨野一帯を開発した渡来系氏族の[[秦氏]]との関連を示唆する古墳として注目される。 古墳域は、現在では個人敷地内で保存されている。 == 埋葬施設 == [[File:Kabutozuka Kofun (Kyoto), 3D-P.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|石室パース図}}]] 埋葬施設としては両袖式[[横穴式石室]]が構築されており、南方向に開口する。石室の規模は次の通り{{Sfn|広瀬和雄|2013}}。 * 石室全長：14.4メートル * 玄室：長さ5.2メートル、幅2.9メートル、現在高さ3.4メートル * 羨道：長さ9.2メートル、幅1.7メートル、現在高さ1.8メートル 玄室の奥壁は2段積みと推定され（祠が存在するため明確としない）、側壁は2石の2段積み、前壁は1段積みによって構築される。玄門部は1石、羨道の側壁は2段積みである。天井石は、玄室では3石、羨道では4石{{Sfn|広瀬和雄|2013}}。 File:Kabutozuka Kofun (Kyoto), 3D-1.jpg|石室俯瞰図
{{small|左から、羨道・玄室。}} File:Kabutozuka Kofun (Kyoto), 3D-2.jpg|石室展開図 File:Kabutozuka Kofun (Kyoto), genshitsu-1.jpg|玄室（奥壁方向） File:Kabutozuka Kofun (Kyoto), genshitsu-2.jpg|玄室（開口部方向） File:Kabutozuka Kofun (Kyoto), sendou-2.jpg|羨道（開口部方向） File:Kabutozuka Kofun (Kyoto), sendou-1.jpg|羨道（玄室方向） File:Kabutozuka Kofun (Kyoto), funkyu.jpg|墳丘 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == 参考文献 == {{small|（記事執筆に使用した文献）}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1979|chapter=冑塚|title=[[日本歴史地名大系]] 27 京都市の地名|publisher=[[平凡社]]|isbn=978-4-582-49027-5|ref={{Harvid|冑塚（平凡社）|1979}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|author=[[広瀬和雄]]|year=2013|chapter=山城・蛇塚古墳をめぐる二,三の問題|title=国立歴史民俗博物館研究報告 第178集|publisher=[[国立歴史民俗博物館]]|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|広瀬和雄|2013}}}} == 関連文献 == {{small|（記事執筆に使用していない関連文献）}} * {{Cite journal|和書|author=|title=右京区甲塚町兜塚古墳実測図付記|date=1964|journal=とれんち|publisher=京都大学考古学研究会|volume=|number=11|pages=|url=|naid=|doi=|ref=}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=[[京都大学]]考古学研究会|author=|year=1971|chapter=各古墳の概要 > 甲塚古墳|title=嵯峨野の古墳時代 -御堂ヶ池群集墳発掘調査報告-|publisher=京大考古学研究会出版事務局|isbn=|ref=}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=京都市|author=|year=1983|chapter=古墳時代 > 右京区|title=史料京都の歴史 第2巻 考古|publisher=[[平凡社]]|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|史料京都の歴史 第2巻 考古|1983}}}} == 外部リンク == * {{Commonscat-inline|Kabutozuka Kofun (Kyoto)}} {{DEFAULTSORT:かふとつかこふん きようとし}} [[Category:太秦・嵯峨野古墳群]] [[Category:京都府の古墳]] [[Category:右京区の歴史]] [[Category:円墳]] [[Category:秦氏|墓かふとつかこふん]] [[Category:太秦]]

{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+太秦・嵯峨野地域の主な古墳{{Sfn|広瀬和雄|2013}} !期!!古墳名!!形状!!規模!!石室全長!!築造時期!!陵墓・史跡 |- | ||[[垂箕山古墳]]||[[前方後円墳]]||65m|| ||5c末-6c初頭||[[宮内庁]]治定墓 |- |rowspan=2|3||rowspan=2|[[天塚古墳]]||rowspan=2|前方後円墳||rowspan=2|73m||(8.1m)||rowspan=2|6c前半||rowspan=2|国の[[史跡]] |- |(7.7m) |- |rowspan=4|4
・
5||[[清水山古墳]]||前方後円墳||57m|| ||rowspan=2|6c中葉-後半||（消滅） |- |段ノ山古墳||前方後円墳|| || ||（消滅） |- |[[双ヶ岡1号墳]]||[[円墳]]||44m||15.8m||rowspan=2|6世紀後半||なし |- |衣笠山1号墳||円墳||26m||(6.0m)||なし |- |rowspan=6|6||[[蛇塚古墳]]||前方後円墳||75m||17.8m||rowspan=6|6c末-7c初頭||国の史跡 |- |[[円山古墳 (京都市)|円山古墳
（大覚寺1号墳）]]||円墳||50m||14.7m||[[陵墓参考地]] |- |[[狐塚古墳 (京都市)|狐塚古墳
（大覚寺4号墳）]]||円墳||28m||(12.8m)||なし |- |[[入道塚古墳|入道塚古墳
（大覚寺2号墳）]]||[[方墳]]||30m|||11.2m||陵墓参考地 |- |[[大覚寺古墳群|南天塚古墳
（大覚寺3号墳）]]||円墳||25-30m||8.1m||（埋没） |- |御堂ヶ池1号墳||円墳||30m||(8.3m)||京都市登録史跡 |- |rowspan=2|7||'''甲塚古墳'''||円墳||38m||14.4m||rowspan=2|7c前半||なし |- |広沢古墳||円墳||30m||12.0m||なし |}
[[京都盆地]]（山城盆地）西部、嵯峨野台地上に築造された単独の大型円墳である。古墳名は冑の形をした墳丘外観に由来し、現在は石室奥に祠が祀られている{{Sfn|冑塚（平凡社）|1979}}。これまでに盗掘に遭っているほか、発掘調査は実施されていない。 墳形は円形で、直径38メートル以上・高さ5.5メートル以上を測る{{Sfn|広瀬和雄|2013}}。埋葬施設は両袖式の[[横穴式石室]]で、南方向に開口する。石室全長14.4メートルを測る大型石室であり、石材には巨石が使用される。副葬品は詳らかでない{{Sfn|冑塚（平凡社）|1979}}。 築造時期は、[[古墳時代]][[終末期古墳|終末期]]の[[7世紀]]前半頃と推定される{{Sfn|広瀬和雄|2013}}。太秦・嵯峨野地域の古墳のうちでは、[[蛇塚古墳]]・円山古墳に後続する最後の首長墓に位置づけられ{{Sfn|広瀬和雄|2013}}、嵯峨野一帯を開発した渡来系氏族の[[秦氏]]との関連を示唆する古墳として注目される。 古墳域は、現在では個人敷地内で保存されている。 == 埋葬施設 == [[File:Kabutozuka Kofun (Kyoto), 3D-P.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|石室パース図}}]] 埋葬施設としては両袖式[[横穴式石室]]が構築されており、南方向に開口する。石室の規模は次の通り{{Sfn|広瀬和雄|2013}}。 * 石室全長：14.4メートル * 玄室：長さ5.2メートル、幅2.9メートル、現在高さ3.4メートル * 羨道：長さ9.2メートル、幅1.7メートル、現在高さ1.8メートル 玄室の奥壁は2段積みと推定され（祠が存在するため明確としない）、側壁は2石の2段積み、前壁は1段積みによって構築される。玄門部は1石、羨道の側壁は2段積みである。天井石は、玄室では3石、羨道では4石{{Sfn|広瀬和雄|2013}}。 File:Kabutozuka Kofun (Kyoto), 3D-B.jpg|石室俯瞰図
{{small|左から、羨道・玄室。}} File:Kabutozuka Kofun (Kyoto), 3D-2.jpg|石室展開図 File:Kabutozuka Kofun (Kyoto), genshitsu-1.jpg|玄室（奥壁方向） File:Kabutozuka Kofun (Kyoto), genshitsu-2.jpg|玄室（開口部方向） File:Kabutozuka Kofun (Kyoto), sendou-2.jpg|羨道（開口部方向） File:Kabutozuka Kofun (Kyoto), sendou-1.jpg|羨道（玄室方向） File:Kabutozuka Kofun (Kyoto), funkyu.jpg|墳丘 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == 参考文献 == {{small|（記事執筆に使用した文献）}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1979|chapter=冑塚|title=[[日本歴史地名大系]] 27 京都市の地名|publisher=[[平凡社]]|isbn=978-4-582-49027-5|ref={{Harvid|冑塚（平凡社）|1979}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|author=[[広瀬和雄]]|year=2013|chapter=山城・蛇塚古墳をめぐる二,三の問題|title=国立歴史民俗博物館研究報告 第178集|publisher=[[国立歴史民俗博物館]]|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|広瀬和雄|2013}}}} == 関連文献 == {{small|（記事執筆に使用していない関連文献）}} * {{Cite journal|和書|author=|title=右京区甲塚町兜塚古墳実測図付記|date=1964|journal=とれんち|publisher=京都大学考古学研究会|volume=|number=11|pages=|url=|naid=|doi=|ref=}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=[[京都大学]]考古学研究会|author=|year=1971|chapter=各古墳の概要 > 甲塚古墳|title=嵯峨野の古墳時代 -御堂ヶ池群集墳発掘調査報告-|publisher=京大考古学研究会出版事務局|isbn=|ref=}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=京都市|author=|year=1983|chapter=古墳時代 > 右京区|title=史料京都の歴史 第2巻 考古|publisher=[[平凡社]]|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|史料京都の歴史 第2巻 考古|1983}}}} == 外部リンク == * {{Commonscat-inline|Kabutozuka Kofun (Kyoto)}} {{DEFAULTSORT:かふとつかこふん きようとし}} [[Category:太秦・嵯峨野古墳群]] [[Category:京都府の古墳]] [[Category:右京区の歴史]] [[Category:円墳]] [[Category:秦氏|墓かふとつかこふん]] [[Category:太秦]]

[[京都盆地]]（山城盆地）西部、嵯峨野台地上に築造された単独の大型円墳である。古墳名は冑の形をした墳丘外観に由来し、現在は石室奥に祠が祀られている{{Sfn|冑塚（平凡社）|1979}}。これまでに盗掘に遭っているほか、発掘調査は実施されていない。 墳形は円形で、直径38メートル以上・高さ5.5メートル以上を測る{{Sfn|広瀬和雄|2013}}。埋葬施設は両袖式の[[横穴式石室]]で、南方向に開口する。石室全長14.4メートルを測る大型石室であり、石材には巨石が使用される。副葬品は詳らかでない{{Sfn|冑塚（平凡社）|1979}}。 築造時期は、[[古墳時代]][[終末期古墳|終末期]]の[[7世紀]]前半頃と推定される{{Sfn|広瀬和雄|2013}}。太秦・嵯峨野地域の古墳のうちでは、[[蛇塚古墳]]・円山古墳に後続する最後の首長墓に位置づけられ{{Sfn|広瀬和雄|2013}}、嵯峨野一帯を開発した渡来系氏族の[[秦氏]]との関連を示唆する古墳として注目される。 古墳域は、現在では個人敷地内で保存されている。 == revision.text:
'''Kabutozuka Kofun''' (甲塚古墳) is an ancient burial mound (kofun) located in Sagakabuto-cho, [[Ukyo Ward]], [[Kyoto City]], [[Kyoto Prefecture]]. The mound has a circular shape and is not designated as a historical site.
== Overview ==
The Kabutozuka Kofun is a standalone, large circular burial mound built on the Sagano Plateau in the western part of the [[Kyoto Basin]]. The mound's name originates from its helmet-like appearance. Currently, a small shrine is enshrined within the inner chamber of the stone burial facility{{Sfn|Kabutozuka (Heibonsha)|1979}}. While the mound has suffered from looting, no formal excavations have been conducted.

The mound is associated with the [[Hata clan]], a powerful immigrant lineage that developed the Sagano area{{Sfn|Hirose Kazuo|2013}}.

'''Kabutozuka Kofun''' (甲塚古墳) is an ancient burial mound (kofun) located in Sagakabuto-cho, [[Ukyo Ward]], [[Kyoto City]], [[Kyoto Prefecture]]. The mound has a circular shape and is not designated as a historical site.
== Overview ==
The Kabutozuka Kofun is a standalone, large circular burial mound built on the Sagano Plateau in the western part of the [[Kyoto Basin]]. The mound's name originates from its helmet-like appearance. Currently, a small shrine is enshrined within the inner chamber of the stone burial facility{{Sfn|Kabutozuka (Heibonsha)|1979}}. While the mound has suffered from looting, no formal excavations have been conducted.
The mound is circular in shape, with an estimated diameter of 38 meters or more and a height exceeding 5.5 meters{{Sfn|Hirose Kazuo|2013}}. The burial facility is a double-sleeved [[corridor-style stone chamber]] (*yokoana-shiki sekishitsu*), which opens to the south. It is a large chamber measuring 14.4 meters in total length, constructed with massive stones. The funerary artifacts are not known{{Sfn|Kabutozuka (Heibonsha)|1979}}.
The construction is estimated to date to the early 7th century, during the late Kofun period. It is considered one of the last burial mounds for regional chieftains in the Uzumasa-Sagano area, following the [[Jazuka Kofun]] and [[Maruyama Kofun]]. The mound is associated with the [[Hata clan]], a powerful immigrant lineage that developed the Sagano area{{Sfn|Hirose Kazuo|2013}}.
Today, the kofun site is preserved within private property.
== Burial Facility ==
[[File:Kabutozuka Kofun (Kyoto), 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Bird’s eye view of the stone chamber}}]]
The burial facility is a double-sleeved [[corridor-style stone chamber]] opening to the south. The dimensions of the chamber are as follows{{Sfn|Hirose Kazuo|2013}}: * Total length: 14.4 meters * Main chamber (*gen-shitsu*): length 5.2 meters, width 2.9 meters, current height 3.4 meters * Corridor (*sen-dō*): length 9.2 meters, width 1.7 meters, current height 1.8 meters
The rear wall of the main chamber is assumed to consist of two stacked layers of stones (its details are obscured by the presence of a shrine). The side walls of the main chamber consist of two stones stacked in two layers, while the front wall is a single layer. The chamber entrance consists of a single stone, and the corridor walls are two layers. The ceiling stones comprise three stones in the main chamber and four in the corridor{{Sfn|Hirose Kazuo|2013}}.

'''Kabutozuka Kofun''' (甲塚古墳) is an ancient burial mound (kofun) located in Sagakabuto-cho, [[Ukyo Ward]], [[Kyoto City]], [[Kyoto Prefecture]]. The mound has a circular shape and is not designated as a historical site.
== Overview ==
The Kabutozuka Kofun is a standalone, large circular burial mound built on the Sagano Plateau in the western part of the [[Kyoto Basin]]. The mound's name originates from its helmet-like appearance. Currently, a small shrine is enshrined within the inner chamber of the stone burial facility{{Sfn|Kabutozuka (Heibonsha)|1979}}. While the mound has suffered from looting, no formal excavations have been conducted.
The mound is circular in shape, with an estimated diameter of 38 meters or more and a height exceeding 5.5 meters{{Sfn|Hirose Kazuo|2013}}. The burial facility is a double-sleeved [[corridor-style stone chamber]] (*yokoana-shiki sekishitsu*), which opens to the south. It is a large chamber measuring 14.4 meters in total length, constructed with massive stones. The funerary artifacts are not known{{Sfn|Kabutozuka (Heibonsha)|1979}}.
The construction is estimated to date to the early 7th century, during the late Kofun period. It is considered one of the last burial mounds for regional chieftains in the Uzumasa-Sagano area, following the [[Jazuka Kofun]] and [[Maruyama Kofun]]. The mound is associated with the [[Hata clan]], a powerful immigrant lineage that developed the Sagano area{{Sfn|Hirose Kazuo|2013}}.
Today, the kofun site is preserved within private property.
== Burial Facility ==
[[File:Kabutozuka Kofun (Kyoto), 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Bird’s eye view of the stone chamber}}]]
The burial facility is a double-sleeved [[corridor-style stone chamber]] opening to the south. The dimensions of the chamber are as follows{{Sfn|Hirose Kazuo|2013}}: * Total length: 14.4 meters * Main chamber (*gen-shitsu*): length 5.2 meters, width 2.9 meters, current height 3.4 meters * Corridor (*sen-dō*): length 9.2 meters, width 1.7 meters, current height 1.8 meters
The rear wall of the main chamber is assumed to consist of two stacked layers of stones (its details are obscured by the presence of a shrine). The side walls of the main chamber consist of two stones stacked in two layers, while the front wall is a single layer. The chamber entrance consists of a single stone, and the corridor walls are two layers. The ceiling stones comprise three stones in the main chamber and four in the corridor{{Sfn|Hirose Kazuo|2013}}.
<gallery> Kabutozuka Kofun (Kyoto), 3D-2.jpg|Expanded view of the stone chamber Kabutozuka Kofun (Kyoto), genshitsu-1.jpg|Main chamber (towards the rear wall) Kabutozuka Kofun (Kyoto), genshitsu-2.jpg|Main chamber (towards the entrance) Kabutozuka Kofun (Kyoto), sendou-2.jpg|Corridor (towards the entrance) Kabutozuka Kofun (Kyoto), sendou-1.jpg|Corridor (towards the main chamber) Kabutozuka Kofun (Kyoto), funkyu.jpg|The burial mound </gallery>

'''Kabutozuka Kofun''' (甲塚古墳) is an ancient burial mound (kofun) located in Sagakabuto-cho, [[Ukyo Ward]], [[Kyoto City]], [[Kyoto Prefecture]]. The mound has a circular shape and is not designated as a historical site.
== Overview ==
The Kabutozuka Kofun is a standalone, large circular burial mound built on the Sagano Plateau in the western part of the [[Kyoto Basin]]. The mound's name originates from its helmet-like appearance. Currently, a small shrine is enshrined within the inner chamber of the stone burial facility{{Sfn|Kabutozuka (Heibonsha)|1979}}. While the mound has suffered from looting, no formal excavations have been conducted.
The mound is circular in shape, with an estimated diameter of 38 meters or more and a height exceeding 5.5 meters{{Sfn|Hirose Kazuo|2013}}. The burial facility is a double-sleeved [[corridor-style stone chamber]] (*yokoana-shiki sekishitsu*), which opens to the south. It is a large chamber measuring 14.4 meters in total length, constructed with massive stones. The funerary artifacts are not known{{Sfn|Kabutozuka (Heibonsha)|1979}}.
The construction is estimated to date to the early 7th century, during the late Kofun period. It is considered one of the last burial mounds for regional chieftains in the Uzumasa-Sagano area, following the [[Jazuka Kofun]] and [[Maruyama Kofun]]. The mound is associated with the [[Hata clan]], a powerful immigrant lineage that developed the Sagano area{{Sfn|Hirose Kazuo|2013}}.
Today, the kofun site is preserved within private property.
== Burial Facility ==
[[File:Kabutozuka Kofun (Kyoto), 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Bird’s eye view of the stone chamber}}]]
The burial facility is a double-sleeved [[corridor-style stone chamber]] opening to the south. The dimensions of the chamber are as follows{{Sfn|Hirose Kazuo|2013}}: * Total length: 14.4 meters * Main chamber (*gen-shitsu*): length 5.2 meters, width 2.9 meters, current height 3.4 meters * Corridor (*sen-dō*): length 9.2 meters, width 1.7 meters, current height 1.8 meters
The rear wall of the main chamber is assumed to consist of two stacked layers of stones (its details are obscured by the presence of a shrine). The side walls of the main chamber consist of two stones stacked in two layers, while the front wall is a single layer. The chamber entrance consists of a single stone, and the corridor walls are two layers. The ceiling stones comprise three stones in the main chamber and four in the corridor{{Sfn|Hirose Kazuo|2013}}.
<gallery> Kabutozuka Kofun (Kyoto), 3D-2.jpg|Expanded view of the stone chamber Kabutozuka Kofun (Kyoto), genshitsu-1.jpg|Main chamber (towards the rear wall) Kabutozuka Kofun (Kyoto), genshitsu-2.jpg|Main chamber (towards the entrance) Kabutozuka Kofun (Kyoto), sendou-2.jpg|Corridor (towards the entrance) Kabutozuka Kofun (Kyoto), sendou-1.jpg|Corridor (towards the main chamber) Kabutozuka Kofun (Kyoto), funkyu.jpg|The burial mound </gallery>

revision.text:
'''Kabutozuka Kofun''' (甲塚古墳) is an ancient burial mound (kofun) located in Sagakabuto-cho, [[Ukyo Ward]], [[Kyoto City]], [[Kyoto Prefecture]]. The mound has a circular shape and is not designated as a historical site.
== Overview ==
The Kabutozuka Kofun is a standalone, large circular burial mound built on the Sagano Plateau in the western part of the [[Kyoto Basin]]. The mound's name originates from its helmet-like appearance. Currently, a small shrine is enshrined within the inner chamber of the stone burial facility{{Sfn|Kabutozuka (Heibonsha)|1979}}. While the mound has suffered from looting, no formal excavations have been conducted.
The mound is circular in shape, with an estimated diameter of 38 meters or more and a height exceeding 5.5 meters{{Sfn|Hirose Kazuo|2013}}. The burial facility is a double-sleeved [[corridor-style stone chamber]] (*yokoana-shiki sekishitsu*), which opens to the south. It is a large chamber measuring 14.4 meters in total length, constructed with massive stones. The funerary artifacts are not known{{Sfn|Kabutozuka (Heibonsha)|1979}}.
The construction is estimated to date to the early 7th century, during the late Kofun period. It is considered one of the last burial mounds for regional chieftains in the Uzumasa-Sagano area, following the [[Jazuka Kofun]] and [[Maruyama Kofun]]. The mound is associated with the [[Hata clan]], a powerful immigrant lineage that developed the Sagano area{{Sfn|Hirose Kazuo|2013}}.
Today, the kofun site is preserved within private property.
== Burial Facility ==
[[File:Kabutozuka Kofun (Kyoto), 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Bird’s eye view of the stone chamber}}]]
The burial facility is a double-sleeved [[corridor-style stone chamber]] opening to the south. The dimensions of the chamber are as follows{{Sfn|Hirose Kazuo|2013}}: * Total length: 14.4 meters * Main chamber (*gen-shitsu*): length 5.2 meters, width 2.9 meters, current height 3.4 meters * Corridor (*sen-dō*): length 9.2 meters, width 1.7 meters, current height 1.8 meters
The rear wall of the main chamber is assumed to consist of two stacked layers of stones (its details are obscured by the presence of a shrine). The side walls of the main chamber consist of two stones stacked in two layers, while the front wall is a single layer. The chamber entrance consists of a single stone, and the corridor walls are two layers. The ceiling stones comprise three stones in the main chamber and four in the corridor{{Sfn|Hirose Kazuo|2013}}.
<gallery> Kabutozuka Kofun (Kyoto), 3D-2.jpg|Expanded view of the stone chamber Kabutozuka Kofun (Kyoto), genshitsu-1.jpg|Main chamber (towards the rear wall) Kabutozuka Kofun (Kyoto), genshitsu-2.jpg|Main chamber (towards the entrance) Kabutozuka Kofun (Kyoto), sendou-2.jpg|Corridor (towards the entrance) Kabutozuka Kofun (Kyoto), sendou-1.jpg|Corridor (towards the main chamber) Kabutozuka Kofun (Kyoto), funkyu.jpg|The burial mound </gallery>

== Related Reading == {{small|(Related literature not used in this article)}} * {{Cite journal|language=ja|author=|title=Detailed Measurement Diagram of Kabutozuka Kofun, Ukyo Ward|date=1964|journal=Trench|publisher=Kyoto University Archaeology Research Association|volume=|number=11|pages=|url=|naid=|doi=|ref=}} * {{Cite book|language=ja|editor=[[Kyoto University]] Archaeology Research Association|author=|year=1971|chapter=Overview of Burial Mounds > Kabutozuka Kofun|title=The Kofun Era of Sagano - Excavation Report of the Mitogagaike Group of Burial Mounds|publisher=Kyoto University Archaeology Research Association Publication Office|isbn=|ref=}} * {{Cite book|language=ja|editor=Kyoto City|author=|year=1983|chapter=The Kofun Era > Ukyo Ward|title=Historical Materials of Kyoto's History|volume=Vol. 2, Archaeology|publisher=[[Heibonsha]]|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Historical Materials of Kyoto Vol. 2|1983}}}}

== Overview ==
The Kabutozuka Kofun is a standalone, large circular burial mound built on the Sagano Plateau in the western part of the [[Kyoto Basin]]. The mound's name originates from its helmet-like appearance. The mound is associated with the [[Hata clan]], a powerful immigrant lineage that developed the Sagano area{{Sfn|Hirose Kazuo|2013}}.

The side walls of the main chamber consist of two stones stacked in two layers, while the front wall is a single layer. It is a large chamber measuring 14.4 meters in total length, constructed with massive stones. The funerary artifacts are not known{{Sfn|Kabutozuka (Heibonsha)|1979}}. The construction is estimated to date to the early 7th century, during the late Kofun period. It is considered one of the last burial mounds for regional chieftains in the Uzumasa-Sagano area, following the [[Jazuka Kofun]] and [[Maruyama Kofun]]. The mound is associated with the [[Hata clan]], a powerful immigrant lineage that developed the Sagano area{{Sfn|Hirose Kazuo|2013}}. Today, the kofun site is preserved within private property. == Burial Facility == [[File:Kabutozuka Kofun (Kyoto), 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Bird’s eye view of the stone chamber}}]] The burial facility is a double-sleeved [[corridor-style stone chamber]] opening to the south. The dimensions of the chamber are as follows{{Sfn|Hirose Kazuo|2013}}: * Total length: 14.4 meters * Main chamber (*gen-shitsu*): length 5.2 meters, width 2.9 meters, current height 3.4 meters * Corridor (*sen-dō*): length 9.2 meters, width 1.7 meters, current height 1.8 meters The rear wall of the main chamber is assumed to consist of two stacked layers of stones (its details are obscured by the presence of a shrine). The side walls of the main chamber consist of two stones stacked in two layers, while the front wall is a single layer. The chamber entrance consists of a single stone, and the corridor walls are two layers. The ceiling stones comprise three stones in the main chamber and four in the corridor{{Sfn|Hirose Kazuo|2013}}. The burial facility is a double-sleeved [[corridor-style stone chamber]] (*yokoana-shiki sekishitsu*), which opens to the south. It is a large chamber measuring 14.4 meters in total length, constructed with massive stones. The funerary artifacts are not known{{Sfn|Kabutozuka (Heibonsha)|1979}}. The construction is estimated to date to the early 7th century, during the late Kofun period. It is considered one of the last burial mounds for regional chieftains in the Uzumasa-Sagano area, following the [[Jazuka Kofun]] and [[Maruyama Kofun]]. The mound is associated with the [[Hata clan]], a powerful immigrant lineage that developed the Sagano area{{Sfn|Hirose Kazuo|2013}}. Today, the kofun site is preserved within private property. == Burial Facility == [[File:Kabutozuka Kofun (Kyoto), 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Bird’s eye view of the stone chamber}}]] The burial facility is a double-sleeved [[corridor-style stone chamber]] opening to the south. The dimensions of the chamber are as follows{{Sfn|Hirose Kazuo|2013}}: * Total length: 14.4 meters * Main chamber (*gen-shitsu*): length 5.2 meters, width 2.9 meters, current height 3.4 meters * Corridor (*sen-dō*): length 9.2 meters, width 1.7 meters, current height 1.8 meters The rear wall of the main chamber is assumed to consist of two stacked layers of stones (its details are obscured by the presence of a shrine). The side walls of the main chamber consist of two stones stacked in two layers, while the front wall is a single layer. Height: over 5.5 m |burial_facility = Two-sleeved [[yokoana-style stone chamber|yokoana-style stone chamber]] |artifacts_found = |associated_burial_mounds = |construction_period = Early [[7th century]] |person_buried = (Presumed) member of the [[Hata clan (Japan)|Hata clan]] |imperial_mausoleum = |historical_site = None |tangible_cultural_properties = |notable_features = |map = Japan Kyoto city |icon = Empun |label = Kabutozuka Kofun |label_position = bottom }} '''Kabutozuka Kofun''' (甲塚古墳) is an ancient burial mound (kofun) located in Sagakabuto-cho, [[Ukyo Ward]], [[Kyoto City]], [[Kyoto Prefecture]]. The mound has a circular shape and is not designated as a historical site. == Overview == The Kabutozuka Kofun is a standalone, large circular burial mound built on the Sagano Plateau in the western part of the [[Kyoto Basin]]. The mound's name originates from its helmet-like appearance. Currently, a small shrine is enshrined within the inner chamber of the stone burial facility{{Sfn|Kabutozuka (Heibonsha)|1979}}. While the mound has suffered from looting, no formal excavations have been conducted. The mound is circular in shape, with an estimated diameter of 38 meters or more and a height exceeding 5.5 meters{{Sfn|Hirose Kazuo|2013}}. The burial facility is a double-sleeved [[corridor-style stone chamber]] (*yokoana-shiki sekishitsu*), which opens to the south. It is a large chamber measuring 14.4 meters in total length, constructed with massive stones. The funerary artifacts are not known{{Sfn|Kabutozuka (Heibonsha)|1979}}. The construction is estimated to date to the early 7th century, during the late Kofun period. It is considered one of the last burial mounds for regional chieftains in the Uzumasa-Sagano area, following the [[Jazuka Kofun]] and [[Maruyama Kofun]]. The mound is associated with the [[Hata clan]], a powerful immigrant lineage that developed the Sagano area{{Sfn|Hirose Kazuo|2013}}. Today, the kofun site is preserved within private property. == Burial Facility == [[File:Kabutozuka Kofun (Kyoto), 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Bird’s eye view of the stone chamber}}]] The burial facility is a double-sleeved [[corridor-style stone chamber]] opening to the south. The dimensions of the chamber are as follows{{Sfn|Hirose Kazuo|2013}}: * Total length: 14.4 meters * Main chamber (*gen-shitsu*): length 5.2 meters, width 2.9 meters, current height 3.4 meters * Corridor (*sen-dō*): length 9.2 meters, width 1.7 meters, current height 1.8 meters The rear wall of the main chamber is assumed to consist of two stacked layers of stones (its details are obscured by the presence of a shrine). The side walls of the main chamber consist of two stones stacked in two layers, while the front wall is a single layer. The chamber entrance consists of a single stone, and the corridor walls are two layers. The ceiling stones comprise three stones in the main chamber and four in the corridor{{Sfn|Hirose Kazuo|2013}}. File:Kabutozuka Kofun (Kyoto), 3D-P.jpg| Kabutozuka Kofun (Kyoto), 3D-2.jpg|Expanded view of the stone chamber Kabutozuka Kofun (Kyoto), genshitsu-1.jpg|Main chamber (towards the rear wall) Kabutozuka Kofun (Kyoto), genshitsu-2.jpg|Main chamber (towards the entrance) Kabutozuka Kofun (Kyoto), sendou-2.jpg|Corridor (towards the entrance) Kabutozuka Kofun (Kyoto), sendou-1.jpg|Corridor (towards the main chamber) Kabutozuka Kofun (Kyoto), funkyu.jpg|The burial mound == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|(References used in article compilation)}} * {{Cite book|language=ja|editor=|author=|year=1979|chapter=甲塚|title=京都市の地名|series=[[Japanese Historical Place Names Series]] 27|publisher=[[Heibonsha]]|isbn=978-4-582-49027-5|ref={{Harvid|Kabutozuka (Heibonsha)|1979}}}} * {{Cite book|author=[[Kazuo Hirose]]|year=2013|chapter=Questions Regarding Yamashiro's Jazuka Kofun|title=Research Reports of the National Museum of Japanese History|volume=Vol. 178|publisher=[[National Museum of Japanese History]]|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Hirose Kazuo|2013}}}} == Related Reading == {{small|(Related literature not used in this article)}} * {{Cite journal|language=ja|author=|title=Detailed Measurement Diagram of Kabutozuka Kofun, Ukyo Ward|date=1964|journal=Trench|publisher=Kyoto University Archaeology Research Association|volume=|number=11|pages=|url=|naid=|doi=|ref=}} * {{Cite book|language=ja|editor=[[Kyoto University]] Archaeology Research Association|author=|year=1971|chapter=Overview of Burial Mounds > Kabutozuka Kofun|title=The Kofun Era of Sagano - Excavation Report of the Mitogagaike Group of Burial Mounds|publisher=Kyoto University Archaeology Research Association Publication Office|isbn=|ref=}} * {{Cite book|language=ja|editor=Kyoto City|author=|year=1983|chapter=The Kofun Era > Ukyo Ward|title=Historical Materials of Kyoto's History|volume=Vol. 2, Archaeology|publisher=[[Heibonsha]]|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Historical Materials of Kyoto Vol. 2|1983}}}} == External Links == * {{Commonscat-inline|Kabutozuka Kofun (Kyoto)}} [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Uzumasu-Sagano Burial Mounds]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Ancient Burial Mounds in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:History of Ukyo Ward]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Circular Burial Mounds]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Hata clan]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Uzumasu]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:unsorted completion categories]] [[ja:甲塚古墳 (京都市)]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Kabutozuka Kofun (Kyoto)]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Kabutozuka Kofun (Kyoto)]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:mass bump]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:1 revision imported]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:bump 1-25]] [[Category:kk]] {{Kofun navbar}} [[Category:kk]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Kabutozuka Kofun (Kyoto)]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:Kabutozuka Kofun (Kyoto)]][[Category:qq]] [[ja:甲塚古墳]]

The chamber entrance consists of a single stone, and the corridor walls are two layers. The ceiling stones comprise three stones in the main chamber and four in the corridor{{Sfn|Hirose Kazuo|2013}}. Excavation Report of the Mitogagaike Group of Burial Mounds|publisher=Kyoto University Archaeology Research Association Publication Office|isbn=|ref=}} * {{Cite book|language=ja|editor=Kyoto City|author=|year=1983|chapter=The Kofun Era > Ukyo Ward|title=Historical Materials of Kyoto's History|volume=Vol. 2, Archaeology|publisher=[[Heibonsha]]|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Historical Materials of Kyoto Vol. 2|1983}}}} == External Links == * {{Commonscat-inline|Kabutozuka Kofun (Kyoto)}} [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Uzumasu-Sagano Burial Mounds]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Ancient Burial Mounds in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:History of Ukyo Ward]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Circular Burial Mounds]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Hata clan]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Uzumasu]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-Kabutozuka Kofun (Kyoto)-qq]] [[ja:甲塚古墳 (京都市)]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Kabutozuka Kofun (Kyoto)]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Kabutozuka Kofun (Kyoto)]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:mass bump]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:1 revision imported]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:bump 1-25]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] {{Kofun navbar}} [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Kabutozuka Kofun (Kyoto)]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:Kabutozuka Kofun (Kyoto)]][[ja:甲塚古墳]]

{{infobox ancient site |name = Kabutozuka Kofun |image = [[File:Kabutozuka Kofun (Kyoto), entrance.jpg|280px]]
Entrance to the stone chamber |other_names = Kabutozuka/Ōtsuka{{Sfn|Kabutozuka (Heibonsha)|1979}} |affiliation = |location = Sagakabutozuka-cho, [[Ukyo Ward, Kyoto|Ukyo Ward]], [[Kyoto]], [[Kyoto Prefecture]] |latitude = 35|lat_minutes = 1|lat_seconds = 10.80 |longitude = 135|long_minutes = 41|long_seconds = 24.85 |ISO = JP-26 |shape = [[Round burial mound|Round mound (empun)]] |scale = Diameter: over 38 m
Height: over 5.5 m |burial_facility = Two-sleeved [[yokoana-style stone chamber|yokoana-style stone chamber]] |artifacts_found = |associated_burial_mounds = |construction_period = Early [[7th century]] |person_buried = (Presumed) member of the [[Hata clan (Japan)|Hata clan]] |imperial_mausoleum = |historical_site = None |tangible_cultural_properties = |notable_features = |map = Japan Kyoto city |icon = Empun |label = Kabutozuka Kofun |label_position = bottom }} '''Kabutozuka Kofun''' (甲塚古墳) is an ancient burial mound (kofun) located in Sagakabuto-cho, [[Ukyo Ward]], [[Kyoto City]], [[Kyoto Prefecture]]. The mound has a circular shape and is not designated as a historical site. == Overview == The Kabutozuka Kofun is a standalone, large circular burial mound built on the Sagano Plateau in the western part of the [[Kyoto Basin]]. The mound's name originates from its helmet-like appearance. Currently, a small shrine is enshrined within the inner chamber of the stone burial facility{{Sfn|Kabutozuka (Heibonsha)|1979}}. While the mound has suffered from looting, no formal excavations have been conducted. The mound is circular in shape, with an estimated diameter of 38 meters or more and a height exceeding 5.5 meters{{Sfn|Hirose Kazuo|2013}}. The burial facility is a double-sleeved [[corridor-style stone chamber]] (*yokoana-shiki sekishitsu*), which opens to the south. It is a large chamber measuring 14.4 meters in total length, constructed with massive stones. The funerary artifacts are not known{{Sfn|Kabutozuka (Heibonsha)|1979}}. The construction is estimated to date to the early 7th century, during the late Kofun period. It is considered one of the last burial mounds for regional chieftains in the Uzumasa-Sagano area, following the [[Jazuka Kofun]] and [[Maruyama Kofun]]. The mound is associated with the [[Hata clan]], a powerful immigrant lineage that developed the Sagano area{{Sfn|Hirose Kazuo|2013}}. Today, the kofun site is preserved within private property. == Burial Facility == [[File:Kabutozuka Kofun (Kyoto), 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Bird’s eye view of the stone chamber}}]] The burial facility is a double-sleeved [[corridor-style stone chamber]] opening to the south. The dimensions of the chamber are as follows{{Sfn|Hirose Kazuo|2013}}: * Total length: 14.4 meters * Main chamber (*gen-shitsu*): length 5.2 meters, width 2.9 meters, current height 3.4 meters * Corridor (*sen-dō*): length 9.2 meters, width 1.7 meters, current height 1.8 meters The rear wall of the main chamber is assumed to consist of two stacked layers of stones (its details are obscured by the presence of a shrine). The side walls of the main chamber consist of two stones stacked in two layers, while the front wall is a single layer. The chamber entrance consists of a single stone, and the corridor walls are two layers. The ceiling stones comprise three stones in the main chamber and four in the corridor{{Sfn|Hirose Kazuo|2013}}. File:Kabutozuka Kofun (Kyoto), 3D-P.jpg| Kabutozuka Kofun (Kyoto), 3D-2.jpg|Expanded view of the stone chamber Kabutozuka Kofun (Kyoto), genshitsu-1.jpg|Main chamber (towards the rear wall) Kabutozuka Kofun (Kyoto), genshitsu-2.jpg|Main chamber (towards the entrance) Kabutozuka Kofun (Kyoto), sendou-2.jpg|Corridor (towards the entrance) Kabutozuka Kofun (Kyoto), sendou-1.jpg|Corridor (towards the main chamber) Kabutozuka Kofun (Kyoto), funkyu.jpg|The burial mound == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|(References used in article compilation)}} * {{Cite book|language=ja|editor=|author=|year=1979|chapter=甲塚|title=京都市の地名|series=[[Japanese Historical Place Names Series]] 27|publisher=[[Heibonsha]]|isbn=978-4-582-49027-5|ref={{Harvid|Kabutozuka (Heibonsha)|1979}}}} * {{Cite book|author=[[Kazuo Hirose]]|year=2013|chapter=Questions Regarding Yamashiro's Jazuka Kofun|title=Research Reports of the National Museum of Japanese History|volume=Vol. 178|publisher=[[National Museum of Japanese History]]|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Hirose Kazuo|2013}}}} == Related Reading == {{small|(Related literature not used in this article)}} * {{Cite journal|language=ja|author=|title=Detailed Measurement Diagram of Kabutozuka Kofun, Ukyo Ward|date=1964|journal=Trench|publisher=Kyoto University Archaeology Research Association|volume=|number=11|pages=|url=|naid=|doi=|ref=}} * {{Cite book|language=ja|editor=[[Kyoto University]] Archaeology Research Association|author=|year=1971|chapter=Overview of Burial Mounds > Kabutozuka Kofun|title=The Kofun Era of Sagano - Excavation Report of the Mitogagaike Group of Burial Mounds|publisher=Kyoto University Archaeology Research Association Publication Office|isbn=|ref=}} * {{Cite book|language=ja|editor=Kyoto City|author=|year=1983|chapter=The Kofun Era > Ukyo Ward|title=Historical Materials of Kyoto's History|volume=Vol. 2, Archaeology|publisher=[[Heibonsha]]|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Historical Materials of Kyoto Vol. 2|1983}}}} == External Links == * {{Commonscat-inline|Kabutozuka Kofun (Kyoto)}} [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Uzumasu-Sagano Burial Mounds]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Ancient Burial Mounds in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:History of Ukyo Ward]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Circular Burial Mounds]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Hata clan]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Uzumasu]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-{{subst:MAINSPACE}}--qq]] [[Category:qq-Kabutozuka Kofun (Kyoto)-qq]] [[ja:甲塚古墳 (京都市)]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Kabutozuka Kofun (Kyoto)]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Kabutozuka Kofun (Kyoto)]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:mass bump]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:1 revision imported]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:bump 1-25]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] {{Kofun navbar}} [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Kabutozuka Kofun (Kyoto)]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:Kabutozuka Kofun (Kyoto)]][[ja:甲塚古墳]]

Today, the kofun site is preserved within private property. == Burial Facility == [[File:Kabutozuka Kofun (Kyoto), 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Bird’s eye view of the stone chamber}}]] The burial facility is a double-sleeved [[corridor-style stone chamber]] opening to the south. The dimensions of the chamber are as follows{{Sfn|Hirose Kazuo|2013}}: * Total length: 14.4 meters * Main chamber (*gen-shitsu*): length 5.2 meters, width 2.9 meters, current height 3.4 meters * Corridor (*sen-dō*): length 9.2 meters, width 1.7 meters, current height 1.8 meters The rear wall of the main chamber is assumed to consist of two stacked layers of stones (its details are obscured by the presence of a shrine). The side walls of the main chamber consist of two stones stacked in two layers, while the front wall is a single layer. The chamber entrance consists of a single stone, and the corridor walls are two layers. The ceiling stones comprise three stones in the main chamber and four in the corridor{{Sfn|Hirose Kazuo|2013}}. Height: over 5.5 m |burial_facility = Two-sleeved [[yokoana-style stone chamber|yokoana-style stone chamber]] |artifacts_found = |associated_burial_mounds = |construction_period = Early [[7th century]] |person_buried = (Presumed) member of the [[Hata clan (Japan)|Hata clan]] |imperial_mausoleum = |historical_site = None |tangible_cultural_properties = |notable_features = |map = Japan Kyoto city |icon = Empun |label = Kabutozuka Kofun |label_position = bottom }} '''Kabutozuka Kofun''' (甲塚古墳) is an ancient burial mound (kofun) located in Sagakabuto-cho, [[Ukyo Ward]], [[Kyoto City]], [[Kyoto Prefecture]]. The mound has a circular shape and is not designated as a historical site. == Overview == The Kabutozuka Kofun is a standalone, large circular burial mound built on the Sagano Plateau in the western part of the [[Kyoto Basin]]. The mound's name originates from its helmet-like appearance. Currently, a small shrine is enshrined within the inner chamber of the stone burial facility{{Sfn|Kabutozuka (Heibonsha)|1979}}. While the mound has suffered from looting, no formal excavations have been conducted. The mound is circular in shape, with an estimated diameter of 38 meters or more and a height exceeding 5.5 meters{{Sfn|Hirose Kazuo|2013}}. The burial facility is a double-sleeved [[corridor-style stone chamber]] (*yokoana-shiki sekishitsu*), which opens to the south. It is a large chamber measuring 14.4 meters in total length, constructed with massive stones. The funerary artifacts are not known{{Sfn|Kabutozuka (Heibonsha)|1979}}. The construction is estimated to date to the early 7th century, during the late Kofun period. It is considered one of the last burial mounds for regional chieftains in the Uzumasa-Sagano area, following the [[Jazuka Kofun]] and [[Maruyama Kofun]]. The mound is associated with the [[Hata clan]], a powerful immigrant lineage that developed the Sagano area{{Sfn|Hirose Kazuo|2013}}. Today, the kofun site is preserved within private property. == Burial Facility == [[File:Kabutozuka Kofun (Kyoto), 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Bird’s eye view of the stone chamber}}]] The burial facility is a double-sleeved [[corridor-style stone chamber]] opening to the south. The dimensions of the chamber are as follows{{Sfn|Hirose Kazuo|2013}}: * Total length: 14.4 meters * Main chamber (*gen-shitsu*): length 5.2 meters, width 2.9 meters, current height 3.4 meters * Corridor (*sen-dō*): length 9.2 meters, width 1.7 meters, current height 1.8 meters The rear wall of the main chamber is assumed to consist of two stacked layers of stones (its details are obscured by the presence of a shrine). The side walls of the main chamber consist of two stones stacked in two layers, while the front wall is a single layer. The chamber entrance consists of a single stone, and the corridor walls are two layers. The ceiling stones comprise three stones in the main chamber and four in the corridor{{Sfn|Hirose Kazuo|2013}}. {{infobox ancient site |name = Kabutozuka Kofun |image = [[File:Kabutozuka Kofun (Kyoto), entrance.jpg|280px]]
Entrance to the stone chamber |other_names = Kabutozuka/Ōtsuka{{Sfn|Kabutozuka (Heibonsha)|1979}} |affiliation = |location = Sagakabutozuka-cho, [[Ukyo Ward, Kyoto|Ukyo Ward]], [[Kyoto]], [[Kyoto Prefecture]] |latitude = 35|lat_minutes = 1|lat_seconds = 10.80 |longitude = 135|long_minutes = 41|long_seconds = 24.85 |ISO = JP-26 |shape = [[Round burial mound|Round mound (empun)]] |scale = Diameter: over 38 m
Height: over 5.5 m |burial_facility = Two-sleeved [[yokoana-style stone chamber|yokoana-style stone chamber]] |artifacts_found = |associated_burial_mounds = |construction_period = Early [[7th century]] |person_buried = (Presumed) member of the [[Hata clan (Japan)|Hata clan]] |imperial_mausoleum = |historical_site = None |tangible_cultural_properties = |notable_features = |map = Japan Kyoto city |icon = Empun |label = Kabutozuka Kofun |label_position = bottom }} '''Kabutozuka Kofun''' (甲塚古墳) is an ancient burial mound (kofun) located in Sagakabuto-cho, [[Ukyo Ward]], [[Kyoto City]], [[Kyoto Prefecture]]. The mound has a circular shape and is not designated as a historical site. == Overview == The Kabutozuka Kofun is a standalone, large circular burial mound built on the Sagano Plateau in the western part of the [[Kyoto Basin]]. The mound's name originates from its helmet-like appearance. Currently, a small shrine is enshrined within the inner chamber of the stone burial facility{{Sfn|Kabutozuka (Heibonsha)|1979}}. While the mound has suffered from looting, no formal excavations have been conducted. The mound is circular in shape, with an estimated diameter of 38 meters or more and a height exceeding 5.5 meters{{Sfn|Hirose Kazuo|2013}}. The burial facility is a double-sleeved [[corridor-style stone chamber]] (*yokoana-shiki sekishitsu*), which opens to the south. It is a large chamber measuring 14.4 meters in total length, constructed with massive stones. The funerary artifacts are not known{{Sfn|Kabutozuka (Heibonsha)|1979}}. The construction is estimated to date to the early 7th century, during the late Kofun period. It is considered one of the last burial mounds for regional chieftains in the Uzumasa-Sagano area, following the [[Jazuka Kofun]] and [[Maruyama Kofun]]. The mound is associated with the [[Hata clan]], a powerful immigrant lineage that developed the Sagano area{{Sfn|Hirose Kazuo|2013}}. Today, the kofun site is preserved within private property. == Burial Facility == [[File:Kabutozuka Kofun (Kyoto), 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Bird’s eye view of the stone chamber}}]] The burial facility is a double-sleeved [[corridor-style stone chamber]] opening to the south. The dimensions of the chamber are as follows{{Sfn|Hirose Kazuo|2013}}: * Total length: 14.4 meters * Main chamber (*gen-shitsu*): length 5.2 meters, width 2.9 meters, current height 3.4 meters * Corridor (*sen-dō*): length 9.2 meters, width 1.7 meters, current height 1.8 meters The rear wall of the main chamber is assumed to consist of two stacked layers of stones (its details are obscured by the presence of a shrine). The side walls of the main chamber consist of two stones stacked in two layers, while the front wall is a single layer. The chamber entrance consists of a single stone, and the corridor walls are two layers. The ceiling stones comprise three stones in the main chamber and four in the corridor{{Sfn|Hirose Kazuo|2013}}. {{infobox ancient site |name = Kabutozuka Kofun |image = [[File:Kabutozuka Kofun (Kyoto), entrance.jpg|280px]]
Entrance to the stone chamber |other_names = Kabutozuka/Ōtsuka{{Sfn|Kabutozuka (Heibonsha)|1979}} |affiliation = |location = Sagakabutozuka-cho, [[Ukyo Ward, Kyoto|Ukyo Ward]], [[Kyoto]], [[Kyoto Prefecture]] |latitude = 35|lat_minutes = 1|lat_seconds = 10.80 |longitude = 135|long_minutes = 41|long_seconds = 24.85 |ISO = JP-26 |type = [[Round burial mound|Round mound (empun)]] |scale = Diameter: over 38 m
Height: over 5.5 m |burial_facility = Two-sleeved [[yokoana-style stone chamber|yokoana-style stone chamber]] |artifacts_found = |associated_burial_mounds = |construction_period = Early [[7th century]] |person_buried = (Presumed) member of the [[Hata clan (Japan)|Hata clan]] |imperial_mausoleum = |historical_site = None |tangible_cultural_properties = |notable_features = |map = Japan Kyoto city |icon = Empun |label = Kabutozuka Kofun |label_position = bottom }} '''Kabutozuka Kofun''' (甲塚古墳) is an ancient burial mound (kofun) located in Sagakabuto-cho, [[Ukyo Ward]], [[Kyoto City]], [[Kyoto Prefecture]]. The mound has a circular shape and is not designated as a historical site. == Overview == The Kabutozuka Kofun is a standalone, large circular burial mound built on the Sagano Plateau in the western part of the [[Kyoto Basin]]. The mound's name originates from its helmet-like appearance. Currently, a small shrine is enshrined within the inner chamber of the stone burial facility{{Sfn|Kabutozuka (Heibonsha)|1979}}. While the mound has suffered from looting, no formal excavations have been conducted. The mound is circular in shape, with an estimated diameter of 38 meters or more and a height exceeding 5.5 meters{{Sfn|Hirose Kazuo|2013}}. The burial facility is a double-sleeved [[corridor-style stone chamber]] (*yokoana-shiki sekishitsu*), which opens to the south. It is a large chamber measuring 14.4 meters in total length, constructed with massive stones. The funerary artifacts are not known{{Sfn|Kabutozuka (Heibonsha)|1979}}. The construction is estimated to date to the early 7th century, during the late Kofun period. It is considered one of the last burial mounds for regional chieftains in the Uzumasa-Sagano area, following the [[Jazuka Kofun]] and [[Maruyama Kofun]]. The mound is associated with the [[Hata clan]], a powerful immigrant lineage that developed the Sagano area{{Sfn|Hirose Kazuo|2013}}. Today, the kofun site is preserved within private property. == Burial Facility == [[File:Kabutozuka Kofun (Kyoto), 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Bird’s eye view of the stone chamber}}]] The burial facility is a double-sleeved [[corridor-style stone chamber]] opening to the south. The dimensions of the chamber are as follows{{Sfn|Hirose Kazuo|2013}}: * Total length: 14.4 meters * Main chamber (*gen-shitsu*): length 5.2 meters, width 2.9 meters, current height 3.4 meters * Corridor (*sen-dō*): length 9.2 meters, width 1.7 meters, current height 1.8 meters The rear wall of the main chamber is assumed to consist of two stacked layers of stones (its details are obscured by the presence of a shrine). The side walls of the main chamber consist of two stones stacked in two layers, while the front wall is a single layer. The chamber entrance consists of a single stone, and the corridor walls are two layers. The ceiling stones comprise three stones in the main chamber and four in the corridor{{Sfn|Hirose Kazuo|2013}}. The dimensions of the chamber are as follows{{Sfn|Hirose Kazuo|2013}}: * Total length: 14.4 meters * Main chamber (*gen-shitsu*): length 5.2 meters, width 2.9 meters, current height 3.4 meters * Corridor (*sen-dō*): length 9.2 meters, width 1.7 meters, current height 1.8 meters The rear wall of the main chamber is assumed to consist of two stacked layers of stones (its details are obscured by the presence of a shrine). The side walls of the main chamber consist of two stones stacked in two layers, while the front wall is a single layer. The chamber entrance consists of a single stone, and the corridor walls are two layers. The ceiling stones comprise three stones in the main chamber and four in the corridor{{Sfn|Hirose Kazuo|2013}}. {{infobox ancient site |name = Kabutozuka Kofun |image = [[File:Kabutozuka Kofun (Kyoto), entrance.jpg|280px]]
Entrance to the stone chamber |other_names = Kabutozuka/Ōtsuka{{Sfn|Kabutozuka (Heibonsha)|1979}} |affiliation = |location = Sagakabutozuka-cho, [[Ukyo Ward, Kyoto|Ukyo Ward]], [[Kyoto]], [[Kyoto Prefecture]] |latitude = 35|lat_minutes = 1|lat_seconds = 10.80 |longitude = 135|long_minutes = 41|long_seconds = 24.85 |ISO = JP-26 |type = [[Round burial mound|Round mound (empun)]] |scale = Diameter: over 38 m
Height: over 5.5 m |burial_facility = Two-sleeved [[yokoana-style stone chamber|yokoana-style stone chamber]] |artifacts_found = |associated_burial_mounds = |construction_period = Early [[7th century]] |person_buried = (Presumed) member of the [[Hata clan (Japan)|Hata clan]] |imperial_mausoleum = |historical_site = None |tangible_cultural_properties = |notable_features = |map = Japan Kyoto city |icon = Empun |label = Kabutozuka Kofun |label_position = bottom }} '''Kabutozuka Kofun''' (甲塚古墳) is an ancient burial mound (kofun) located in Sagakabuto-cho, [[Ukyo Ward]], [[Kyoto City]], [[Kyoto Prefecture]]. The mound has a circular shape and is not designated as a historical site. == Overview == The Kabutozuka Kofun is a standalone, large circular burial mound built on the Sagano Plateau in the western part of the [[Kyoto Basin]]. The mound's name originates from its helmet-like appearance. Currently, a small shrine is enshrined within the inner chamber of the stone burial facility{{Sfn|Kabutozuka (Heibonsha)|1979}}. While the mound has suffered from looting, no formal excavations have been conducted. The mound is circular in shape, with an estimated diameter of 38 meters or more and a height exceeding 5.5 meters{{Sfn|Hirose Kazuo|2013}}. The burial facility is a double-sleeved [[corridor-style stone chamber]] (*yokoana-shiki sekishitsu*), which opens to the south. It is a large chamber measuring 14.4 meters in total length, constructed with massive stones. The funerary artifacts are not known{{Sfn|Kabutozuka (Heibonsha)|1979}}. The construction is estimated to date to the early 7th century, during the late Kofun period. It is considered one of the last burial mounds for regional chieftains in the Uzumasa-Sagano area, following the [[Jazuka Kofun]] and [[Maruyama Kofun]]. The mound is associated with the [[Hata clan]], a powerful immigrant lineage that developed the Sagano area{{Sfn|Hirose Kazuo|2013}}. Today, the kofun site is preserved within private property. == Burial Facility == [[File:Kabutozuka Kofun (Kyoto), 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Bird’s eye view of the stone chamber}}]] The burial facility is a double-sleeved [[corridor-style stone chamber]] opening to the south. The dimensions of the chamber are as follows{{Sfn|Hirose Kazuo|2013}}: * Total length: 14.4 meters * Main chamber (*gen-shitsu*): length 5.2 meters, width 2.9 meters, current height 3.4 meters * Corridor (*sen-dō*): length 9.2 meters, width 1.7 meters, current height 1.8 meters The rear wall of the main chamber is assumed to consist of two stacked layers of stones (its details are obscured by the presence of a shrine). The side walls of the main chamber consist of two stones stacked in two layers, while the front wall is a single layer. The chamber entrance consists of a single stone, and the corridor walls are two layers. The ceiling stones comprise three stones in the main chamber and four in the corridor{{Sfn|Hirose Kazuo|2013}}. File:Kabutozuka Kofun (Kyoto), 3D-P.jpg| Kabutozuka Kofun (Kyoto), 3D-2.jpg|Expanded view of the stone chamber Kabutozuka Kofun (Kyoto), genshitsu-1.jpg|Main chamber (towards the rear wall) Kabutozuka The chamber entrance consists of a single stone, and the corridor walls are two layers. The ceiling stones comprise three stones in the main chamber and four in the corridor{{Sfn|Hirose Kazuo|2013}}. Excavation Report of the Mitogagaike Group of Burial Mounds|publisher=Kyoto University Archaeology Research Association Publication Office|isbn=|ref=}} * {{Cite book|language=ja|editor=Kyoto City|author=|year=1983|chapter=The Kofun Era > Ukyo Ward|title=Historical Materials of Kyoto's History|volume=Vol. 2, Archaeology|publisher=[[Heibonsha]]|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Historical Materials of Kyoto Vol. 2|1983}}}} == External Links == * {{Commonscat-inline|Kabutozuka Kofun (Kyoto)}} [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Uzumasu-Sagano Burial Mounds]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Ancient Burial Mounds in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:History of Ukyo Ward]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Circular Burial Mounds]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Hata clan]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Uzumasu]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq deprecated open]] [[ja:甲塚古墳 (京都市)]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Kabutozuka Kofun (Kyoto)]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Kabutozuka Kofun (Kyoto)]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:mass bump]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:bump 1-25]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] {{Kofun navbar}} [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Kabutozuka Kofun (Kyoto)]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:Kabutozuka Kofun (Kyoto)]] [[Category:qqqq]] [[ja:甲塚古墳]] [[Category:null category]][[Category:new pages most likely untranslated or similar]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kabutozuka Kofun (Kyoto)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kabutozuka Kofun (Kyoto)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kabutozuka Kofun (Kyoto)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kabutozuka Kofun (Kyoto)]][[Category:qq]]

{{infobox ancient site |name = Kabutozuka Kofun |image = [[File:Kabutozuka Kofun (Kyoto), entrance.jpg|280px]]
Entrance to the stone chamber |other_names = Kabutozuka/Ōtsuka{{Sfn|Kabutozuka (Heibonsha)|1979}} |affiliation = |location = Sagakabutozuka-cho, [[Ukyo Ward, Kyoto|Ukyo Ward]], [[Kyoto]], [[Kyoto Prefecture]] |latitude = 35|lat_minutes = 1|lat_seconds = 10.80 |longitude = 135|long_minutes = 41|long_seconds = 24.85 |ISO = JP-26 |type = [[Round burial mound|Round mound (empun)]] |scale = Diameter: over 38 m
Height: over 5.5 m |burial_facility = Two-sleeved [[yokoana-style stone chamber|yokoana-style stone chamber]] |artifacts_found = |associated_burial_mounds = |construction_period = Early [[7th century]] |person_buried = (Presumed) member of the [[Hata clan (Japan)|Hata clan]] |imperial_mausoleum = |historical_site = None |tangible_cultural_properties = |notable_features = |map = Japan Kyoto city |icon = Empun |label = Kabutozuka Kofun |label_position = bottom }} '''Kabutozuka Kofun''' (甲塚古墳) is an ancient burial mound (kofun) located in Sagakabuto-cho, [[Ukyo Ward]], [[Kyoto City]], [[Kyoto Prefecture]]. The mound has a circular shape and is not designated as a historical site. == Overview == The Kabutozuka Kofun is a standalone, large circular burial mound built on the Sagano Plateau in the western part of the [[Kyoto Basin]]. The mound's name originates from its helmet-like appearance. Currently, a small shrine is enshrined within the inner chamber of the stone burial facility{{Sfn|Kabutozuka (Heibonsha)|1979}}. While the mound has suffered from looting, no formal excavations have been conducted. The mound is circular in shape, with an estimated diameter of 38 meters or more and a height exceeding 5.5 meters{{Sfn|Hirose Kazuo|2013}}. The burial facility is a double-sleeved [[corridor-style stone chamber]] (*yokoana-shiki sekishitsu*), which opens to the south. It is a large chamber measuring 14.4 meters in total length, constructed with massive stones. The funerary artifacts are not known{{Sfn|Kabutozuka (Heibonsha)|1979}}. The construction is estimated to date to the early 7th century, during the late Kofun period. It is considered one of the last burial mounds for regional chieftains in the Uzumasa-Sagano area, following the [[Jazuka Kofun]] and [[Maruyama Kofun]]. The mound is associated with the [[Hata clan]], a powerful immigrant lineage that developed the Sagano area{{Sfn|Hirose Kazuo|2013}}. Today, the kofun site is preserved within private property. == Burial Facility == [[File:Kabutozuka Kofun (Kyoto), 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Bird’s eye view of the stone chamber}}]] The burial facility is a double-sleeved [[corridor-style stone chamber]] opening to the south. The dimensions of the chamber are as follows{{Sfn|Hirose Kazuo|2013}}: * Total length: 14.4 meters * Main chamber (*gen-shitsu*): length 5.2 meters, width 2.9 meters, current height 3.4 meters * Corridor (*sen-dō*): length 9.2 meters, width 1.7 meters, current height 1.8 meters The rear wall of the main chamber is assumed to consist of two stacked layers of stones (its details are obscured by the presence of a shrine). The side walls of the main chamber consist of two stones stacked in two layers, while the front wall is a single layer. The chamber entrance consists of a single stone, and the corridor walls are two layers. The ceiling stones comprise three stones in the main chamber and four in the corridor{{Sfn|Hirose Kazuo|2013}}. File:Kabutozuka Kofun (Kyoto), 3D-P.jpg| Kabutozuka Kofun (Kyoto), 3D-2.jpg|Expanded view of the stone chamber Kabutozuka Kofun (Kyoto), genshitsu-1.jpg|Main chamber (towards the rear wall) Kabutozuka Kofun (Kyoto), genshitsu-2.jpg|Main chamber (towards the entrance) Kabutozuka Kofun (Kyoto), sendou-2.jpg|Corridor (towards the entrance) Kabutozuka Kofun (Kyoto), sendou-1.jpg|Corridor (towards the main chamber) Kabutozuka Kofun (Kyoto), funkyu.jpg|The burial mound == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|(References used in article compilation)}} * {{Cite book|language=ja|editor=|author=|year=1979|chapter=甲塚|title=京都市の地名|series=[[Japanese Historical Place Names Series]] 27|publisher=[[Heibonsha]]|isbn=978-4-582-49027-5|ref={{Harvid|Kabutozuka (Heibonsha)|1979}}}} * {{Cite book|author=[[Kazuo Hirose]]|year=2013|chapter=Questions Regarding Yamashiro's Jazuka Kofun|title=Research Reports of the National Museum of Japanese History|volume=Vol. 178|publisher=[[National Museum of Japanese History]]|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Hirose Kazuo|2013}}}} == Related Reading == {{small|(Related literature not used in this article)}} * {{Cite journal|language=ja|author=|title=Detailed Measurement Diagram of Kabutozuka Kofun, Ukyo Ward|date=1964|journal=Trench|publisher=Kyoto University Archaeology Research Association|volume=|number=11|pages=|url=|naid=|doi=|ref=}} * {{Cite book|language=ja|editor=[[Kyoto University]] Archaeology Research Association|author=|year=1971|chapter=Overview of Burial Mounds > Kabutozuka Kofun|title=The Kofun Era of Sagano - Excavation Report of the Mitogagaike Group of Burial Mounds|publisher=Kyoto University Archaeology Research Association Publication Office|isbn=|ref=}} * {{Cite book|language=ja|editor=Kyoto City|author=|year=1983|chapter=The Kofun Era > Ukyo Ward|title=Historical Materials of Kyoto's History|volume=Vol. 2, Archaeology|publisher=[[Heibonsha]]|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Historical Materials of Kyoto Vol. 2|1983}}}} == External Links == * {{Commonscat-inline|Kabutozuka Kofun (Kyoto)}} [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Uzumasu-Sagano Burial Mounds]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Ancient Burial Mounds in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:History of Ukyo Ward]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Circular Burial Mounds]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Hata clan]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Uzumasu]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[ja:甲塚古墳 (京都市)]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Kabutozuka Kofun (Kyoto)]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Kabutozuka Kofun (Kyoto)]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:mass bump]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:bump 1-25]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] {{Kofun navbar}} [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Kabutozuka Kofun (Kyoto)]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:Kabutozuka Kofun (Kyoto)]] [[Category:qqqq]] [[ja:甲塚古墳]] [[Category:null category]][[Category:new pages most likely untranslated or similar]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kabutozuka Kofun (Kyoto)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kabutozuka Kofun (Kyoto)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kabutozuka Kofun (Kyoto)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kabutozuka Kofun (Kyoto)]][[Category:qq]]
