{{infobox ancient site |name = Kabutozuka Kofun |image = [[File:Kabutozuka Kofun (Kyoto), entrance.jpg|280px]]
Entrance to the stone chamber |other_names = Kabutozuka/Ōtsuka{{Sfn|Kabutozuka (Heibonsha)|1979}} |affiliation = |location = Sagakabutozuka-cho, [[Ukyo Ward, Kyoto|Ukyo Ward]], [[Kyoto]], [[Kyoto Prefecture]] |latitude = 35|lat_minutes = 1|lat_seconds = 10.80 |longitude = 135|long_minutes = 41|long_seconds = 24.85 |ISO = JP-26 |type = [[Round burial mound|Round mound (empun)]] |scale = Diameter: over 38 m
Height: over 5.5 m |burial_facility = Two-sleeved [[yokoana-style stone chamber|yokoana-style stone chamber]] |artifacts_found = |associated_burial_mounds = |construction_period = Early [[7th century]] |person_buried = (Presumed) member of the [[Hata clan (Japan)|Hata clan]] |imperial_mausoleum = |historical_site = None |tangible_cultural_properties = |notable_features = |map = Japan Kyoto city |icon = Empun |label = Kabutozuka Kofun |label_position = bottom }} '''Kabutozuka Kofun''' (甲塚古墳) is an ancient burial mound (kofun) located in Sagakabuto-cho, [[Ukyo Ward]], [[Kyoto City]], [[Kyoto Prefecture]]. The mound has a circular shape and is not designated as a historical site. == Overview == The Kabutozuka Kofun is a standalone, large circular burial mound built on the Sagano Plateau in the western part of the [[Kyoto Basin]]. The mound's name originates from its helmet-like appearance. Currently, a small shrine is enshrined within the inner chamber of the stone burial facility{{Sfn|Kabutozuka (Heibonsha)|1979}}. While the mound has suffered from looting, no formal excavations have been conducted. The mound is circular in shape, with an estimated diameter of 38 meters or more and a height exceeding 5.5 meters{{Sfn|Hirose Kazuo|2013}}. The burial facility is a double-sleeved [[corridor-style stone chamber]] (*yokoana-shiki sekishitsu*), which opens to the south. It is a large chamber measuring 14.4 meters in total length, constructed with massive stones. The funerary artifacts are not known{{Sfn|Kabutozuka (Heibonsha)|1979}}. The construction is estimated to date to the early 7th century, during the late Kofun period. It is considered one of the last burial mounds for regional chieftains in the Uzumasa-Sagano area, following the [[Jazuka Kofun]] and [[Maruyama Kofun]]. The mound is associated with the [[Hata clan]], a powerful immigrant lineage that developed the Sagano area{{Sfn|Hirose Kazuo|2013}}. Today, the kofun site is preserved within private property. == Burial Facility == [[File:Kabutozuka Kofun (Kyoto), 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Bird’s eye view of the stone chamber}}]] The burial facility is a double-sleeved [[corridor-style stone chamber]] opening to the south. The dimensions of the chamber are as follows{{Sfn|Hirose Kazuo|2013}}: * Total length: 14.4 meters * Main chamber (*gen-shitsu*): length 5.2 meters, width 2.9 meters, current height 3.4 meters * Corridor (*sen-dō*): length 9.2 meters, width 1.7 meters, current height 1.8 meters The rear wall of the main chamber is assumed to consist of two stacked layers of stones (its details are obscured by the presence of a shrine). The side walls of the main chamber consist of two stones stacked in two layers, while the front wall is a single layer. The chamber entrance consists of a single stone, and the corridor walls are two layers. The ceiling stones comprise three stones in the main chamber and four in the corridor{{Sfn|Hirose Kazuo|2013}}. File:Kabutozuka Kofun (Kyoto), 3D-P.jpg| Kabutozuka Kofun (Kyoto), 3D-2.jpg|Expanded view of the stone chamber Kabutozuka Kofun (Kyoto), genshitsu-1.jpg|Main chamber (towards the rear wall) Kabutozuka Kofun (Kyoto), genshitsu-2.jpg|Main chamber (towards the entrance) Kabutozuka Kofun (Kyoto), sendou-2.jpg|Corridor (towards the entrance) Kabutozuka Kofun (Kyoto), sendou-1.jpg|Corridor (towards the main chamber) Kabutozuka Kofun (Kyoto), funkyu.jpg|The burial mound == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|(References used in article compilation)}} * {{Cite book|language=ja|editor=|author=|year=1979|chapter=甲塚|title=京都市の地名|series=[[Japanese Historical Place Names Series]] 27|publisher=[[Heibonsha]]|isbn=978-4-582-49027-5|ref={{Harvid|Kabutozuka (Heibonsha)|1979}}}} * {{Cite book|author=[[Kazuo Hirose]]|year=2013|chapter=Questions Regarding Yamashiro's Jazuka Kofun|title=Research Reports of the National Museum of Japanese History|volume=Vol. 178|publisher=[[National Museum of Japanese History]]|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Hirose Kazuo|2013}}}} == Related Reading == {{small|(Related literature not used in this article)}} * {{Cite journal|language=ja|author=|title=Detailed Measurement Diagram of Kabutozuka Kofun, Ukyo Ward|date=1964|journal=Trench|publisher=Kyoto University Archaeology Research Association|volume=|number=11|pages=|url=|naid=|doi=|ref=}} * {{Cite book|language=ja|editor=[[Kyoto University]] Archaeology Research Association|author=|year=1971|chapter=Overview of Burial Mounds > Kabutozuka Kofun|title=The Kofun Era of Sagano - Excavation Report of the Mitogagaike Group of Burial Mounds|publisher=Kyoto University Archaeology Research Association Publication Office|isbn=|ref=}} * {{Cite book|language=ja|editor=Kyoto City|author=|year=1983|chapter=The Kofun Era > Ukyo Ward|title=Historical Materials of Kyoto's History|volume=Vol. 2, Archaeology|publisher=[[Heibonsha]]|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Historical Materials of Kyoto Vol. 2|1983}}}} == External Links == * {{Commonscat-inline|Kabutozuka Kofun (Kyoto)}} [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Uzumasu-Sagano Burial Mounds]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Ancient Burial Mounds in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:History of Ukyo Ward]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Circular Burial Mounds]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Hata clan]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Uzumasu]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Ugoodbye $1 completion categories]] [[ja:甲塚古墳 (京都市)]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Kabutozuka Kofun (Kyoto)]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Kabutozuka Kofun (Kyoto)]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:mass bump]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:bump 1-25]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] {{Kofun navbar}} [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Kabutozuka Kofun (Kyoto)]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:Kabutozuka Kofun (Kyoto)]] [[Category:qq]] [[ja:甲塚古墳]] [[Category:null category]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kabutozuka Kofun (Kyoto)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kabutozuka Kofun (Kyoto)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kabutozuka Kofun (Kyoto)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kabutozuka Kofun (Kyoto)]][[Category:qq]]

Entrance to the stone chamber |other_names = Kabutozuka/Ōtsuka{{Sfn|Kabutozuka (Heibonsha)|1979}} |affiliation = |location = Sagakabutozuka-cho, [[Ukyo Ward, Kyoto|Ukyo Ward]], [[Kyoto]], [[Kyoto Prefecture]] |latitude = 35|lat_minutes = 1|lat_seconds = 10.80 |longitude = 135|long_minutes = 41|long_seconds = 24.85 |ISO = JP-26 |type = [[Round burial mound|Round mound (empun)]] |scale = Diameter: over 38 m
Height: over 5.5 m |burial_facility = Two-sleeved [[yokoana-style stone chamber|yokoana-style stone chamber]] |artifacts_found = |associated_burial_mounds = |construction_period = Early [[7th century]] |person_buried = (Presumed) member of the [[Hata clan (Japan)|Hata clan]] |imperial_mausoleum = |historical_site = None |tangible_cultural_properties = |notable_features = |map = Japan Kyoto city |icon = Empun |label = Kabutozuka Kofun |label_position = bottom }} '''Kabutozuka Kofun''' (甲塚古墳) is an ancient burial mound (kofun) located in Sagakabuto-cho, [[Ukyo Ward]], [[Kyoto City]], [[Kyoto Prefecture]]. The mound has a circular shape and is not designated as a historical site. == Overview == The Kabutozuka Kofun is a standalone, large circular burial mound built on the Sagano Plateau in the western part of the [[Kyoto Basin]]. The mound's name originates from its helmet-like appearance. Currently, a small shrine is enshrined within the inner chamber of the stone burial facility{{Sfn|Kabutozuka (Heibonsha)|1979}}. While the mound has suffered from looting, no formal excavations have been conducted. The mound is circular in shape, with an estimated diameter of 38 meters or more and a height exceeding 5.5 meters{{Sfn|Hirose Kazuo|2013}}. The burial facility is a double-sleeved [[corridor-style stone chamber]] (*yokoana-shiki sekishitsu*), which opens to the south. It is a large chamber measuring 14.4 meters in total length, constructed with massive stones. The funerary artifacts are not known{{Sfn|Kabutozuka (Heibonsha)|1979}}. The construction is estimated to date to the early 7th century, during the late Kofun period. It is considered one of the last burial mounds for regional chieftains in the Uzumasa-Sagano area, following the [[Jazuka Kofun]] and [[Maruyama Kofun]]. The mound is associated with the [[Hata clan]], a powerful immigrant lineage that developed the Sagano area{{Sfn|Hirose Kazuo|2013}}. Today, the kofun site is preserved within private property. == Burial Facility == [[File:Kabutozuka Kofun (Kyoto), 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Bird’s eye view of the stone chamber}}]] The burial facility is a double-sleeved [[corridor-style stone chamber]] opening to the south. The dimensions of the chamber are as follows{{Sfn|Hirose Kazuo|2013}}: * Total length: 14.4 meters * Main chamber (*gen-shitsu*): length 5.2 meters, width 2.9 meters, current height 3.4 meters * Corridor (*sen-dō*): length 9.2 meters, width 1.7 meters, current height 1.8 meters The rear wall of the main chamber is assumed to consist of two stacked layers of stones (its details are obscured by the presence of a shrine). The side walls of the main chamber consist of two stones stacked in two layers, while the front wall is a single layer. The chamber entrance consists of a single stone, and the corridor walls are two layers. The ceiling stones comprise three stones in the main chamber and four in the corridor{{Sfn|Hirose Kazuo|2013}}. The mound has a circular shape and is not designated as a historical site. == Overview == The Kabutozuka Kofun is a standalone, large circular burial mound built on the Sagano Plateau in the western part of the [[Kyoto Basin]]. The mound's name originates from its helmet-like appearance. Currently, a small shrine is enshrined within the inner chamber of the stone burial facility{{Sfn|Kabutozuka (Heibonsha)|1979}}. While the mound has suffered from looting, no formal excavations have been conducted. The construction is estimated to date to the early 7th century, during the late Kofun period. It is considered one of the last burial mounds for regional chieftains in the Uzumasa-Sagano area, following the [[Jazuka Kofun]] and [[Maruyama Kofun]]. The mound is associated with the [[Hata clan]], a powerful immigrant lineage that developed the Sagano area{{Sfn|Hirose Kazuo|2013}}. The side walls of the main chamber consist of two stones stacked in two layers, while the front wall is a single layer. The chamber entrance consists of a single stone, and the corridor walls are two layers. The ceiling stones comprise three stones in the main chamber and four in the corridor{{Sfn|Hirose Kazuo|2013}}. {{infobox ancient site |name = Kabutozuka Kofun |image = [[File:Kabutozuka Kofun (Kyoto), entrance.jpg|280px]]
Entrance to the stone chamber |other_names = Kabutozuka/Ōtsuka{{Sfn|Kabutozuka (Heibonsha)|1979}} |affiliation = |location = Sagakabutozuka-cho, [[Ukyo Ward, Kyoto|Ukyo Ward]], [[Kyoto]], [[Kyoto Prefecture]] |latitude = 35|lat_minutes = 1|lat_seconds = 10.80 |longitude = 135|long_minutes = 41|long_seconds = 24.85 |ISO = JP-26 |type = [[Round burial mound|Round mound (empun)]] |scale = Diameter: over 38 m
Height: over 5.5 m |burial_facility = Two-sleeved [[yokoana-style stone chamber|yokoana-style stone chamber]] |artifacts_found = |associated_burial_mounds = |construction_period = Early [[7th century]] |person_buried = (Presumed) member of the [[Hata clan (Japan)|Hata clan]] |imperial_mausoleum = |historical_site = None |tangible_cultural_properties = |notable_features = |map = Japan Kyoto city |icon = Empun |label = Kabutozuka Kofun |label_position = bottom }} '''Kabutozuka Kofun''' (甲塚古墳) is an ancient burial mound (kofun) located in Sagakabuto-cho, [[Ukyo Ward]], [[Kyoto City]], [[Kyoto Prefecture]]. The mound has a circular shape and is not designated as a historical site. == Overview == The Kabutozuka Kofun is a standalone, large circular burial mound built on the Sagano Plateau in the western part of the [[Kyoto Basin]]. The mound's name originates from its helmet-like appearance. Currently, a small shrine is enshrined within the inner chamber of the stone burial facility{{Sfn|Kabutozuka (Heibonsha)|1979}}. While the mound has suffered from looting, no formal excavations have been conducted. The mound is circular in shape, with an estimated diameter of 38 meters or more and a height exceeding 5.5 meters{{Sfn|Hirose Kazuo|2013}}. The burial facility is a double-sleeved [[corridor-style stone chamber]] (*yokoana-shiki sekishitsu*), which opens to the south. It is a large chamber measuring 14.4 meters in total length, constructed with massive stones. The funerary artifacts are not known{{Sfn|Kabutozuka (Heibonsha)|1979}}. The construction is estimated to date to the early 7th century, during the late Kofun period. It is considered one of the last burial mounds for regional chieftains in the Uzumasa-Sagano area, following the [[Jazuka Kofun]] and [[Maruyama Kofun]]. The mound is associated with the [[Hata clan]], a powerful immigrant lineage that developed the Sagano area{{Sfn|Hirose Kazuo|2013}}. Today, the kofun site is preserved within private property. == Burial Facility == [[File:Kabutozuka Kofun (Kyoto), 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Bird’s eye view of the stone chamber}}]] The burial facility is a double-sleeved [[corridor-style stone chamber]] opening to the south. The dimensions of the chamber are as follows{{Sfn|Hirose Kazuo|2013}}: * Total length: 14.4 meters * Main chamber (*gen-shitsu*): length 5.2 meters, width 2.9 meters, current height 3.4 meters * Corridor (*sen-dō*): length 9.2 meters, width 1.7 meters, current height 1.8 meters The rear wall of the main chamber is assumed to consist of two stacked layers of stones (its details are obscured by the presence of a shrine). The side walls of the main chamber consist of two stones stacked in two layers, while the front wall is a single layer. The chamber entrance consists of a single stone, and the corridor walls are two layers. The ceiling stones comprise three stones in the main chamber and four in the corridor{{Sfn|Hirose Kazuo|2013}}. {{infobox ancient site |name = Kabutozuka Kofun |image = [[File:Kabutozuka Kofun (Kyoto), entrance.jpg|280px]]
Entrance to the stone chamber |other_names = Kabutozuka/Ōtsuka{{Sfn|Kabutozuka (Heibonsha)|1979}} |affiliation = |location = Sagakabutozuka-cho, [[Ukyo Ward, Kyoto|Ukyo Ward]], [[Kyoto]], [[Kyoto Prefecture]] |latitude = 35|lat_minutes = 1|lat_seconds = 10.80 |longitude = 135|long_minutes = 41|long_seconds = 24.85 |ISO = JP-26 |type = [[Round Kofun|Round mound (empun)]] |scale = Diameter: over 38 m
Height: over 5.5 m |burial_facility = Two-sleeved [[yokoana-style stone chamber|yokoana-style stone chamber]] |artifacts_found = |associated_burial_mounds = |construction_period = Early [[7th century]] |person_buried = (Presumed) member of the [[Hata clan (Japan)|Hata clan]] |imperial_mausoleum = |historical_site = None |tangible_cultural_properties = |notable_features = |map = Japan Kyoto city |icon = Empun |label = Kabutozuka Kofun |label_position = bottom }} '''Kabutozuka Kofun''' (甲塚古墳) is an ancient Kofun (kofun) located in Sagakabuto-cho, [[Ukyo Ward]], [[Kyoto City]], [[Kyoto Prefecture]]. The mound has a circular shape and is not designated as a historical site. == Overview == The Kabutozuka Kofun is a standalone, large circular Kofun built on the Sagano Plateau in the western part of the [[Kyoto Basin]]. The mound's name originates from its helmet-like appearance. Currently, a small shrine is enshrined within the inner chamber of the stone burial facility{{Sfn|Kabutozuka (Heibonsha)|1979}}. While the mound has suffered from looting, no formal excavations have been conducted. The mound is circular in shape, with an estimated diameter of 38 meters or more and a height exceeding 5.5 meters{{Sfn|Hirose Kazuo|2013}}. The burial facility is a double-sleeved [[corridor-style stone chamber]] (*yokoana-shiki sekishitsu*), which opens to the south. It is a large chamber measuring 14.4 meters in total length, constructed with massive stones. The funerary artifacts are not known{{Sfn|Kabutozuka (Heibonsha)|1979}}. The construction is estimated to date to the early 7th century, during the late Kofun period. It is considered one of the last Kofuns for regional chieftains in the Uzumasa-Sagano area, following the [[Jazuka Kofun]] and [[Maruyama Kofun]]. The mound is associated with the [[Hata clan]], a powerful immigrant lineage that developed the Sagano area{{Sfn|Hirose Kazuo|2013}}. Today, the kofun site is preserved within private property. == Burial Facility == [[File:Kabutozuka Kofun (Kyoto), 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Bird’s eye view of the stone chamber}}]] The burial facility is a double-sleeved [[corridor-style stone chamber]] opening to the south. The dimensions of the chamber are as follows{{Sfn|Hirose Kazuo|2013}}: * Total length: 14.4 meters * Main chamber (*gen-shitsu*): length 5.2 meters, width 2.9 meters, current height 3.4 meters * Corridor (*sen-dō*): length 9.2 meters, width 1.7 meters, current height 1.8 meters The rear wall of the main chamber is assumed to consist of two stacked layers of stones (its details are obscured by the presence of a shrine). The side walls of the main chamber consist of two stones stacked in two layers, while the front wall is a single layer. The chamber entrance consists of a single stone, and the corridor walls are two layers. The ceiling stones comprise three stones in the main chamber and four in the corridor{{Sfn|Hirose Kazuo|2013}}. While the mound has suffered from looting, no formal excavations have been conducted. The mound is circular in shape, with an estimated diameter of 38 meters or more and a height exceeding 5.5 meters{{Sfn|Hirose Kazuo|2013}}. The burial facility is a double-sleeved [[corridor-style stone chamber]] (*yokoana-shiki sekishitsu*), which opens to the south. It is a large chamber measuring 14.4 meters in total length, constructed with massive stones. The funerary artifacts are not known{{Sfn|Kabutozuka (Heibonsha)|1979}}. The construction is estimated to date to the early 7th century, during the late Kofun period. It is considered one of the last Kofuns for regional chieftains in the Uzumasa-Sagano area, following the [[Jazuka Kofun]] and [[Maruyama Kofun]]. The mound is associated with the [[Hata clan]], a powerful immigrant lineage that developed the Sagano area{{Sfn|Hirose Kazuo|2013}}. Today, the kofun site is preserved within private property. == Burial Facility == [[File:Kabutozuka Kofun (Kyoto), 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Bird’s eye view of the stone chamber}}]] The burial facility is a double-sleeved [[corridor-style stone chamber]] opening to the south. The dimensions of the chamber are as follows{{Sfn|Hirose Kazuo|2013}}: * Total length: 14.4 meters * Main chamber (*gen-shitsu*): length 5.2 meters, width 2.9 meters, current height 3.4 meters * Corridor (*sen-dō*): length 9.2 meters, width 1.7 meters, current height 1.8 meters The rear wall of the main chamber is assumed to consist of two stacked layers of stones (its details are obscured by the presence of a shrine). The side walls of the main chamber consist of two stones stacked in two layers, while the front wall is a single layer. The chamber entrance consists of a single stone, and the corridor walls are two layers. The ceiling stones comprise three stones in the main chamber and four in the corridor{{Sfn|Hirose Kazuo|2013}}. {{infobox ancient site |name = Kabutozuka Kofun |image = [[File:Kabutozuka Kofun (Kyoto), entrance.jpg|280px]]
Entrance to the stone chamber |other_names = Kabutozuka/Ōtsuka{{Sfn|Kabutozuka (Heibonsha)|1979}} |affiliation = |location = Sagakabutozuka-cho, [[Ukyo Ward, Kyoto|Ukyo Ward]], [[Kyoto]], [[Kyoto Prefecture]] |latitude = 35|lat_minutes = 1|lat_seconds = 10.80 |longitude = 135|long_minutes = 41|long_seconds = 24.85 |ISO = JP-26 |type = [[Round Kofun|Round mound (empun)]] |scale = Diameter: over 38 m
Height: over 5.5 m |burial_facility = Two-sleeved [[yokoana-style stone chamber|yokoana-style stone chamber]] |artifacts_found = |associated_burial_mounds = |construction_period = Early [[7th century]] |person_buried = (Presumed) member of the [[Hata clan (Japan)|Hata clan]] |imperial_mausoleum = |historical_site = None |tangible_cultural_properties = |notable_features = |map = Japan Kyoto city |icon = Empun |label = Kabutozuka Kofun |label_position = bottom }} '''Kabutozuka Kofun''' (甲塚古墳) is an ancient Kofun (kofun) located in Sagakabuto-cho, [[Ukyo Ward]], [[Kyoto City]], [[Kyoto Prefecture]]. The mound has a circular shape and is not designated as a historical site. == Overview == The Kabutozuka Kofun is a standalone, large circular Kofun built on the Sagano Plateau in the western part of the [[Kyoto Basin]]. The mound's name originates from its helmet-like appearance. Currently, a small shrine is enshrined within the inner chamber of the stone burial facility{{Sfn|Kabutozuka (Heibonsha)|1979}}. While the mound has suffered from looting, no formal excavations have been conducted. The mound is circular in shape, with an estimated diameter of 38 meters or more and a height exceeding 5.5 meters{{Sfn|Hirose Kazuo|2013}}. The burial facility is a double-sleeved [[corridor-style stone chamber]] (*yokoana-shiki sekishitsu*), which opens to the south. It is a large chamber measuring 14.4 meters in total length, constructed with massive stones. The funerary artifacts are not known{{Sfn|Kabutozuka (Heibonsha)|1979}}. The construction is estimated to date to the early 7th century, during the late Kofun period. It is considered one of the last Kofuns for regional chieftains in the Uzumasa-Sagano area, following the [[Jazuka Kofun]] and [[Maruyama Kofun]]. The mound is associated with the [[Hata clan]], a powerful immigrant lineage that developed the Sagano area{{Sfn|Hirose Kazuo|2013}}. Today, the kofun site is preserved within private property. == Burial Facility == [[File:Kabutozuka Kofun (Kyoto), 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Bird’s eye view of the stone chamber}}]] The burial facility is a double-sleeved [[corridor-style stone chamber]] opening to the south. The dimensions of the chamber are as follows{{Sfn|Hirose Kazuo|2013}}: * Total length: 14.4 meters * Main chamber (*gen-shitsu*): length 5.2 meters, width 2.9 meters, current height 3.4 meters * Corridor (*sen-dō*): length 9.2 meters, width 1.7 meters, current height 1.8 meters The rear wall of the main chamber is assumed to consist of two stacked layers of stones (its details are obscured by the presence of a shrine). The side walls of the main chamber consist of two stones stacked in two layers, while the front wall is a single layer. The chamber entrance consists of a single stone, and the corridor walls are two layers. The ceiling stones comprise three stones in the main chamber and four in the corridor{{Sfn|Hirose Kazuo|2013}}. revision.comment: moving the kofun template to the bottom of each page
revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{infobox ancient site |name = Kabutozuka Kofun |image = [[File:Kabutozuka Kofun (Kyoto), entrance.jpg|280px]]
Entrance to the stone chamber |other_names = Kabutozuka/Ōtsuka{{Sfn|Kabutozuka (Heibonsha)|1979}} |affiliation = |location = Sagakabutozuka-cho, [[Ukyo Ward, Kyoto|Ukyo Ward]], [[Kyoto]], [[Kyoto Prefecture]] |latitude = 35|lat_minutes = 1|lat_seconds = 10.80 |longitude = 135|long_minutes = 41|long_seconds = 24.85 |ISO = JP-26 |type = [[Round Kofun|Round mound (empun)]] |scale = Diameter: over 38 m
Height: over 5.5 m |burial_facility = Two-sleeved [[yokoana-style stone chamber|yokoana-style stone chamber]] |artifacts_found = |associated_burial_mounds = |construction_period = Early [[7th century]] |person_buried = (Presumed) member of the [[Hata clan (Japan)|Hata clan]] |imperial_mausoleum = |historical_site = None |tangible_cultural_properties = |notable_features = |map = Japan Kyoto city |icon = Empun |label = Kabutozuka Kofun |label_position = bottom }} '''Kabutozuka Kofun''' (甲塚古墳) is an ancient Kofun (kofun) located in Sagakabuto-cho, [[Ukyo Ward]], [[Kyoto City]], [[Kyoto Prefecture]]. The mound has a circular shape and is not designated as a historical site. == Overview == The Kabutozuka Kofun is a standalone, large circular Kofun built on the Sagano Plateau in the western part of the [[Kyoto Basin]]. The mound's name originates from its helmet-like appearance. Currently, a small shrine is enshrined within the inner chamber of the stone burial facility{{Sfn|Kabutozuka (Heibonsha)|1979}}. While the mound has suffered from looting, no formal excavations have been conducted. The mound is circular in shape, with an estimated diameter of 38 meters or more and a height exceeding 5.5 meters{{Sfn|Hirose Kazuo|2013}}. The burial facility is a double-sleeved [[corridor-style stone chamber]] (*yokoana-shiki sekishitsu*), which opens to the south. It is a large chamber measuring 14.4 meters in total length, constructed with massive stones. The funerary artifacts are not known{{Sfn|Kabutozuka (Heibonsha)|1979}}. The construction is estimated to date to the early 7th century, during the late Kofun period. It is considered one of the last Kofuns for regional chieftains in the Uzumasa-Sagano area, following the [[Jazuka Kofun]] and [[Maruyama Kofun]]. The mound is associated with the [[Hata clan]], a powerful immigrant lineage that developed the Sagano area{{Sfn|Hirose Kazuo|2013}}. Today, the kofun site is preserved within private property. == Burial Facility == [[File:Kabutozuka Kofun (Kyoto), 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Bird’s eye view of the stone chamber}}]] The burial facility is a double-sleeved [[corridor-style stone chamber]] opening to the south. The dimensions of the chamber are as follows{{Sfn|Hirose Kazuo|2013}}: * Total length: 14.4 meters * Main chamber (*gen-shitsu*): length 5.2 meters, width 2.9 meters, current height 3.4 meters * Corridor (*sen-dō*): length 9.2 meters, width 1.7 meters, current height 1.8 meters The rear wall of the main chamber is assumed to consist of two stacked layers of stones (its details are obscured by the presence of a shrine). The side walls of the main chamber consist of two stones stacked in two layers, while the front wall is a single layer. The chamber entrance consists of a single stone, and the corridor walls are two layers. The ceiling stones comprise three stones in the main chamber and four in the corridor{{Sfn|Hirose Kazuo|2013}}. File:Kabutozuka Kofun (Kyoto), 3D-P.jpg| Kabutozuka Kofun (Kyoto), 3D-2.jpg|Expanded view of the stone chamber Kabutozuka Kofun (Kyoto), genshitsu-1.jpg|Main chamber (towards the rear wall) Kabutozuka Kofun (Kyoto), genshitsu-2.jpg|Main chamber (towards the entrance) Kabutozuka Kofun (Kyoto), sendou-2.jpg|Corridor (towards the entrance) Kabutozuka Kofun (Kyoto), sendou-1.jpg|Corridor (towards the main chamber) Kabutozuka Kofun (Kyoto), funkyu.jpg|The Kofun == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|(References used in article compilation)}} * {{Cite book|language=ja|editor=|author=|year=1979|chapter=甲塚|title=京都市の地名|series=[[Japanese Historical Place Names Series]] 27|publisher=[[Heibonsha]]|isbn=978-4-582-49027-5|ref={{Harvid|Kabutozuka (Heibonsha)|1979}}}} * {{Cite book|author=[[Kazuo Hirose]]|year=2013|chapter=Questions Regarding Yamashiro's Jazuka Kofun|title=Research Reports of the National Museum of Japanese History|volume=Vol. 178|publisher=[[National Museum of Japanese History]]|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Hirose Kazuo|2013}}}} == Related Reading == {{small|(Related literature not used in this article)}} * {{Cite journal|language=ja|author=|title=Detailed Measurement Diagram of Kabutozuka Kofun, Ukyo Ward|date=1964|journal=Trench|publisher=Kyoto University Archaeology Research Association|volume=|number=11|pages=|url=|naid=|doi=|ref=}} * {{Cite book|language=ja|editor=[[Kyoto University]] Archaeology Research Association|author=|year=1971|chapter=Overview of Kofuns > Kabutozuka Kofun|title=The Kofun Era of Sagano - Excavation Report of the Mitogagaike Group of Kofuns|publisher=Kyoto University Archaeology Research Association Publication Office|isbn=|ref=}} * {{Cite book|language=ja|editor=Kyoto City|author=|year=1983|chapter=The Kofun Era > Ukyo Ward|title=Historical Materials of Kyoto's History|volume=Vol. 2, Archaeology|publisher=[[Heibonsha]]|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Historical Materials of Kyoto Vol. 2|1983}}}} == External Links == * {{Commonscat-inline|Kabutozuka Kofun (Kyoto)}} revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{infobox ancient site |name = Kabutozuka Kofun |image = [[File:Kabutozuka Kofun (Kyoto), entrance.jpg|280px]]
Entrance to the stone chamber |other_names = Kabutozuka/Ōtsuka{{Sfn|Kabutozuka (Heibonsha)|1979}} |affiliation = |location = Sagakabutozuka-cho, [[Ukyo Ward, Kyoto|Ukyo Ward]], [[Kyoto]], [[Kyoto Prefecture]] |latitude = 35|lat_minutes = 1|lat_seconds = 10.80 |longitude = 135|long_minutes = 41|long_seconds = 24.85 |ISO = JP-26 |type = [[Round Kofun|Round mound (empun)]] |scale = Diameter: over 38 m
Height: over 5.5 m |burial_facility = Two-sleeved [[yokoana-style stone chamber|yokoana-style stone chamber]] |burial goods_found = |associated_burial_mounds = |construction_period = Early [[7th century]] |person_buried = (Presumed) member of the [[Hata clan (Japan)|Hata clan]] |imperial_mausoleum = |historical_site = None |tangible_cultural_properties = |notable_features = |map = Japan Kyoto city |icon = Empun |label = Kabutozuka Kofun |label_position = bottom }} '''Kabutozuka Kofun''' (甲塚古墳) is an ancient Kofun (kofun) located in Sagakabuto-cho, [[Ukyo Ward]], [[Kyoto City]], [[Kyoto Prefecture]]. The mound has a circular shape and is not designated as a historical site. == Overview == The Kabutozuka Kofun is a standalone, large circular Kofun built on the Sagano Plateau in the western part of the [[Kyoto Basin]]. The mound's name originates from its helmet-like appearance. Currently, a small shrine is enshrined within the inner chamber of the stone burial facility{{Sfn|Kabutozuka (Heibonsha)|1979}}. While the mound has suffered from looting, no formal excavations have been conducted. The mound is circular in shape, with an estimated diameter of 38 meters or more and a height exceeding 5.5 meters{{Sfn|Hirose Kazuo|2013}}. The burial facility is a double-sleeved [[corridor-style stone chamber]] (*yokoana-shiki sekishitsu*), which opens to the south. It is a large chamber measuring 14.4 meters in total length, constructed with massive stones. The funerary artifacts are not known{{Sfn|Kabutozuka (Heibonsha)|1979}}. The construction is estimated to date to the early 7th century, during the late Kofun period. It is considered one of the last Kofuns for regional chieftains in the Uzumasa-Sagano area, following the [[Jazuka Kofun]] and [[Maruyama Kofun]]. The mound is associated with the [[Hata clan]], a powerful immigrant lineage that developed the Sagano area{{Sfn|Hirose Kazuo|2013}}. Today, the kofun site is preserved within private property. == Burial Facility == [[File:Kabutozuka Kofun (Kyoto), 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Bird’s eye view of the stone chamber}}]] The burial facility is a double-sleeved [[corridor-style stone chamber]] opening to the south. The dimensions of the chamber are as follows{{Sfn|Hirose Kazuo|2013}}: * Total length: 14.4 meters * Main chamber (*gen-shitsu*): length 5.2 meters, width 2.9 meters, current height 3.4 meters * Corridor (*sen-dō*): length 9.2 meters, width 1.7 meters, current height 1.8 meters The rear wall of the main chamber is assumed to consist of two stacked layers of stones (its details are obscured by the presence of a shrine). The side walls of the main chamber consist of two stones stacked in two layers, while the front wall is a single layer. The chamber entrance consists of a single stone, and the corridor walls are two layers. The ceiling stones comprise three stones in the main chamber and four in the corridor{{Sfn|Hirose Kazuo|2013}}. File:Kabutozuka Kofun (Kyoto), 3D-P.jpg| Kabutozuka Kofun (Kyoto), 3D-2.jpg|Expanded view of the stone chamber Kabutozuka Kofun (Kyoto), genshitsu-1.jpg|Main chamber (towards the rear wall) Kabutozuka Kofun (Kyoto), genshitsu-2.jpg|Main chamber (towards the entrance) Kabutozuka Kofun (Kyoto), sendou-2.jpg|Corridor (towards the entrance) Kabutozuka Kofun (Kyoto), sendou-1.jpg|Corridor (towards the main chamber) Kabutozuka Kofun (Kyoto), funkyu.jpg|The Kofun == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|(References used in article compilation)}} * {{Cite book|language=ja|editor=|author=|year=1979|chapter=甲塚|title=京都市の地名|series=[[Japanese Historical Place Names Series]] 27|publisher=[[Heibonsha]]|isbn=978-4-582-49027-5|ref={{Harvid|Kabutozuka (Heibonsha)|1979}}}} * {{Cite book|author=[[Kazuo Hirose]]|year=2013|chapter=Questions Regarding Yamashiro's Jazuka Kofun|title=Research Reports of the National Museum of Japanese History|volume=Vol. 178|publisher=[[National Museum of Japanese History]]|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Hirose Kazuo|2013}}}} == Related Reading == {{small|(Related literature not used in this article)}} * {{Cite journal|language=ja|author=|title=Detailed Measurement Diagram of Kabutozuka Kofun, Ukyo Ward|date=1964|journal=Trench|publisher=Kyoto University Archaeology Research Association|volume=|number=11|pages=|url=|naid=|doi=|ref=}} * {{Cite book|language=ja|editor=[[Kyoto University]] Archaeology Research Association|author=|year=1971|chapter=Overview of Kofuns > Kabutozuka Kofun|title=The Kofun Era of Sagano - Excavation Report of the Mitogagaike Group of Kofuns|publisher=Kyoto University Archaeology Research Association Publication Office|isbn=|ref=}} * {{Cite book|language=ja|editor=Kyoto City|author=|year=1983|chapter=The Kofun Era > Ukyo Ward|title=Historical Materials of Kyoto's History|volume=Vol. 2, Archaeology|publisher=[[Heibonsha]]|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Historical Materials of Kyoto Vol. 2|1983}}}} == External Links == * {{Commonscat-inline|Kabutozuka Kofun (Kyoto)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'Burial Facility' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:01, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{infobox ancient site |name = Kabutozuka Kofun |image = [[File:Kabutozuka Kofun (Kyoto), entrance.jpg|280px]]
Entrance to the stone chamber |other_names = Kabutozuka/Ōtsuka{{Sfn|Kabutozuka (Heibonsha)|1979}} |affiliation = |location = Sagakabutozuka-cho, [[Ukyo Ward, Kyoto|Ukyo Ward]], [[Kyoto]], [[Kyoto Prefecture]] |latitude = 35|lat_minutes = 1|lat_seconds = 10.80 |longitude = 135|long_minutes = 41|long_seconds = 24.85 |ISO = JP-26 |type = [[Round Kofun|Round mound (empun)]] |scale = Diameter: over 38 m
Height: over 5.5 m |Burial Facility = Two-sleeved [[yokoana-style stone chamber|yokoana-style stone chamber]] |burial goods_found = |associated_burial_mounds = |construction_period = Early [[7th century]] |person_buried = (Presumed) member of the [[Hata clan (Japan)|Hata clan]] |imperial_mausoleum = |historical_site = None |tangible_cultural_properties = |notable_features = |map = Japan Kyoto city |icon = Empun |label = Kabutozuka Kofun |label_position = bottom }} '''Kabutozuka Kofun''' (甲塚古墳) is an ancient Kofun (kofun) located in Sagakabuto-cho, [[Ukyo Ward]], [[Kyoto City]], [[Kyoto Prefecture]]. The mound has a circular shape and is not designated as a historical site. == Overview == The Kabutozuka Kofun is a standalone, large circular Kofun built on the Sagano Plateau in the western part of the [[Kyoto Basin]]. The mound's name originates from its helmet-like appearance. Currently, a small shrine is enshrined within the inner chamber of the stone burial facility{{Sfn|Kabutozuka (Heibonsha)|1979}}. While the mound has suffered from looting, no formal excavations have been conducted. The mound is circular in shape, with an estimated diameter of 38 meters or more and a height exceeding 5.5 meters{{Sfn|Hirose Kazuo|2013}}. The burial facility is a double-sleeved [[corridor-style stone chamber]] (*yokoana-shiki sekishitsu*), which opens to the south. It is a large chamber measuring 14.4 meters in total length, constructed with massive stones. The funerary artifacts are not known{{Sfn|Kabutozuka (Heibonsha)|1979}}. The construction is estimated to date to the early 7th century, during the late Kofun period. It is considered one of the last Kofuns for regional chieftains in the Uzumasa-Sagano area, following the [[Jazuka Kofun]] and [[Maruyama Kofun]]. The mound is associated with the [[Hata clan]], a powerful immigrant lineage that developed the Sagano area{{Sfn|Hirose Kazuo|2013}}. Today, the kofun site is preserved within private property. == Burial Facility == [[File:Kabutozuka Kofun (Kyoto), 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Bird’s eye view of the stone chamber}}]] The burial facility is a double-sleeved [[corridor-style stone chamber]] opening to the south. The dimensions of the chamber are as follows{{Sfn|Hirose Kazuo|2013}}: * Total length: 14.4 meters * Main chamber (*gen-shitsu*): length 5.2 meters, width 2.9 meters, current height 3.4 meters * Corridor (*sen-dō*): length 9.2 meters, width 1.7 meters, current height 1.8 meters The rear wall of the main chamber is assumed to consist of two stacked layers of stones (its details are obscured by the presence of a shrine). The side walls of the main chamber consist of two stones stacked in two layers, while the front wall is a single layer. The chamber entrance consists of a single stone, and the corridor walls are two layers. The ceiling stones comprise three stones in the main chamber and four in the corridor{{Sfn|Hirose Kazuo|2013}}. File:Kabutozuka Kofun (Kyoto), 3D-P.jpg| Kabutozuka Kofun (Kyoto), 3D-2.jpg|Expanded view of the stone chamber Kabutozuka Kofun (Kyoto), genshitsu-1.jpg|Main chamber (towards the rear wall) Kabutozuka Kofun (Kyoto), genshitsu-2.jpg|Main chamber (towards the entrance) Kabutozuka Kofun (Kyoto), sendou-2.jpg|Corridor (towards the entrance) Kabutozuka Kofun (Kyoto), sendou-1.jpg|Corridor (towards the main chamber) Kabutozuka Kofun (Kyoto), funkyu.jpg|The Kofun == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|(References used in article compilation)}} * {{Cite book|language=ja|editor=|author=|year=1979|chapter=甲塚|title=京都市の地名|series=[[Japanese Historical Place Names Series]] 27|publisher=[[Heibonsha]]|isbn=978-4-582-49027-5|ref={{Harvid|Kabutozuka (Heibonsha)|1979}}}} * {{Cite book|author=[[Kazuo Hirose]]|year=2013|chapter=Questions Regarding Yamashiro's Jazuka Kofun|title=Research Reports of the National Museum of Japanese History|volume=Vol. 178|publisher=[[National Museum of Japanese History]]|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Hirose Kazuo|2013}}}} == Related Reading == {{small|(Related literature not used in this article)}} * {{Cite journal|language=ja|author=|title=Detailed Measurement Diagram of Kabutozuka Kofun, Ukyo Ward|date=1964|journal=Trench|publisher=Kyoto University Archaeology Research Association|volume=|number=11|pages=|url=|naid=|doi=|ref=}} * {{Cite book|language=ja|editor=[[Kyoto University]] Archaeology Research Association|author=|year=1971|chapter=Overview of Kofuns > Kabutozuka Kofun|title=The Kofun Era of Sagano - Excavation Report of the Mitogagaike Group of Kofuns|publisher=Kyoto University Archaeology Research Association Publication Office|isbn=|ref=}} * {{Cite book|language=ja|editor=Kyoto City|author=|year=1983|chapter=The Kofun Era > Ukyo Ward|title=Historical Materials of Kyoto's History|volume=Vol. 2, Archaeology|publisher=[[Heibonsha]]|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Historical Materials of Kyoto Vol. 2|1983}}}} == External Links == * {{Commonscat-inline|Kabutozuka Kofun (Kyoto)}} Entrance to the stone chamber |other_names = Kabutozuka/Ōtsuka{{Sfn|Kabutozuka (Heibonsha)|1979}} |affiliation = |location = Sagakabutozuka-cho, [[Ukyo Ward, Kyoto|Ukyo Ward]], [[Kyoto]], [[Kyoto Prefecture]] |latitude = 35|lat_minutes = 1|lat_seconds = 10.80 |longitude = 135|long_minutes = 41|long_seconds = 24.85 |ISO = JP-26 |type = [[Round Kofun|Round mound (empun)]] |map_size = Diameter: over 38 m
Height: over 5.5 m |Burial Facility = Two-sleeved [[yokoana-style stone chamber|yokoana-style stone chamber]] |burial goods_found = |associated_burial_mounds = |built = Early [[7th century]] |person_buried = (Presumed) member of the [[Hata clan (Japan)|Hata clan]] |imperial_mausoleum = |historical_site = None |tangible_cultural_properties = |notable_features = |map = Japan Kyoto city |icon = Empun |label = Kabutozuka Kofun |label_position = bottom }} '''Kabutozuka Kofun''' (甲塚古墳) is an ancient Kofun (kofun) located in Sagakabuto-cho, [[Ukyo Ward]], [[Kyoto City]], [[Kyoto Prefecture]]. The mound has a circular shape and is not designated as a historical site. == Overview == The Kabutozuka Kofun is a standalone, large circular Kofun built on the Sagano Plateau in the western part of the [[Kyoto Basin]]. The mound's name originates from its helmet-like appearance. Currently, a small shrine is enshrined within the inner chamber of the stone burial facility{{Sfn|Kabutozuka (Heibonsha)|1979}}. While the mound has suffered from looting, no formal excavations have been conducted. The mound is circular in shape, with an estimated diameter of 38 meters or more and a height exceeding 5.5 meters{{Sfn|Hirose Kazuo|2013}}. The burial facility is a double-sleeved [[corridor-style stone chamber]] (*yokoana-shiki sekishitsu*), which opens to the south. It is a large chamber measuring 14.4 meters in total length, constructed with massive stones. The funerary artifacts are not known{{Sfn|Kabutozuka (Heibonsha)|1979}}. The construction is estimated to date to the early 7th century, during the late Kofun period. It is considered one of the last Kofuns for regional chieftains in the Uzumasa-Sagano area, following the [[Jazuka Kofun]] and [[Maruyama Kofun]]. The mound is associated with the [[Hata clan]], a powerful immigrant lineage that developed the Sagano area{{Sfn|Hirose Kazuo|2013}}. Today, the kofun site is preserved within private property. == Burial Facility == [[File:Kabutozuka Kofun (Kyoto), 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Bird’s eye view of the stone chamber}}]] The burial facility is a double-sleeved [[corridor-style stone chamber]] opening to the south. The dimensions of the chamber are as follows{{Sfn|Hirose Kazuo|2013}}: * Total length: 14.4 meters * Main chamber (*gen-shitsu*): length 5.2 meters, width 2.9 meters, current height 3.4 meters * Corridor (*sen-dō*): length 9.2 meters, width 1.7 meters, current height 1.8 meters The rear wall of the main chamber is assumed to consist of two stacked layers of stones (its details are obscured by the presence of a shrine). The side walls of the main chamber consist of two stones stacked in two layers, while the front wall is a single layer. The chamber entrance consists of a single stone, and the corridor walls are two layers. The ceiling stones comprise three stones in the main chamber and four in the corridor{{Sfn|Hirose Kazuo|2013}}. File:Kabutozuka Kofun (Kyoto), 3D-P.jpg| Kabutozuka Kofun (Kyoto), 3D-2.jpg|Expanded view of the stone chamber Kabutozuka Kofun (Kyoto), genshitsu-1.jpg|Main chamber (towards the rear wall) Kabutozuka Kofun (Kyoto), genshitsu-2.jpg|Main chamber (towards the entrance) Kabutozuka Kofun (Kyoto), sendou-2.jpg|Corridor (towards the entrance) Kabutozuka Kofun (Kyoto), sendou-1.jpg|Corridor (towards the {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "Burial Facility"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:01, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{infobox ancient site |name = Kabutozuka Kofun |image = [[File:Kabutozuka Kofun (Kyoto), entrance.jpg|280px]]
Entrance to the stone chamber |other_names = Kabutozuka/Ōtsuka{{Sfn|Kabutozuka (Heibonsha)|1979}} |affiliation = |location = Sagakabutozuka-cho, [[Ukyo Ward, Kyoto|Ukyo Ward]], [[Kyoto]], [[Kyoto Prefecture]] |latitude = 35|lat_minutes = 1|lat_seconds = 10.80 |longitude = 135|long_minutes = 41|long_seconds = 24.85 |ISO = JP-26 |type = [[Round Kofun|Round mound (empun)]] |map_size = Diameter: over 38 m
Height: over 5.5 m |burial = Two-sleeved [[yokoana-style stone chamber|yokoana-style stone chamber]] |burial goods_found = |associated_burial_mounds = |built = Early [[7th century]] |person_buried = (Presumed) member of the [[Hata clan (Japan)|Hata clan]] |imperial_mausoleum = |historical_site = None |tangible_cultural_properties = |notable_features = |map = Japan Kyoto city |icon = Empun |label = Kabutozuka Kofun |label_position = bottom }} '''Kabutozuka Kofun''' (甲塚古墳) is an ancient Kofun (kofun) located in Sagakabuto-cho, [[Ukyo Ward]], [[Kyoto City]], [[Kyoto Prefecture]]. The mound has a circular type and is not designated as a historical site. == Overview == The Kabutozuka Kofun is a standalone, large circular Kofun built on the Sagano Plateau in the western part of the [[Kyoto Basin]]. The mound's name originates from its helmet-like appearance. Currently, a small shrine is enshrined within the inner chamber of the stone burial facility{{Sfn|Kabutozuka (Heibonsha)|1979}}. While the mound has suffered from looting, no formal excavations have been conducted. The mound is circular in type, with an estimated diameter of 38 meters or more and a height exceeding 5.5 meters{{Sfn|Hirose Kazuo|2013}}. The burial facility is a double-sleeved [[corridor-style stone chamber]] (*yokoana-shiki sekishitsu*), which opens to the south. It is a large chamber measuring 14.4 meters in total length, constructed with massive stones. The funerary artifacts are not known{{Sfn|Kabutozuka (Heibonsha)|1979}}. The construction is estimated to date to the early 7th century, during the late Kofun period. It is considered one of the last Kofuns for regional chieftains in the Uzumasa-Sagano area, following the [[Jazuka Kofun]] and [[Maruyama Kofun]]. The mound is associated with the [[Hata clan]], a powerful immigrant lineage that developed the Sagano area{{Sfn|Hirose Kazuo|2013}}. Today, the kofun site is preserved within private property. == burial == [[File:Kabutozuka Kofun (Kyoto), 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Bird’s eye view of the stone chamber}}]] The burial facility is a double-sleeved [[corridor-style stone chamber]] opening to the south. The dimensions of the chamber are as follows{{Sfn|Hirose Kazuo|2013}}: * Total length: 14.4 meters * Main chamber (*gen-shitsu*): length 5.2 meters, width 2.9 meters, current height 3.4 meters * Corridor (*sen-dō*): length 9.2 meters, width 1.7 meters, current height 1.8 meters The rear wall of the main chamber is assumed to consist of two stacked layers of stones (its details are obscured by the presence of a shrine). The side walls of the main chamber consist of two stones stacked in two layers, while the front wall is a single layer. The chamber entrance consists of a single stone, and the corridor walls are two layers. The ceiling stones comprise three stones in the main chamber and four in the corridor{{Sfn|Hirose Kazuo|2013}}. File:Kabutozuka Kofun (Kyoto), 3D-P.jpg| Kabutozuka Kofun (Kyoto), 3D-2.jpg|Expanded view of the stone chamber Kabutozuka Kofun (Kyoto), genshitsu-1.jpg|Main chamber (towards the rear wall) Kabutozuka Kofun (Kyoto), genshitsu-2.jpg|Main chamber (towards the entrance) Kabutozuka Kofun (Kyoto), sendou-2.jpg|Corridor (towards the entrance) Kabutozuka Kofun (Kyoto), sendou-1.jpg|Corridor (towards the main chamber) Kabutozuka Kofun (Kyoto), funkyu.jpg|The Kofun == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|(References used in article compilation)}} * {{Cite book|language=ja|editor=|author=|year=1979|chapter=甲塚|title=京都市の地名|series=[[Japanese Historical Place Names Series]] 27|publisher=[[Heibonsha]]|isbn=978-4-582-49027-5|ref={{Harvid|Kabutozuka (Heibonsha)|1979}}}} * {{Cite book|author=[[Kazuo Hirose]]|year=2013|chapter=Questions Regarding Yamashiro's Jazuka Kofun|title=Research Reports of the National Museum of Japanese History|volume=Vol. 178|publisher=[[National Museum of Japanese History]]|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Hirose Kazuo|2013}}}} == Related Reading == {{small|(Related literature not used in this article)}} * {{Cite journal|language=ja|author=|title=Detailed Measurement Diagram of Kabutozuka Kofun, Ukyo Ward|date=1964|journal=Trench|publisher=Kyoto University Archaeology Research Association|volume=|number=11|pages=|url=|naid=|doi=|ref=}} * {{Cite book|language=ja|editor=[[Kyoto University]] Archaeology Research Association|author=|year=1971|chapter=Overview of Kofuns > Kabutozuka Kofun|title=The Kofun Era of Sagano - Excavation Report of the Mitogagaike Group of Kofuns|publisher=Kyoto University Archaeology Research Association Publication Office|isbn=|ref=}} * {{Cite book|language=ja|editor=Kyoto City|author=|year=1983|chapter=The Kofun Era > Ukyo Ward|title=Historical Materials of Kyoto's History|volume=Vol. 2, Archaeology|publisher=[[Heibonsha]]|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Historical Materials of Kyoto Vol. 2|1983}}}} == External Links == * {{Commonscat-inline|Kabutozuka Kofun (Kyoto)}} {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "Burial Facility"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:01, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{infobox ancient site |name = Kabutozuka Kofun |image = [[File:Kabutozuka Kofun (Kyoto), entrance.jpg|280px]]
Entrance to the stone chamber |other_names = Kabutozuka/Ōtsuka{{Sfn|Kabutozuka (Heibonsha)|1979}} |affiliation = |location = Sagakabutozuka-cho, [[Ukyo Ward, Kyoto|Ukyo Ward]], [[Kyoto]], [[Kyoto Prefecture]] |latitude = 35|lat_minutes = 1|lat_seconds = 10.80 |longitude = 135|long_minutes = 41|long_seconds = 24.85 |ISO = JP-26 |type = [[Round Kofun|Round mound (empun)]] |map_size = Diameter: over 38 m
Height: over 5.5 m |burial = Two-sleeved [[yokoana-style stone chamber|yokoana-style stone chamber]] |burial goods_found = |associated_burial_mounds = |built = Early [[7th century]] |person_buried = (Presumed) member of the [[Hata clan (Japan)|Hata clan]] |imperial_mausoleum = |historical_site = None |tangible_cultural_properties = |notable_features = |map = Japan Kyoto city |icon = Empun |label = Kabutozuka Kofun |label_position = bottom }} '''Kabutozuka Kofun''' (甲塚古墳) is an ancient Kofun (kofun) located in Sagakabuto-cho, [[Ukyo Ward]], [[Kyoto City]], [[Kyoto Prefecture]]. The mound has a circular type and is not designated as a historical site. == Overview == The Kabutozuka Kofun is a standalone, large circular Kofun built on the Sagano Plateau in the western part of the [[Kyoto Basin]]. The mound's name originates from its helmet-like appearance. Currently, a small shrine is enshrined within the inner chamber of the stone burial facility{{Sfn|Kabutozuka (Heibonsha)|1979}}. While the mound has suffered from looting, no formal excavations have been conducted. The mound is circular in type, with an estimated diameter of 38 meters or more and a height exceeding 5.5 meters{{Sfn|Hirose Kazuo|2013}}. The burial facility is a double-sleeved [[corridor-style stone chamber]] (*yokoana-shiki sekishitsu*), which opens to the south. It is a large chamber measuring 14.4 meters in total length, constructed with massive stones. The funerary artifacts are not known{{Sfn|Kabutozuka (Heibonsha)|1979}}. The construction is estimated to date to the early 7th century, during the late Kofun period. It is considered one of the last Kofuns for regional chieftains in the Uzumasa-Sagano area, following the [[Jazuka Kofun]] and [[Maruyama Kofun]]. The mound is associated with the [[Hata clan]], a powerful immigrant lineage that developed the Sagano area{{Sfn|Hirose Kazuo|2013}}. Today, the kofun site is preserved within private property. == burial == [[File:Kabutozuka Kofun (Kyoto), 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Bird’s eye view of the stone chamber}}]] The burial facility is a double-sleeved [[corridor-style stone chamber]] opening to the south. The dimensions of the chamber are as follows{{Sfn|Hirose Kazuo|2013}}: * Total length: 14.4 meters * Main chamber (*gen-shitsu*): length 5.2 meters, width 2.9 meters, current height 3.4 meters * Corridor (*sen-dō*): length 9.2 meters, width 1.7 meters, current height 1.8 meters The rear wall of the main chamber is assumed to consist of two stacked layers of stones (its details are obscured by the presence of a shrine). The side walls of the main chamber consist of two stones stacked in two layers, while the front wall is a single layer. The chamber entrance consists of a single stone, and the corridor walls are two layers. The ceiling stones comprise three stones in the main chamber and four in the corridor{{Sfn|Hirose Kazuo|2013}}. File:Kabutozuka Kofun (Kyoto), 3D-P.jpg| Kabutozuka Kofun (Kyoto), 3D-2.jpg|Expanded view of the stone chamber Kabutozuka Kofun (Kyoto), genshitsu-1.jpg|Main chamber (towards the rear wall) Kabutozuka Kofun (Kyoto), genshitsu-2.jpg|Main chamber (towards the entrance) Kabutozuka Kofun (Kyoto), sendou-2.jpg|Corridor (towards the entrance) Kabutozuka Kofun (Kyoto), sendou-1.jpg|Corridor (towards the main chamber) Kabutozuka Kofun (Kyoto), funkyu.jpg|The Kofun == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|(References used in article compilation)}} * {{Cite It is a large chamber measuring 14.4 meters in total length, constructed with massive stones. The funerary artifacts are not known{{Sfn|Kabutozuka (Heibonsha)|1979}}. The construction is estimated to date to the early 7th century, during the late Kofun period. It is considered one of the last Kofuns for regional chieftains in the Uzumasa-Sagano area, following the [[Jazuka Kofun]] and [[Maruyama Kofun]]. The mound is associated with the [[Hata clan]], a powerful immigrant lineage that developed the Sagano area{{Sfn|Hirose Kazuo|2013}}. Today, the kofun site is preserved within private property. == burial == [[File:Kabutozuka Kofun (Kyoto), 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Bird’s eye view of the stone chamber}}]] The burial facility is a double-sleeved [[corridor-style stone chamber]] opening to the south. The dimensions of the chamber are as follows{{Sfn|Hirose Kazuo|2013}}: * Total length: 14.4 meters * Main chamber (*gen-shitsu*): length 5.2 meters, width 2.9 meters, current height 3.4 meters * Corridor (*sen-dō*): length 9.2 meters, width 1.7 meters, current height 1.8 meters The rear wall of the main chamber is assumed to consist of two stacked layers of stones (its details are obscured by the presence of a shrine). The side walls of the main chamber consist of two stones stacked in two layers, while the front wall is a single layer. The chamber entrance consists of a single stone, and the corridor walls are two layers. The ceiling stones comprise three stones in the main chamber and four in the corridor{{Sfn|Hirose Kazuo|2013}}. {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "Burial Facility"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:01, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{infobox ancient site |name = Kabutozuka Kofun |image = [[File:Kabutozuka Kofun (Kyoto), entrance.jpg|280px]]
Entrance to the stone chamber |other_names = Kabutozuka/Ōtsuka{{Sfn|Kabutozuka (Heibonsha)|1979}} |affiliation = |location = Sagakabutozuka-cho, [[Ukyo Ward, Kyoto|Ukyo Ward]], [[Kyoto]], [[Kyoto Prefecture]] |latitude = 35|lat_minutes = 1|lat_seconds = 10.80 |longitude = 135|long_minutes = 41|long_seconds = 24.85 |omitted = JP-26 |type = [[Round Kofun|Round mound (empun)]] |map_size = Diameter: over 38 m
Height: over 5.5 m |burial = Two-sleeved [[yokoana-style stone chamber|yokoana-style stone chamber]] |burial goods_found = |associated_burial_mounds = |built = Early [[7th century]] |person_buried = (Presumed) member of the [[Hata clan (Japan)|Hata clan]] |imperial_mausoleum = |historical_site = None |tangible_cultural_properties = |notable_features = |map = Japan Kyoto city |icon = Empun |label = Kabutozuka Kofun |label_position = bottom }} '''Kabutozuka Kofun''' (甲塚古墳) is an ancient Kofun (kofun) located in Sagakabuto-cho, [[Ukyo Ward]], [[Kyoto City]], [[Kyoto Prefecture]]. The mound has a circular type and is not designated as a historical site. == Overview == The Kabutozuka Kofun is a standalone, large circular Kofun built on the Sagano Plateau in the western part of the [[Kyoto Basin]]. The mound's name originates from its helmet-like appearance. Currently, a small shrine is enshrined within the inner chamber of the stone burial facility{{Sfn|Kabutozuka (Heibonsha)|1979}}. While the mound has suffered from looting, no formal excavations have been conducted. The mound is circular in type, with an estimated diameter of 38 meters or more and a height exceeding 5.5 meters{{Sfn|Hirose Kazuo|2013}}. The burial facility is a double-sleeved [[corridor-style stone chamber]] (*yokoana-shiki sekishitsu*), which opens to the south. It is a large chamber measuring 14.4 meters in total length, constructed with massive stones. The funerary artifacts are not known{{Sfn|Kabutozuka (Heibonsha)|1979}}. The construction is estimated to date to the early 7th century, during the late Kofun period. It is considered one of the last Kofuns for regional chieftains in the Uzumasa-Sagano area, following the [[Jazuka Kofun]] and [[Maruyama Kofun]]. The mound is associated with the [[Hata clan]], a powerful immigrant lineage that developed the Sagano area{{Sfn|Hirose Kazuo|2013}}. Today, the kofun site is preserved within private property. == burial == [[File:Kabutozuka Kofun (Kyoto), 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Bird’s eye view of the stone chamber}}]] The burial facility is a double-sleeved [[corridor-style stone chamber]] opening to the south. The dimensions of the chamber are as follows{{Sfn|Hirose Kazuo|2013}}: * Total length: 14.4 meters * Main chamber (*gen-shitsu*): length 5.2 meters, width 2.9 meters, current height 3.4 meters * Corridor (*sen-dō*): length 9.2 meters, width 1.7 meters, current height 1.8 meters The rear wall of the main chamber is assumed to consist of two stacked layers of stones (its details are obscured by the presence of a shrine). The side walls of the main chamber consist of two stones stacked in two layers, while the front wall is a single layer. The chamber entrance consists of a single stone, and the corridor walls are two layers. The ceiling stones comprise three stones in the main chamber and four in the corridor{{Sfn|Hirose Kazuo|2013}}. Entrance to the stone chamber |other_names = Kabutozuka/Ōtsuka{{Sfn|Kabutozuka (Heibonsha)|1979}} |affiliation = |location = Sagakabutozuka-cho, [[Ukyo Ward, Kyoto|Ukyo Ward]], [[Kyoto]], [[Kyoto Prefecture]] |latitude = 35|lat_minutes = 1|lat_seconds = 10.80 |longitude = 135|long_minutes = 41|long_seconds = 24.85 |omitted = JP-26 |type = [[Round Kofun|Round mound (empun)]] |map_size = Diameter: over 38 m
Height: over 5.5 m |burial = Two-sleeved [[yokoana-style stone chamber|yokoana-style stone chamber]] |burial goods_found = |associated_burial_mounds = |built = Early [[7th century]] |person_buried = (Presumed) member of the [[Hata clan (Japan)|Hata clan]] |imperial_mausoleum = |historical_site = None |tangible_cultural_properties = |notable_features = |map = Japan Kyoto city |icon = Empun |label = Kabutozuka Kofun |label_position = bottom }} '''Kabutozuka Kofun''' (甲塚古墳) is an ancient Kofun (kofun) located in Sagakabuto-cho, [[Ukyo Ward]], [[Kyoto City]], [[Kyoto Prefecture]]. The mound has a circular type and is not designated as a historical site. == Overview == The Kabutozuka Kofun is a standalone, large circular Kofun built on the Sagano Plateau in the western part of the [[Kyoto Basin]]. The mound's name originates from its helmet-like appearance. Currently, a small shrine is enshrined within the inner chamber of the stone burial facility{{Sfn|Kabutozuka (Heibonsha)|1979}}. While the mound has suffered from looting, no formal excavations have been conducted. The mound is circular in type, with an estimated diameter of 38 meters or more and a height exceeding 5.5 meters{{Sfn|Hirose Kazuo|2013}}. The burial facility is a double-sleeved [[corridor-style stone chamber]] (*yokoana-shiki sekishitsu*), which opens to the south. It is a large chamber measuring 14.4 meters in total length, constructed with massive stones. The funerary artifacts are not known{{Sfn|Kabutozuka (Heibonsha)|1979}}. The construction is estimated to date to the early 7th century, during the late Kofun period. It is considered one of the last Kofuns for regional chieftains in the Uzumasa-Sagano area, following the [[Jazuka Kofun]] and [[Maruyama Kofun]]. The mound is associated with the [[Hata clan]], a powerful immigrant lineage that developed the Sagano area{{Sfn|Hirose Kazuo|2013}}. Today, the kofun site is preserved within private property. == burial == [[File:Kabutozuka Kofun (Kyoto), 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Bird’s eye view of the stone chamber}}]] The burial facility is a double-sleeved [[corridor-style stone chamber]] opening to the south. The dimensions of the chamber are as follows{{Sfn|Hirose Kazuo|2013}}: * Total length: 14.4 meters * Main chamber (*gen-shitsu*): length 5.2 meters, width 2.9 meters, current height 3.4 meters * Corridor (*sen-dō*): length 9.2 meters, width 1.7 meters, current height 1.8 meters The rear wall of the main chamber is assumed to consist of two stacked layers of stones (its details are obscured by the presence of a shrine). The side walls of the main chamber consist of two stones stacked in two layers, while the front wall is a single layer. The chamber entrance consists of a single stone, and the corridor walls are two layers. The ceiling stones comprise three stones in the main chamber and four in the corridor{{Sfn|Hirose Kazuo|2013}}. revision.comment: Copying from [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] to [[Category:qq]][[sn:{{subst:PAGENAME}}]][[Category:qq]] using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "Burial Facility"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:01, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{infobox ancient site |name = Kabutozuka Kofun |image = [[File:Kabutozuka Kofun (Kyoto), entrance.jpg|280px]]
Entrance to the stone chamber |other_names = Kabutozuka/Ōtsuka{{Sfn|Kabutozuka (Heibonsha)|1979}} |affiliation = |location = Sagakabutozuka-cho, [[Ukyo Ward, Kyoto|Ukyo Ward]], [[Kyoto]], [[Kyoto Prefecture]] |coordinates = 35|lat_minutes = 1|lat_seconds = 10.80 |coordinates = 135|long_minutes = 41|long_seconds = 24.85 |omitted = JP-26 |type = [[Round Kofun|Round mound (empun)]] |map_size = Diameter: over 38 m
Height: over 5.5 m |burial = Two-sleeved [[yokoana-style stone chamber|yokoana-style stone chamber]] |burial goods_found = |associated_burial_mounds = |built = Early [[7th century]] |person_buried = (Presumed) member of the [[Hata clan (Japan)|Hata clan]] |imperial_mausoleum = |historical_site = None |tangible_cultural_properties = |notable_features = |map = Japan Kyoto city |type = Empun |label = Kabutozuka Kofun |label_position = bottom }} '''Kabutozuka Kofun''' (甲塚古墳) is an ancient Kofun (kofun) located in Sagakabuto-cho, [[Ukyo Ward]], [[Kyoto City]], [[Kyoto Prefecture]]. The mound has a circular type and is not designation1 as a historical site. == Overview == The Kabutozuka Kofun is a standalone, large circular Kofun built on the Sagano Plateau in the western part of the [[Kyoto Basin]]. The mound's name originates from its helmet-like appearance. Currently, a small shrine is enshrined within the inner chamber of the stone burial facility{{Sfn|Kabutozuka (Heibonsha)|1979}}. While the mound has suffered from looting, no formal excavations have been conducted. The mound is circular in type, with an estimated diameter of 38 meters or more and a height exceeding 5.5 meters{{Sfn|Hirose Kazuo|2013}}. The burial facility is a double-sleeved [[corridor-style stone chamber]] (*yokoana-shiki sekishitsu*), which opens to the south. It is a large chamber measuring 14.4 meters in total length, constructed with massive stones. The funerary burial goods are not known{{Sfn|Kabutozuka (Heibonsha)|1979}}. The construction is estimated to date to the early 7th century, during the late Kofun period. It is considered one of the last Kofuns for regional chieftains in the Uzumasa-Sagano area, following the [[Jazuka Kofun]] and [[Maruyama Kofun]]. The mound is associated with the [[Hata clan]], a powerful immigrant lineage that developed the Sagano area{{Sfn|Hirose Kazuo|2013}}. Today, the kofun site is preserved within private property. == burial == [[File:Kabutozuka Kofun (Kyoto), 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Bird’s eye view of the stone chamber}}]] The burial facility is a double-sleeved [[corridor-style stone chamber]] opening to the south. The area of the chamber are as follows{{Sfn|Hirose Kazuo|2013}}: * Total length: 14.4 meters * Main chamber (*gen-shitsu*): length 5.2 meters, width 2.9 meters, current height 3.4 meters * Corridor (*sen-dō*): length 9.2 meters, width 1.7 meters, current height 1.8 meters The rear wall of the main chamber is assumed to consist of two stacked layers of stones (its details are obscured by the presence of a shrine). The side walls of the main chamber consist of two stones stacked in two layers, while the front wall is a single layer. The chamber entrance consists of a single stone, and the corridor walls are two layers. The ceiling stones comprise three stones in the main chamber and four in the corridor{{Sfn|Hirose Kazuo|2013}}. File:Kabutozuka Kofun (Kyoto), 3D-P.jpg| Kabutozuka Kofun (Kyoto), 3D-2.jpg|Expanded view of the stone chamber Kabutozuka Kofun (Kyoto), genshitsu-1.jpg|Main chamber (towards the rear wall) Kabutozuka Kofun (Kyoto), genshitsu-2.jpg|Main chamber (towards the entrance) Kabutozuka Kofun (Kyoto), sendou-2.jpg|Corridor (towards the entrance) Kabutozuka Kofun (Kyoto), sendou-1.jpg|Corridor (towards the main chamber) Kabutozuka Kofun (Kyoto), funkyu.jpg|The Kofun == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|(References used in article compilation)}} * {{Cite book|language=ja|editor=|author=|year=1979|chapter=甲塚|title=京都市の地名|series=[[Japanese Historical Place names Series]] 27|publisher=[[Heibonsha]]|isbn=978-4-582-49027-5|ref={{Harvid|Kabutozuka (Heibonsha)|1979}}}} * {{Cite book|author=[[Kazuo Hirose]]|year=2013|chapter=Questions Regarding Yamashiro's Jazuka Kofun|title=Research Reports of the National Museum of Japanese History|volume=Vol. 178|publisher=[[National Museum of Japanese History]]|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Hirose Kazuo|2013}}}} == Related Reading == {{small|(Related literature not used in this article)}} * {{Cite journal|language=ja|author=|title=Detailed Measurement Diagram of Kabutozuka Kofun, Ukyo Ward|date=1964|journal=Trench|publisher=Kyoto University Archaeology Research Association|volume=|number=11|pages=|url=|naid=|doi=|ref=}} * {{Cite book|language=ja|editor=[[Kyoto University]] Archaeology Research Association|author=|year=1971|chapter=Overview of Kofuns > Kabutozuka Kofun|title=The Kofun Era of Sagano - Excavation Report of the Mitogagaike Group of Kofuns|publisher=Kyoto University Archaeology Research Association Publication Office|isbn=|ref=}} * {{Cite book|language=ja|editor=Kyoto City|author=|year=1983|chapter=The Kofun Era > Ukyo Ward|title=Historical Materials of Kyoto's History|volume=Vol. 2, Archaeology|publisher=[[Heibonsha]]|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Historical Materials of Kyoto Vol. 2|1983}}}} == External Links == * {{Commonscat-inline|Kabutozuka Kofun (Kyoto)}}
