Entrance to the stone chamber |other_names = Kabutozuka/Ōtsuka{{Sfn|Kabutozuka (Heibonsha)|1979}} |affiliation = |location = Sagakabutozuka-cho, [[Ukyo Ward, Kyoto|Ukyo Ward]], [[Kyoto]], [[Kyoto Prefecture]] |coordinates = 35|lat_minutes = 1|lat_seconds = 10.80 |coordinates = 135|long_minutes = 41|long_seconds = 24.85 |omitted = JP-26 |type = [[Round Kofun|Round mound (empun)]] |map_size = Diameter: over 38 m
Height: over 5.5 m |burial = Two-sleeved [[yokoana-style stone chamber|yokoana-style stone chamber]] |burial goods_found = |associated_burial_mounds = |built = Early [[7th century]] |person_buried = (Presumed) member of the [[Hata clan (Japan)|Hata clan]] |imperial_mausoleum = |historical_site = None |tangible_cultural_properties = |notable_features = |map = Japan Kyoto city |type = Empun |map_dot_label = Kabutozuka Kofun |map_dot_map_dot_label = bottom }} '''Kabutozuka Kofun''' (甲塚古墳) is an ancient Kofun (kofun) located in Sagakabuto-cho, [[Ukyo Ward]], [[Kyoto City]], [[Kyoto Prefecture]]. The mound has a circular type and is not other_designation1 as a historical site. == Overview == The Kabutozuka Kofun is a standalone, large circular Kofun built on the Sagano Plateau in the western part of the [[Kyoto Basin]]. The mound's name originates from its helmet-like appearance. Currently, a small shrine is enshrined within the inner chamber of the stone burial facility{{Sfn|Kabutozuka (Heibonsha)|1979}}. While the mound has suffered from looting, no typeal excavations have been conducted. The mound is circular in type, with an estimated diameter of 38 meters or more and a height exceeding 5.5 meters{{Sfn|Hirose Kazuo|2013}}. The burial facility is a double-sleeved [[corridor-style stone chamber]] (*yokoana-shiki sekishitsu*), which opens to the south. It is a large chamber measuring 14.4 meters in total length, constructed with massive stones. The funerary burial goods are not known{{Sfn|Kabutozuka (Heibonsha)|1979}}. The construction is estimated to date to the early 7th century, during the late Kofun period. It is considered one of the last Kofuns for regional chieftains in the Uzumasa-Sagano area, following the [[Jazuka Kofun]] and [[Maruyama Kofun]]. The mound is associated with the [[Hata clan]], a powerful immigrant lineage that developed the Sagano area{{Sfn|Hirose Kazuo|2013}}. Today, the kofun site is preserved within private property. == burial == [[File:Kabutozuka Kofun (Kyoto), 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Bird’s eye view of the stone chamber}}]] The burial facility is a double-sleeved [[corridor-style stone chamber]] opening to the south. The area of the chamber are as follows{{Sfn|Hirose Kazuo|2013}}: * Total length: 14.4 meters * Main chamber (*gen-shitsu*): length 5.2 meters, width 2.9 meters, current height 3.4 meters * Corridor (*sen-dō*): length 9.2 meters, width 1.7 meters, current height 1.8 meters The rear wall of the main chamber is assumed to consist of two stacked layers of stones (its details are obscured by the presence of a shrine). The side walls of the main chamber consist of two stones stacked in two layers, while the front wall is a single layer. The chamber entrance consists of a single stone, and the corridor walls are two layers. The ceiling stones comprise three stones in the main chamber and four in the corridor{{Sfn|Hirose Kazuo|2013}}. File:Kabutozuka Kofun (Kyoto), 3D-P.jpg| Kabutozuka Kofun (Kyoto), 3D-2.jpg|Expanded view of the stone chamber Kabutozuka Kofun (Kyoto), genshitsu-1.jpg|Main chamber (towards the rear wall) Kabutozuka Kofun (Kyoto), genshitsu-2.jpg|Main chamber (towards the entrance) Kabutozuka Kofun (Kyoto), sendou-2.jpg|Corridor (towards the entrance) Kabutozuka Kofun (Kyoto), sendou-1.jpg|Corridor (towards the It is considered one of the last Kofuns for regional chieftains in the Uzumasa-Sagano area, following the [[Jazuka Kofun]] and [[Maruyama Kofun]]. The mound is associated with the [[Hata clan]], a powerful immigrant lineage that developed the Sagano area{{Sfn|Hirose Kazuo|2013}}. Today, the kofun site is preserved within private property. == burial == [[File:Kabutozuka Kofun (Kyoto), 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Bird’s eye view of the stone chamber}}]] The burial facility is a double-sleeved [[corridor-style stone chamber]] opening to the south. The area of the chamber are as follows{{Sfn|Hirose Kazuo|2013}}: * Total length: 14.4 meters * Main chamber (*gen-shitsu*): length 5.2 meters, width 2.9 meters, current height 3.4 meters * Corridor (*sen-dō*): length 9.2 meters, width 1.7 meters, current height 1.8 meters The rear wall of the main chamber is assumed to consist of two stacked layers of stones (its details are obscured by the presence of a shrine). The side walls of the main chamber consist of two stones stacked in two layers, while the front wall is a single layer. The chamber entrance consists of a single stone, and the corridor walls are two layers. The ceiling stones comprise three stones in the main chamber and four in the corridor{{Sfn|Hirose Kazuo|2013}}. revision.comment: Copying from [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] to [[Category:qq]][[sn:{{subst:PAGENAME}}]][[Category:qq]] using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "Burial Facility"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:01, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{infobox ancient site |name = Kabutozuka Kofun |image = [[File:Kabutozuka Kofun (Kyoto), entrance.jpg|280px]]
Entrance to the stone chamber |other_names = Kabutozuka/Ōtsuka{{Sfn|Kabutozuka (Heibonsha)|1979}} |affiliation = |location = Sagakabutozuka-cho, [[Ukyo Ward, Kyoto|Ukyo Ward]], [[Kyoto]], [[Kyoto Prefecture]] |coordinates = 35|lat_minutes = 1|lat_seconds = 10.80 |coordinates = 135|long_minutes = 41|long_seconds = 24.85 |omitted = JP-26 |type = [[Round Kofun|Round mound (empun)]] |map_size = Diameter: over 38 m
Height: over 5.5 m |burial = Two-sleeved [[yokoana-style stone chamber|yokoana-style stone chamber]] |burial goods_found = |associated_burial_mounds = |built = Early [[7th century]] |person_buried = (Presumed) member of the [[Hata clan (Japan)|Hata clan]] |imperial_mausoleum = |historical_site = None |tangible_cultural_properties = |notable_features = |map = Japan Kyoto city |type = Empun |map_dot_label = Kabutozuka Kofun |map_dot_label = bottom }} '''Kabutozuka Kofun''' (甲塚古墳) is an ancient Kofun (kofun) located in Sagakabuto-cho, [[Ukyo Ward]], [[Kyoto City]], [[Kyoto Prefecture]]. The mound has a circular type and is not other_designation1 as a historical site. == Overview == The Kabutozuka Kofun is a standalone, large circular Kofun built on the Sagano Plateau in the western part of the [[Kyoto Basin]]. The mound's name originates from its helmet-like appearance. Currently, a small shrine is enshrined within the inner chamber of the stone burial facility{{Sfn|Kabutozuka (Heibonsha)|1979}}. While the mound has suffered from looting, no typeal excavations have been conducted. The mound is circular in type, with an estimated diameter of 38 meters or more and a height exceeding 5.5 meters{{Sfn|Hirose Kazuo|2013}}. The burial facility is a double-sleeved [[corridor-style stone chamber]] (*yokoana-shiki sekishitsu*), which opens to the south. It is a large chamber measuring 14.4 meters in total length, constructed with massive stones. The funerary burial goods are not known{{Sfn|Kabutozuka (Heibonsha)|1979}}. The construction is estimated to date to the early 7th century, during the late Kofun period. It is considered one of the last Kofuns for regional chieftains in the Uzumasa-Sagano area, following the [[Jazuka Kofun]] and [[Maruyama Kofun]]. The mound is associated with the [[Hata clan]], a powerful immigrant lineage that developed the Sagano area{{Sfn|Hirose Kazuo|2013}}. Today, the kofun site is preserved within private property. == burial == [[File:Kabutozuka Kofun (Kyoto), 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Bird’s eye view of the stone chamber}}]] The burial facility is a double-sleeved [[corridor-style stone chamber]] opening to the south. The area of the chamber are as follows{{Sfn|Hirose Kazuo|2013}}: * Total length: 14.4 meters * Main chamber (*gen-shitsu*): length 5.2 meters, width 2.9 meters, current height 3.4 meters * Corridor (*sen-dō*): length 9.2 meters, width 1.7 meters, current height 1.8 meters The rear wall of the main chamber is assumed to consist of two stacked layers of stones (its details are obscured by the presence of a shrine). The side walls of the main chamber consist of two stones stacked in two layers, while the front wall is a single layer. The chamber entrance consists of a single stone, and the corridor walls are two layers. The ceiling stones comprise three stones in the main chamber and four in the corridor{{Sfn|Hirose Kazuo|2013}}. File:Kabutozuka Kofun (Kyoto), 3D-P.jpg| Kabutozuka Kofun (Kyoto), 3D-2.jpg|Expanded view of the stone chamber Kabutozuka Kofun (Kyoto), genshitsu-1.jpg|Main chamber (towards the rear wall) Kabutozuka Kofun (Kyoto), genshitsu-2.jpg|Main chamber (towards the entrance) Kabutozuka Kofun (Kyoto), sendou-2.jpg|Corridor (towards the entrance) Kabutozuka Kofun (Kyoto), sendou-1.jpg|Corridor (towards the main chamber) Kabutozuka Kofun (Kyoto), funkyu.jpg|The Kofun == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|(References used in article compilation)}} * {{Cite This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{infobox ancient site |name = Kabutozuka Kofun |image = [[File:Kabutozuka Kofun (Kyoto), entrance.jpg|280px]]
Entrance to the stone chamber |other_names = Kabutozuka/Ōtsuka{{Sfn|Kabutozuka (Heibonsha)|1979}} |affiliation = |location = Sagakabutozuka-cho, [[Ukyo Ward, Kyoto|Ukyo Ward]], [[Kyoto]], [[Kyoto Prefecture]] |coordinates = 35|lat_minutes = 1|lat_seconds = 10.80 |coordinates = 135|long_minutes = 41|long_seconds = 24.85 |omitted = JP-26 |type = [[Round Kofun|Round mound (empun)]] |map_size = Diameter: over 38 m
Height: over 5.5 m |burial = Two-sleeved [[yokoana-style stone chamber|yokoana-style stone chamber]] |burial goods_found = |associated_burial_mounds = |built = Early [[7th century]] |person_buried = (Presumed) member of the [[Hata clan (Japan)|Hata clan]] |imperial_mausoleum = |historical_site = None |tangible_cultural_properties = |notes = |map = Japan Kyoto city |type = Empun |map_dot_label = Kabutozuka Kofun |map_dot_label = bottom }} '''Kabutozuka Kofun''' (甲塚古墳) is an ancient Kofun (kofun) located in Sagakabuto-cho, [[Ukyo Ward]], [[Kyoto City]], [[Kyoto Prefecture]]. The mound has a circular type and is not other_designation1 as a historical site. == Overview == The Kabutozuka Kofun is a standalone, large circular Kofun built on the Sagano Plateau in the western part of the [[Kyoto Basin]]. The mound's name originates from its helmet-like appearance. Currently, a small shrine is enshrined within the inner chamber of the stone burial facility{{Sfn|Kabutozuka (Heibonsha)|1979}}. While the mound has suffered from looting, no typeal excavations have been conducted. The mound is circular in type, with an estimated diameter of 38 meters or more and a height exceeding 5.5 meters{{Sfn|Hirose Kazuo|2013}}. The burial facility is a double-sleeved [[corridor-style stone chamber]] (*yokoana-shiki sekishitsu*), which opens to the south. It is a large chamber measuring 14.4 meters in total length, constructed with massive stones. The funerary burial goods are not known{{Sfn|Kabutozuka (Heibonsha)|1979}}. The construction is estimated to date to the early 7th century, during the late Kofun period. It is considered one of the last Kofuns for regional chieftains in the Uzumasa-Sagano area, following the [[Jazuka Kofun]] and [[Maruyama Kofun]]. The mound is associated with the [[Hata clan]], a powerful immigrant lineage that developed the Sagano area{{Sfn|Hirose Kazuo|2013}}. Today, the kofun site is preserved within private property. == burial == [[File:Kabutozuka Kofun (Kyoto), 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Bird’s eye view of the stone chamber}}]] The burial facility is a double-sleeved [[corridor-style stone chamber]] opening to the south. The area of the chamber are as follows{{Sfn|Hirose Kazuo|2013}}: * Total length: 14.4 meters * Main chamber (*gen-shitsu*): length 5.2 meters, width 2.9 meters, current height 3.4 meters * Corridor (*sen-dō*): length 9.2 meters, width 1.7 meters, current height 1.8 meters The rear wall of the main chamber is assumed to consist of two stacked layers of stones (its details are obscured by the presence of a shrine). The side walls of the main chamber consist of two stones stacked in two layers, while the front wall is a single layer. The chamber entrance consists of a single stone, and the corridor walls are two layers. The ceiling stones comprise three stones in the main chamber and four in the corridor{{Sfn|Hirose Kazuo|2013}}. File:Kabutozuka Kofun (Kyoto), 3D-P.jpg| Kabutozuka Kofun (Kyoto), 3D-2.jpg|Expanded view of the stone chamber Kabutozuka Kofun (Kyoto), genshitsu-1.jpg|Main chamber (towards the rear wall) Kabutozuka Entrance to the stone chamber |other_names = Kabutozuka/Ōtsuka{{Sfn|Kabutozuka (Heibonsha)|1979}} |affiliation = |location = Sagakabutozuka-cho, [[Ukyo Ward, Kyoto|Ukyo Ward]], [[Kyoto]], [[Kyoto Prefecture]] |coordinates = 35|lat_minutes = 1|lat_seconds = 10.80 |coordinates = 135|long_minutes = 41|long_seconds = 24.85 |omitted = JP-26 |type = [[Round Kofun|Round mound (empun)]] |map_size = Diameter: over 38 m
Height: over 5.5 m |burial = Two-sleeved [[yokoana-style stone chamber|yokoana-style stone chamber]] |burial goods_found = |associated_burial_mounds = |built = Early [[7th century]] |person_buried = (Presumed) member of the [[Hata clan (Japan)|Hata clan]] |imperial_mausoleum = |historical_site = None |tangible_cultural_properties = |notable_features = |map = Japan Kyoto city |type = Empun |map_dot_label = Kabutozuka Kofun |map_dot_label = bottom }} '''Kabutozuka Kofun''' (甲塚古墳) is an ancient Kofun (kofun) located in Sagakabuto-cho, [[Ukyo Ward]], [[Kyoto City]], [[Kyoto Prefecture]]. The mound has a circular type and is not other_designation1 as a historical site. == Overview == The Kabutozuka Kofun is a standalone, large circular Kofun built on the Sagano Plateau in the western part of the [[Kyoto Basin]]. The mound's name originates from its helmet-like appearance. Currently, a small shrine is enshrined within the inner chamber of the stone burial facility{{Sfn|Kabutozuka (Heibonsha)|1979}}. While the mound has suffered from looting, no typeal excavations have been conducted. The mound is circular in type, with an estimated diameter of 38 meters or more and a height exceeding 5.5 meters{{Sfn|Hirose Kazuo|2013}}. The burial facility is a double-sleeved [[corridor-style stone chamber]] (*yokoana-shiki sekishitsu*), which opens to the south. It is a large chamber measuring 14.4 meters in total length, constructed with massive stones. The funerary burial goods are not known{{Sfn|Kabutozuka (Heibonsha)|1979}}. The construction is estimated to date to the early 7th century, during the late Kofun period. It is considered one of the last Kofuns for regional chieftains in the Uzumasa-Sagano area, following the [[Jazuka Kofun]] and [[Maruyama Kofun]]. The mound is associated with the [[Hata clan]], a powerful immigrant lineage that developed the Sagano area{{Sfn|Hirose Kazuo|2013}}. Today, the kofun site is preserved within private property. == burial == [[File:Kabutozuka Kofun (Kyoto), 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Bird’s eye view of the stone chamber}}]] The burial facility is a double-sleeved [[corridor-style stone chamber]] opening to the south. The area of the chamber are as follows{{Sfn|Hirose Kazuo|2013}}: * Total length: 14.4 meters * Main chamber (*gen-shitsu*): length 5.2 meters, width 2.9 meters, current height 3.4 meters * Corridor (*sen-dō*): length 9.2 meters, width 1.7 meters, current height 1.8 meters The rear wall of the main chamber is assumed to consist of two stacked layers of stones (its details are obscured by the presence of a shrine). The side walls of the main chamber consist of two stones stacked in two layers, while the front wall is a single layer. The chamber entrance consists of a single stone, and the corridor walls are two layers. The ceiling stones comprise three stones in the main chamber and four in the corridor{{Sfn|Hirose Kazuo|2013}}. File:Kabutozuka Kofun (Kyoto), 3D-P.jpg| Kabutozuka Kofun (Kyoto), 3D-2.jpg|Expanded view of the stone chamber Kabutozuka Kofun (Kyoto), genshitsu-1.jpg|Main chamber (towards the rear wall) Kabutozuka Kofun (Kyoto), genshitsu-2.jpg|Main chamber (towards the entrance) Kabutozuka Kofun (Kyoto), sendou-2.jpg|Corridor (towards the entrance) Kabutozuka Kofun (Kyoto), sendou-1.jpg|Corridor (towards the {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:01, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{infobox ancient site |name = Kabutozuka Kofun |image = [[File:Kabutozuka Kofun (Kyoto), entrance.jpg|280px]]
Entrance to the stone chamber |alternate_names = Kabutozuka/Ōtsuka{{Sfn|Kabutozuka (Heibonsha)|1979}} |affiliation = |location = Sagakabutozuka-cho, [[Ukyo Ward, Kyoto|Ukyo Ward]], [[Kyoto]], [[Kyoto Prefecture]] |coordinates = 35|coordinatesutes = 1|coordinatesonds = 10.80 |coordinates = 135|coordinatesutes = 41|coordinatesonds = 24.85 |omitted = JP-26 |type = [[Round Kofun|Round mound (empun)]] |map_size = Diameter: over 38 m
Height: over 5.5 m |burial = Two-sleeved [[yokoana-style stone chamber|yokoana-style stone chamber]] |burial goods_found = |associated_burial_mounds = |built = Early [[7th century]] |person_buried = (Presumed) member of the [[Hata clan (Japan)|Hata clan]] |imperial_mausoleum = |historical_site = None |tangible_cultural_properties = |notable_features = |map = Japan Kyoto city |type = Empun |map_dot_label = Kabutozuka Kofun |map_dot_label = bottom }} '''Kabutozuka Kofun''' (甲塚古墳) is an ancient Kofun (kofun) located in Sagakabuto-cho, [[Ukyo Ward]], [[Kyoto City]], [[Kyoto Prefecture]]. The mound has a circular type and is not other_designation1 as a historical site. == Overview == The Kabutozuka Kofun is a standalone, large circular Kofun built on the Sagano Plateau in the western part of the [[Kyoto Basin]]. The mound's name originates from its helmet-like appearance. Currently, a small shrine is enshrined within the inner chamber of the stone burial facility{{Sfn|Kabutozuka (Heibonsha)|1979}}. While the mound has suffered from looting, no typeal excavations have been conducted. The mound is circular in type, with an estimated diameter of 38 meters or more and a height exceeding 5.5 meters{{Sfn|Hirose Kazuo|2013}}. The burial facility is a double-sleeved [[corridor-style stone chamber]] (*yokoana-shiki sekishitsu*), which opens to the south. It is a large chamber measuring 14.4 meters in total length, constructed with massive stones. The funerary burial goods are not known{{Sfn|Kabutozuka (Heibonsha)|1979}}. The construction is estimated to date to the early 7th century, during the late Kofun period. It is considered one of the last Kofuns for regional chieftains in the Uzumasa-Sagano area, following the [[Jazuka Kofun]] and [[Maruyama Kofun]]. The mound is associated with the [[Hata clan]], a powerful immigrant lineage that developed the Sagano area{{Sfn|Hirose Kazuo|2013}}. Today, the kofun site is preserved within private property. == burial == [[File:Kabutozuka Kofun (Kyoto), 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Bird’s eye view of the stone chamber}}]] The burial facility is a double-sleeved [[corridor-style stone chamber]] opening to the south. The area of the chamber are as follows{{Sfn|Hirose Kazuo|2013}}: * Total length: 14.4 meters * Main chamber (*gen-shitsu*): length 5.2 meters, width 2.9 meters, current height 3.4 meters * Corridor (*sen-dō*): length 9.2 meters, width 1.7 meters, current height 1.8 meters The rear wall of the main chamber is assumed to consist of two stacked layers of stones (its details are obscured by the presence of a shrine). The side walls of the main chamber consist of two stones stacked in two layers, while the front wall is a single layer. The chamber entrance consists of a single stone, and the corridor walls are two layers. The ceiling stones comprise three stones in the main chamber and four in the corridor{{Sfn|Hirose Kazuo|2013}}. File:Kabutozuka Kofun (Kyoto), 3D-P.jpg| Kabutozuka Kofun (Kyoto), 3D-2.jpg|Expanded view of the stone chamber Kabutozuka Kofun (Kyoto), genshitsu-1.jpg|Main chamber (towards the rear wall) Kabutozuka Kofun (Kyoto), genshitsu-2.jpg|Main chamber (towards the entrance) Kabutozuka Kofun (Kyoto), sendou-2.jpg|Corridor (towards the entrance) Kabutozuka Kofun (Kyoto), sendou-1.jpg|Corridor (towards the Entrance to the stone chamber |alternate_names = Kabutozuka/Ōtsuka{{Sfn|Kabutozuka (Heibonsha)|1979}} |affiliation = |location = Sagakabutozuka-cho, [[Ukyo Ward, Kyoto|Ukyo Ward]], [[Kyoto]], [[Kyoto Prefecture]] |coordinates = 35|coordinatesutes = 1|coordinatesonds = 10.80 |coordinates = 135|coordinatesutes = 41|coordinatesonds = 24.85 |omitted = JP-26 |type = [[Round Kofun|Round mound (empun)]] |map_size = Diameter: over 38 m
Height: over 5.5 m |burial = Two-sleeved [[yokoana-style stone chamber|yokoana-style stone chamber]] |burial goods_found = |associated_burial_mounds = |built = Early [[7th century]] |person_buried = (Presumed) member of the [[Hata clan (Japan)|Hata clan]] |imperial_mausoleum = |historical_site = None |tangible_cultural_properties = |notable_features = |map = Japan Kyoto city |type = Empun |map_dot_label = Kabutozuka Kofun |map_dot_label = bottom }} '''Kabutozuka Kofun''' (甲塚古墳) is an ancient Kofun (kofun) located in Sagakabuto-cho, [[Ukyo Ward]], [[Kyoto City]], [[Kyoto Prefecture]]. The mound has a circular type and is not other_designation1 as a historical site. == Overview == The Kabutozuka Kofun is a standalone, large circular Kofun built on the Sagano Plateau in the western part of the [[Kyoto Basin]]. The mound's name originates from its helmet-like appearance. Currently, a small shrine is enshrined within the inner chamber of the stone burial facility{{Sfn|Kabutozuka (Heibonsha)|1979}}. While the mound has suffered from looting, no typeal excavations have been conducted. The mound is circular in type, with an estimated diameter of 38 meters or more and a height exceeding 5.5 meters{{Sfn|Hirose Kazuo|2013}}. The burial facility is a double-sleeved [[corridor-style stone chamber]] (*yokoana-shiki sekishitsu*), which opens to the south. It is a large chamber measuring 14.4 meters in total length, constructed with massive stones. The funerary burial goods are not known{{Sfn|Kabutozuka (Heibonsha)|1979}}. The construction is estimated to date to the early 7th century, during the late Kofun period. It is considered one of the last Kofuns for regional chieftains in the Uzumasa-Sagano area, following the [[Jazuka Kofun]] and [[Maruyama Kofun]]. The mound is associated with the [[Hata clan]], a powerful immigrant lineage that developed the Sagano area{{Sfn|Hirose Kazuo|2013}}. Today, the kofun site is preserved within private property. == burial == [[File:Kabutozuka Kofun (Kyoto), 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Bird’s eye view of the stone chamber}}]] The burial facility is a double-sleeved [[corridor-style stone chamber]] opening to the south. The area of the chamber are as follows{{Sfn|Hirose Kazuo|2013}}: * Total length: 14.4 meters * Main chamber (*gen-shitsu*): length 5.2 meters, width 2.9 meters, current height 3.4 meters * Corridor (*sen-dō*): length 9.2 meters, width 1.7 meters, current height 1.8 meters The rear wall of the main chamber is assumed to consist of two stacked layers of stones (its details are obscured by the presence of a shrine). The side walls of the main chamber consist of two stones stacked in two layers, while the front wall is a single layer. The chamber entrance consists of a single stone, and the corridor walls are two layers. The ceiling stones comprise three stones in the main chamber and four in the corridor{{Sfn|Hirose Kazuo|2013}}. File:Kabutozuka Kofun (Kyoto), 3D-P.jpg| Kabutozuka Kofun (Kyoto), 3D-2.jpg|Expanded view of the stone chamber Kabutozuka Kofun (Kyoto), genshitsu-1.jpg|Main chamber (towards the rear wall) Kabutozuka Kofun (Kyoto), genshitsu-2.jpg|Main chamber (towards the entrance) Kabutozuka Kofun (Kyoto), sendou-2.jpg|Corridor (towards the entrance) Kabutozuka Kofun (Kyoto), sendou-1.jpg|Corridor (towards the main chamber) Kabutozuka Kofun (Kyoto), funkyu.jpg|The Kofun == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|(References used in article compilation)}} * {{Cite {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:01, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{infobox ancient site |name = Kabutozuka Kofun |image = [[File:Kabutozuka Kofun (Kyoto), entrance.jpg|280px]]
Entrance to the stone chamber |alternate_name = Kabutozuka/Ōtsuka{{Sfn|Kabutozuka (Heibonsha)|1979}} |affiliation = |location = Sagakabutozuka-cho, [[Ukyo Ward, Kyoto|Ukyo Ward]], [[Kyoto]], [[Kyoto Prefecture]] |coordinates = 35|coordinatesutes = 1|coordinatesonds = 10.80 |coordinates = 135|coordinatesutes = 41|coordinatesonds = 24.85 |omitted = JP-26 |type = [[Round Kofun|Round mound (empun)]] |map_size = Diameter: over 38 m
Height: over 5.5 m |burial = Two-sleeved [[yokoana-style stone chamber|yokoana-style stone chamber]] |burial goods_found = |associated_burial_mounds = |built = Early [[7th century]] |person_buried = (Presumed) member of the [[Hata clan (Japan)|Hata clan]] |imperial_mausoleum = |historical_site = None |tangible_cultural_properties = |notable_features = |map = Japan Kyoto city |type = Empun |map_dot_label = Kabutozuka Kofun |map_dot_label = bottom }} '''Kabutozuka Kofun''' (甲塚古墳) is an ancient Kofun (kofun) located in Sagakabuto-cho, [[Ukyo Ward]], [[Kyoto City]], [[Kyoto Prefecture]]. The mound has a circular type and is not other_designation1 as a historical site. == Overview == The Kabutozuka Kofun is a standalone, large circular Kofun built on the Sagano Plateau in the western part of the [[Kyoto Basin]]. The mound's name originates from its helmet-like appearance. Currently, a small shrine is enshrined within the inner chamber of the stone burial facility{{Sfn|Kabutozuka (Heibonsha)|1979}}. While the mound has suffered from looting, no typeal excavations have been conducted. The mound is circular in type, with an estimated diameter of 38 meters or more and a height exceeding 5.5 meters{{Sfn|Hirose Kazuo|2013}}. The burial facility is a double-sleeved [[corridor-style stone chamber]] (*yokoana-shiki sekishitsu*), which opens to the south. It is a large chamber measuring 14.4 meters in total length, constructed with massive stones. The funerary burial goods are not known{{Sfn|Kabutozuka (Heibonsha)|1979}}. The construction is estimated to date to the early 7th century, during the late Kofun period. It is considered one of the last Kofuns for regional chieftains in the Uzumasa-Sagano area, following the [[Jazuka Kofun]] and [[Maruyama Kofun]]. The mound is associated with the [[Hata clan]], a powerful immigrant lineage that developed the Sagano area{{Sfn|Hirose Kazuo|2013}}. Today, the kofun site is preserved within private property. == burial == [[File:Kabutozuka Kofun (Kyoto), 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Bird’s eye view of the stone chamber}}]] The burial facility is a double-sleeved [[corridor-style stone chamber]] opening to the south. The area of the chamber are as follows{{Sfn|Hirose Kazuo|2013}}: * Total length: 14.4 meters * Main chamber (*gen-shitsu*): length 5.2 meters, width 2.9 meters, current height 3.4 meters * Corridor (*sen-dō*): length 9.2 meters, width 1.7 meters, current height 1.8 meters The rear wall of the main chamber is assumed to consist of two stacked layers of stones (its details are obscured by the presence of a shrine). The side walls of the main chamber consist of two stones stacked in two layers, while the front wall is a single layer. The chamber entrance consists of a single stone, and the corridor walls are two layers. The ceiling stones comprise three stones in the main chamber and four in the corridor{{Sfn|Hirose Kazuo|2013}}. File:Kabutozuka Kofun (Kyoto), 3D-P.jpg| Kabutozuka Kofun (Kyoto), 3D-2.jpg|Expanded view of the stone chamber Kabutozuka Kofun (Kyoto), genshitsu-1.jpg|Main chamber (towards the rear wall) Kabutozuka Kofun (Kyoto), genshitsu-2.jpg|Main chamber (towards the entrance) Kabutozuka Kofun (Kyoto), sendou-2.jpg|Corridor (towards the entrance) Kabutozuka Kofun (Kyoto), sendou-1.jpg|Corridor (towards the main chamber) Kabutozuka Kofun (Kyoto), funkyu.jpg|The Kofun == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|(References used in article compilation)}} * {{Cite book|language=ja|editor=|author=|year=1979|chapter=甲塚|title=京都市の地名|series=[[Japanese Historical Place names Series]] 27|publisher=[[Heibonsha]]|isbn=978-4-582-49027-5|ref={{Harvid|Kabutozuka (Heibonsha)|1979}}}} * {{Cite book|author=[[Kazuo Hirose]]|year=2013|chapter=Questions Regarding Yamashiro's Jazuka Kofun|title=Research Reports of the National Museum of Japanese History|volume=Vol. 178|publisher=[[National Museum of Japanese History]]|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Hirose Kazuo|2013}}}} == Related Reading == {{small|(Related literature not used in this article)}} * {{Cite journal|language=ja|author=|title=Detailed Measurement Diagram of Kabutozuka Kofun, Ukyo Ward|date=1964|journal=Trench|publisher=Kyoto University Archaeology Research Association|volume=|number=11|pages=|url=|naid=|doi=|ref=}} * {{Cite book|language=ja|editor=[[Kyoto University]] Archaeology Research Association|author=|year=1971|chapter=Overview of Kofuns > Kabutozuka Kofun|title=The Kofun built of Sagano - Excavation Report of the Mitogagaike Group of Kofuns|publisher=Kyoto University Archaeology Research Association Publication Office|isbn=|ref=}} * {{Cite book|language=ja|editor=Kyoto City|author=|year=1983|chapter=The Kofun built > Ukyo Ward|title=Historical Materials of Kyoto's History|volume=Vol. 2, Archaeology|publisher=[[Heibonsha]]|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Historical Materials of Kyoto Vol. 2|1983}}}} == External Links == * {{Commonscat-inline|Kabutozuka Kofun (Kyoto)}} The mound is associated with the [[Hata clan]], a powerful immigrant lineage that developed the Sagano area{{Sfn|Hirose Kazuo|2013}}. Today, the kofun site is preserved within private property. == burial == [[File:Kabutozuka Kofun (Kyoto), 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Bird’s eye view of the stone chamber}}]] The burial facility is a double-sleeved [[corridor-style stone chamber]] opening to the south. The area of the chamber are as follows{{Sfn|Hirose Kazuo|2013}}: * Total length: 14.4 meters * Main chamber (*gen-shitsu*): length 5.2 meters, width 2.9 meters, current height 3.4 meters * Corridor (*sen-dō*): length 9.2 meters, width 1.7 meters, current height 1.8 meters The rear wall of the main chamber is assumed to consist of two stacked layers of stones (its details are obscured by the presence of a shrine). The side walls of the main chamber consist of two stones stacked in two layers, while the front wall is a single layer. The chamber entrance consists of a single stone, and the corridor walls are two layers. The ceiling stones comprise three stones in the main chamber and four in the corridor{{Sfn|Hirose Kazuo|2013}}. Entrance to the stone chamber |alternate_name = Kabutozuka/Ōtsuka{{Sfn|Kabutozuka (Heibonsha)|1979}} |affiliation = |location = Sagakabutozuka-cho, [[Ukyo Ward, Kyoto|Ukyo Ward]], [[Kyoto]], [[Kyoto Prefecture]] |coordinates = 35|coordinatesutes = 1|coordinatesonds = 10.80 |coordinates = 135|coordinatesutes = 41|coordinatesonds = 24.85 |omitted = JP-26 |type = [[Round Kofun|Round mound (empun)]] |map_size = Diameter: over 38 m
Height: over 5.5 m |burial = Two-sleeved [[yokoana-style stone chamber|yokoana-style stone chamber]] |burial goods_found = |associated_burial_mounds = |built = Early [[7th century]] |person_buried = (Presumed) member of the [[Hata clan (Japan)|Hata clan]] |imperial_mausoleum = |historical_site = None |tangible_cultural_properties = |notable_features = |map = Japan Kyoto city |type = Empun |map_dot_label = Kabutozuka Kofun |map_dot_label = bottom }} '''Kabutozuka Kofun''' (甲塚古墳) is an ancient Kofun (kofun) located in Sagakabuto-cho, [[Ukyo Ward]], [[Kyoto City]], [[Kyoto Prefecture]]. The mound has a circular type and is not Historic Site1 as a historical site. == Overview == The Kabutozuka Kofun is a standalone, large circular Kofun built on the Sagano Plateau in the western part of the [[Kyoto Basin]]. The mound's name originates from its helmet-like appearance. Currently, a small shrine is enshrined within the inner chamber of the stone burial facility{{Sfn|Kabutozuka (Heibonsha)|1979}}. While the mound has suffered from looting, no typeal excavations have been conducted. The mound is circular in type, with an estimated diameter of 38 meters or more and a height exceeding 5.5 meters{{Sfn|Hirose Kazuo|2013}}. The burial facility is a double-sleeved [[corridor-style stone chamber]] (*yokoana-shiki sekishitsu*), which opens to the south. It is a large chamber measuring 14.4 meters in total length, constructed with massive stones. The funerary burial goods are not known{{Sfn|Kabutozuka (Heibonsha)|1979}}. The construction is estimated to date to the early 7th century, during the late Kofun period. It is considered one of the last Kofuns for regional chieftains in the Uzumasa-Sagano area, following the [[Jazuka Kofun]] and [[Maruyama Kofun]]. The mound is associated with the [[Hata clan]], a powerful immigrant lineage that developed the Sagano area{{Sfn|Hirose Kazuo|2013}}. Today, the kofun site is preserved within private property. == burial == [[File:Kabutozuka Kofun (Kyoto), 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Bird’s eye view of the stone chamber}}]] The burial facility is a double-sleeved [[corridor-style stone chamber]] opening to the south. The area of the chamber are as follows{{Sfn|Hirose Kazuo|2013}}: * Total length: 14.4 meters * Main chamber (*gen-shitsu*): length 5.2 meters, width 2.9 meters, current height 3.4 meters * Corridor (*sen-dō*): length 9.2 meters, width 1.7 meters, current height 1.8 meters The rear wall of the main chamber is assumed to consist of two stacked layers of stones (its details are obscured by the presence of a shrine). The side walls of the main chamber consist of two stones stacked in two layers, while the front wall is a single layer. The chamber entrance consists of a single stone, and the corridor walls are two layers. The ceiling stones comprise three stones in the main chamber and four in the corridor{{Sfn|Hirose Kazuo|2013}}. {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:01, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{infobox ancient site |name = Kabutozuka Kofun |image = [[File:Kabutozuka Kofun (Kyoto), entrance.jpg|280px]]
Entrance to the stone chamber |alternate_name = Kabutozuka/Ōtsuka{{Sfn|Kabutozuka (Heibonsha)|1979}} |affiliation = |location = Sagakabutozuka-cho, [[Ukyo Ward, Kyoto|Ukyo Ward]], [[Kyoto]], [[Kyoto Prefecture]] |coordinates = 35|coordinatesutes = 1|coordinatesonds = 10.80 |coordinates = 135|coordinatesutes = 41|coordinatesonds = 24.85 |omitted = JP-26 |type = [[Round Kofun|Round mound (empun)]] |map_size = Diameter: over 38 m
Height: over 5.5 m |burial = Two-sleeved [[yokoana-style stone chamber|yokoana-style stone chamber]] |burial goods_found = |associated_burial_mounds = |built = Early [[7th century]] |person_buried = (Presumed) member of the [[Hata clan (Japan)|Hata clan]] |imperial_mausoleum = |historical_site = None |tangible_cultural_properties = |notable_features = |map = Japan Kyoto city |type = Empun |map_dot_label = Kabutozuka Kofun |map_dot_label = bottom }} '''Kabutozuka Kofun''' (甲塚古墳) is an ancient Kofun (kofun) located in Sagakabuto-cho, [[Ukyo Ward]], [[Kyoto City]], [[Kyoto Prefecture]]. The mound has a circular type and is not Historic Site1 as a historical site. == Overview == The Kabutozuka Kofun is a standalone, large circular Kofun built on the Sagano Plateau in the western part of the [[Kyoto Basin]]. The mound's name originates from its helmet-like appearance. Currently, a small shrine is enshrined within the inner chamber of the stone burial facility{{Sfn|Kabutozuka (Heibonsha)|1979}}. While the mound has suffered from looting, no typeal excavations have been conducted. The mound is circular in type, with an estimated diameter of 38 meters or more and a height exceeding 5.5 meters{{Sfn|Hirose Kazuo|2013}}. The burial facility is a double-sleeved [[corridor-style stone chamber]] (*yokoana-shiki sekishitsu*), which opens to the south. It is a large chamber measuring 14.4 meters in total length, constructed with massive stones. The funerary burial goods are not known{{Sfn|Kabutozuka (Heibonsha)|1979}}. The construction is estimated to date to the early 7th century, during the late Kofun period. It is considered one of the last Kofuns for regional chieftains in the Uzumasa-Sagano area, following the [[Jazuka Kofun]] and [[Maruyama Kofun]]. The mound is associated with the [[Hata clan]], a powerful immigrant lineage that developed the Sagano area{{Sfn|Hirose Kazuo|2013}}. Today, the kofun site is preserved within private property. == burial == [[File:Kabutozuka Kofun (Kyoto), 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Bird’s eye view of the stone chamber}}]] The burial facility is a double-sleeved [[corridor-style stone chamber]] opening to the south. The area of the chamber are as follows{{Sfn|Hirose Kazuo|2013}}: * Total length: 14.4 meters * Main chamber (*gen-shitsu*): length 5.2 meters, width 2.9 meters, current height 3.4 meters * Corridor (*sen-dō*): length 9.2 meters, width 1.7 meters, current height 1.8 meters The rear wall of the main chamber is assumed to consist of two stacked layers of stones (its details are obscured by the presence of a shrine). The side walls of the main chamber consist of two stones stacked in two layers, while the front wall is a single layer. The chamber entrance consists of a single stone, and the corridor walls are two layers. The ceiling stones comprise three stones in the main chamber and four in the corridor{{Sfn|Hirose Kazuo|2013}}. File:Kabutozuka Kofun (Kyoto), 3D-P.jpg| Kabutozuka Kofun (Kyoto), 3D-2.jpg|Expanded view of the stone chamber Kabutozuka Kofun (Kyoto), genshitsu-1.jpg|Main chamber (towards the rear wall) Kabutozuka Kofun (Kyoto), genshitsu-2.jpg|Main chamber (towards the entrance) Kabutozuka Kofun (Kyoto), sendou-2.jpg|Corridor (towards the entrance) Kabutozuka Kofun (Kyoto), sendou-1.jpg|Corridor (towards the main chamber) Kabutozuka Kofun (Kyoto), funkyu.jpg|The Kofun == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|(References used in article compilation)}} * {{Cite book|language=ja|editor=|author=|year=1979|chapter=甲塚|title=京都市の地名|series=[[Japanese Historical Place names Series]] 27|publisher=[[Heibonsha]]|isbn=978-4-582-49027-5|ref={{Harvid|Kabutozuka (Heibonsha)|1979}}}} * {{Cite book|author=[[Kazuo Hirose]]|year=2013|chapter=Questions Regarding Yamashiro's Jazuka Kofun|title=Research Reports of the National Museum of Japanese History|volume=Vol. 178|publisher=[[National Museum of Japanese History]]|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Hirose Kazuo|2013}}}} == Related Reading == {{small|(Related literature not used in this article)}} * {{Cite journal|language=ja|author=|title=Detailed Measurement Diagram of Kabutozuka Kofun, Ukyo Ward|date=1964|journal=Trench|publisher=Kyoto University Archaeology Research Association|volume=|number=11|pages=|url=|naid=|doi=|ref=}} * {{Cite book|language=ja|editor=[[Kyoto University]] Archaeology Research Association|author=|year=1971|chapter=Overview of Kofuns > Kabutozuka Kofun|title=The Kofun built of Sagano - Excavation Report of the Mitogagaike Group of Kofuns|publisher=Kyoto University Archaeology Research Association Publication Office|isbn=|ref=}} * {{Cite book|language=ja|editor=Kyoto City|author=|year=1983|chapter=The Kofun built > Ukyo Ward|title=Historical Materials of Kyoto's History|volume=Vol. 2, Archaeology|publisher=[[Heibonsha]]|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Historical Materials of Kyoto Vol. 2|1983}}}} == External Links == * {{Commonscat-inline|Kabutozuka Kofun (Kyoto)}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Uzumasu-Sagano Kofuns]] [[Category:Ancient Kofuns in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Ukyo Ward]] [[Category:Circular Kofuns]] [[Category:Hata clan]] [[Category:Uzumasu]] [[Category:Kabutozuka Kofun (Kyoto)]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:tier 6]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:New2]] [[Category:pages which might possibly be incomplete but are most likely complete]] [[Category:I think I cleared all the incomplete or blank pages earlier]] }} [[sn:Kabutozuka Kofun (Kyoto)]] [[ja:甲塚古墳 (京都市)]] [[ja:甲塚古墳]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Kabutozuka Kofun (Kyoto)}}

Height: over 5.5 m |burial = Two-sleeved [[yokoana-style stone chamber|yokoana-style stone chamber]] |burial goods_found = |associated_burial_mounds = |built = Early [[7th century]] |person_buried = (Presumed) member of the [[Hata clan (Japan)|Hata clan]] |imperial_mausoleum = |historical_site = None |tangible_cultural_properties = |notable_features = |map = Japan Kyoto city |type = Empun |map_dot_label = Kabutozuka Kofun |map_dot_label = bottom }} '''Kabutozuka Kofun''' (甲塚古墳) is an ancient Kofun (kofun) located in Sagakabuto-cho, [[Ukyo Ward]], [[Kyoto City]], [[Kyoto Prefecture]]. The mound has a circular type and is not Historic Site1 as a historical site. == Overview == The Kabutozuka Kofun is a standalone, large circular Kofun built on the Sagano Plateau in the western part of the [[Kyoto Basin]]. The mound's name originates from its helmet-like appearance. Currently, a small shrine is enshrined within the inner chamber of the stone burial facility{{Sfn|Kabutozuka (Heibonsha)|1979}}. While the mound has suffered from looting, no typeal excavations have been conducted. The mound is circular in type, with an estimated diameter of 38 meters or more and a height exceeding 5.5 meters{{Sfn|Hirose Kazuo|2013}}. The burial facility is a double-sleeved [[corridor-style stone chamber]] (*yokoana-shiki sekishitsu*), which opens to the south. It is a large chamber measuring 14.4 meters in total length, constructed with massive stones. The funerary burial goods are not known{{Sfn|Kabutozuka (Heibonsha)|1979}}. The construction is estimated to date to the early 7th century, during the late Kofun period. It is considered one of the last Kofuns for regional chieftains in the Uzumasa-Sagano area, following the [[Jazuka Kofun]] and [[Maruyama Kofun]]. The mound is associated with the [[Hata clan]], a powerful immigrant lineage that developed the Sagano area{{Sfn|Hirose Kazuo|2013}}. Today, the kofun site is preserved within private property. == burial == [[File:Kabutozuka Kofun (Kyoto), 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Bird’s eye view of the stone chamber}}]] The burial facility is a double-sleeved [[corridor-style stone chamber]] opening to the south. The area of the chamber are as follows{{Sfn|Hirose Kazuo|2013}}: * Total length: 14.4 meters * Main chamber (*gen-shitsu*): length 5.2 meters, width 2.9 meters, current height 3.4 meters * Corridor (*sen-dō*): length 9.2 meters, width 1.7 meters, current height 1.8 meters The rear wall of the main chamber is assumed to consist of two stacked layers of stones (its details are obscured by the presence of a shrine). The side walls of the main chamber consist of two stones stacked in two layers, while the front wall is a single layer. The chamber entrance consists of a single stone, and the corridor walls are two layers. The ceiling stones comprise three stones in the main chamber and four in the corridor{{Sfn|Hirose Kazuo|2013}}. File:Kabutozuka Kofun (Kyoto), 3D-P.jpg| Kabutozuka Kofun (Kyoto), 3D-2.jpg|Expanded view of the stone chamber Kabutozuka Kofun (Kyoto), genshitsu-1.jpg|Main chamber (towards the rear wall) Kabutozuka Kofun (Kyoto), genshitsu-2.jpg|Main chamber (towards the entrance) Kabutozuka Kofun (Kyoto), sendou-2.jpg|Corridor (towards the entrance) Kabutozuka Kofun (Kyoto), sendou-1.jpg|Corridor (towards the main chamber) Kabutozuka Kofun (Kyoto), funkyu.jpg|The Kofun == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|(References used in article compilation)}} * {{Cite book|language=ja|editor=|author=|year=1979|chapter=甲塚|title=京都市の地名|series=[[Japanese Historical Place names Series]] 27|publisher=[[Heibonsha]]|isbn=978-4-582-49027-5|ref={{Harvid|Kabutozuka (Heibonsha)|1979}}}} * {{Cite book|author=[[Kazuo Hirose]]|year=2013|chapter=Questions Regarding Yamashiro's Jazuka Kofun|title=Research Reports of the National Museum of Japanese History|volume=Vol. 178|publisher=[[National Museum of Japanese History]]|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Hirose Kazuo|2013}}}} == Related Reading == {{small|(Related literature not used in this article)}} * {{Cite journal|language=ja|author=|title=Detailed Measurement Diagram of Kabutozuka Kofun, Ukyo Ward|date=1964|journal=Trench|publisher=Kyoto University Archaeology Research Association|volume=|number=11|pages=|url=|naid=|doi=|ref=}} * {{Cite book|language=ja|editor=[[Kyoto University]] Archaeology Research Association|author=|year=1971|chapter=Overview of Kofuns > Kabutozuka Kofun|title=The Kofun built of Sagano - Excavation Report of the Mitogagaike Group of Kofuns|publisher=Kyoto University Archaeology Research Association Publication Office|isbn=|ref=}} * {{Cite book|language=ja|editor=Kyoto City|author=|year=1983|chapter=The Kofun built > Ukyo Ward|title=Historical Materials of Kyoto's History|volume=Vol. 2, Archaeology|publisher=[[Heibonsha]]|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Historical Materials of Kyoto Vol. 2|1983}}}} == External Links == * {{Commonscat-inline|Kabutozuka Kofun (Kyoto)}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Uzumasu-Sagano Kofuns]] [[Category:Ancient Kofuns in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Ukyo Ward]] [[Category:Circular Kofuns]] [[Category:Hata clan]] [[Category:Uzumasu]] [[Category:Kabutozuka Kofun (Kyoto)]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:tier 6]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:New2]] [[Category:pages which might possibly be incomplete but are most likely complete]] [[Category:I think I cleared all the incomplete or blank pages earlier]] }} [[sn:Kabutozuka Kofun (Kyoto)]] [[ja:甲塚古墳 (京都市)]] [[ja:甲塚古墳]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Kabutozuka Kofun (Kyoto)}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{Kofun navbar}}

The mound has a circular type and is not Historic Site1 as a historical site. == Overview == The Kabutozuka Kofun is a standalone, large circular Kofun built on the Sagano Plateau in the western part of the [[Kyoto Basin]]. The chamber entrance consists of a single stone, and the corridor walls are two layers. The ceiling stones comprise three stones in the main chamber and four in the corridor{{Sfn|Hirose Kazuo|2013}}. The area of the chamber are as follows{{Sfn|Hirose Kazuo|2013}}: * Total length: 14.4 meters * Main chamber (*gen-shitsu*): length 5.2 meters, width 2.9 meters, current height 3.4 meters * Corridor (*sen-dō*): length 9.2 meters, width 1.7 meters, current height 1.8 meters The rear wall of the main chamber is assumed to consist of two stacked layers of stones (its details are obscured by the presence of a shrine). The side walls of the main chamber consist of two stones stacked in two layers, while the front wall is a single layer. The chamber entrance consists of a single stone, and the corridor walls are two layers. The ceiling stones comprise three stones in the main chamber and four in the corridor{{Sfn|Hirose Kazuo|2013}}. File:Kabutozuka Kofun (Kyoto), 3D-P.jpg| Kabutozuka Kofun (Kyoto), 3D-2.jpg|Expanded view of the stone chamber Kabutozuka Kofun (Kyoto), genshitsu-1.jpg|Main chamber (towards the rear wall) Kabutozuka Kofun (Kyoto), genshitsu-2.jpg|Main chamber (towards the entrance) Kabutozuka Kofun (Kyoto), sendou-2.jpg|Corridor (towards the entrance) Kabutozuka Kofun (Kyoto), sendou-1.jpg|Corridor (towards the main chamber) Kabutozuka Kofun (Kyoto), funkyu.jpg|The Kofun == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|(References used in article compilation)}} * {{Cite book|language=ja|editor=|author=|year=1979|chapter=甲塚|title=京都市の地名|series=[[Japanese Historical Place names Series]] 27|publisher=[[Heibonsha]]|isbn=978-4-582-49027-5|ref={{Harvid|Kabutozuka (Heibonsha)|1979}}}} * {{Cite book|author=[[Kazuo Hirose]]|year=2013|chapter=Questions Regarding Yamashiro's Jazuka Kofun|title=Research Reports of the National Museum of Japanese History|volume=Vol. 178|publisher=[[National Museum of Japanese History]]|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Hirose Kazuo|2013}}}} == Related Reading == {{small|(Related literature not used in this article)}} * {{Cite journal|language=ja|author=|title=Detailed Measurement Diagram of Kabutozuka Kofun, Ukyo Ward|date=1964|journal=Trench|publisher=Kyoto University Archaeology Research Association|volume=|number=11|pages=|url=|naid=|doi=|ref=}} * {{Cite book|language=ja|editor=[[Kyoto University]] Archaeology Research Association|author=|year=1971|chapter=Overview of Kofuns > Kabutozuka Kofun|title=The Kofun built of Sagano - {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:01, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{infobox ancient site |name = Kabutozuka Kofun |image = [[File:Kabutozuka Kofun (Kyoto), entrance.jpg|280px]]
Entrance to the stone chamber |alternate_name = Kabutozuka/Ōtsuka{{Sfn|Kabutozuka (Heibonsha)|1979}} |affiliation = |location = Sagakabutozuka-cho, [[Ukyo Ward, Kyoto|Ukyo Ward]], [[Kyoto]], [[Kyoto Prefecture]] |coordinates = 35|coordinatesutes = 1|coordinatesonds = 10.80 |coordinates = 135|coordinatesutes = 41|coordinatesonds = 24.85 |omitted = JP-26 |type = [[Round Kofun|Round mound (empun)]] |map_size = Diameter: over 38 m
Height: over 5.5 m |burial = Two-sleeved [[yokoana-style stone chamber|yokoana-style stone chamber]] |burial goods_found = |associated_burial_mounds = |built = Early [[7th century]] |person_buried = (Presumed) member of the [[Hata clan (Japan)|Hata clan]] |imperial_mausoleum = |historical_site = None |tangible_cultural_properties = |notable_features = |map = Japan Kyoto city |type = Empun |map_dot_label = Kabutozuka Kofun |map_dot_label = bottom }} '''Kabutozuka Kofun''' (甲塚古墳) is an ancient Kofun (kofun) located in Sagakabuto-cho, [[Ukyo Ward]], [[Kyoto City]], [[Kyoto Prefecture]]. The mound has a circular type and is not Historic Site1 as a historical site. == Overview == The Kabutozuka Kofun is a standalone, large circular Kofun built on the Sagano Plateau in the western part of the [[Kyoto Basin]]. The mound's name originates from its helmet-like appearance. Currently, a small shrine is enshrined within the inner chamber of the stone burial facility{{Sfn|Kabutozuka (Heibonsha)|1979}}. While the mound has suffered from looting, no typeal excavations have been conducted. The mound is circular in type, with an estimated diameter of 38 meters or more and a height exceeding 5.5 meters{{Sfn|Hirose Kazuo|2013}}. The burial facility is a double-sleeved [[corridor-style stone chamber]] (*yokoana-shiki sekishitsu*), which opens to the south. It is a large chamber measuring 14.4 meters in total length, constructed with massive stones. The funerary burial goods are not known{{Sfn|Kabutozuka (Heibonsha)|1979}}. The construction is estimated to date to the early 7th century, during the late Kofun period. It is considered one of the last Kofuns for regional chieftains in the Uzumasa-Sagano area, following the [[Jazuka Kofun]] and [[Maruyama Kofun]]. The mound is associated with the [[Hata clan]], a powerful immigrant lineage that developed the Sagano area{{Sfn|Hirose Kazuo|2013}}. Today, the kofun site is preserved within private property. == burial == [[File:Kabutozuka Kofun (Kyoto), 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Bird’s eye view of the stone chamber}}]] The burial facility is a double-sleeved [[corridor-style stone chamber]] opening to the south. The area of the chamber are as follows{{Sfn|Hirose Kazuo|2013}}: * Total length: 14.4 meters * Main chamber (*gen-shitsu*): length 5.2 meters, width 2.9 meters, current height 3.4 meters * Corridor (*sen-dō*): length 9.2 meters, width 1.7 meters, current height 1.8 meters The rear wall of the main chamber is assumed to consist of two stacked layers of stones (its details are obscured by the presence of a shrine). The side walls of the main chamber consist of two stones stacked in two layers, while the front wall is a single layer. The chamber entrance consists of a single stone, and the corridor walls are two layers. The ceiling stones comprise three stones in the main chamber and four in the corridor{{Sfn|Hirose Kazuo|2013}}. Excavation Report of the Mitogagaike Group of Kofuns|publisher=Kyoto University Archaeology Research Association Publication Office|isbn=|ref=}} * {{Cite book|language=ja|editor=Kyoto City|author=|year=1983|chapter=The Kofun built > Ukyo Ward|title=Historical Materials of Kyoto's History|volume=Vol. 2, Archaeology|publisher=[[Heibonsha]]|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Historical Materials of Kyoto Vol. 2|1983}}}} == External Links == * {{Commonscat-inline|Kabutozuka Kofun (Kyoto)}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| }} [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kabutozuka Kofun (Kyoto)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kabutozuka Kofun (Kyoto)]][[Category:qq]] {{draft categories| [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Uzumasu-Sagano Kofuns]] [[Category:Ancient Kofuns in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Ukyo Ward]] [[Category:Circular Kofuns]] [[Category:Hata clan]] [[Category:Uzumasu]] [[Category:Kabutozuka Kofun (Kyoto)]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:New2]] [[Category:pages which might possibly be incomplete but are most likely complete]] [[Category:I think I cleared all the incomplete or blank pages earlier]] }} [[sn:Kabutozuka Kofun (Kyoto)]] [[ja:甲塚古墳 (京都市)]] [[ja:甲塚古墳]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Kabutozuka Kofun (Kyoto)}}

Entrance to the stone chamber |alternate_name = Kabutozuka/Ōtsuka{{Sfn|Kabutozuka (Heibonsha)|1979}} |affiliation = |location = Sagakabutozuka-cho, [[Ukyo Ward, Kyoto|Ukyo Ward]], [[Kyoto]], [[Kyoto Prefecture]] |coordinates = 35|coordinatesutes = 1|coordinatesonds = 10.80 |coordinates = 135|coordinatesutes = 41|coordinatesonds = 24.85 |omitted = JP-26 |type = [[Round Kofun|Round mound (empun)]] |map_size = Diameter: over 38 m
Height: over 5.5 m |burial = Two-sleeved [[yokoana-style stone chamber|yokoana-style stone chamber]] |burial goods_found = |associated_burial_mounds = |built = Early [[7th century]] |person_buried = (Presumed) member of the [[Hata clan (Japan)|Hata clan]] |imperial_mausoleum = |historical_site = None |tangible_cultural_properties = |notable_features = |map = Japan Kyoto city |type = Empun |map_dot_label = Kabutozuka Kofun |map_dot_label = bottom }} '''Kabutozuka Kofun''' (甲塚古墳) is an ancient Kofun (kofun) located in Sagakabuto-cho, [[Ukyo Ward]], [[Kyoto City]], [[Kyoto Prefecture]]. The mound has a circular type and is not Historic Site1 as a historical site. == Overview == The Kabutozuka Kofun is a standalone, large circular Kofun built on the Sagano Plateau in the western part of the [[Kyoto Basin]]. The mound's name originates from its helmet-like appearance. Currently, a small shrine is enshrined within the inner chamber of the stone burial facility{{Sfn|Kabutozuka (Heibonsha)|1979}}. While the mound has suffered from looting, no typeal excavations have been conducted. The mound is circular in type, with an estimated diameter of 38 meters or more and a height exceeding 5.5 meters{{Sfn|Hirose Kazuo|2013}}. The burial facility is a double-sleeved [[corridor-style stone chamber]] (*yokoana-shiki sekishitsu*), which opens to the south. It is a large chamber measuring 14.4 meters in total length, constructed with massive stones. The funerary burial goods are not known{{Sfn|Kabutozuka (Heibonsha)|1979}}. The construction is estimated to date to the early 7th century, during the late Kofun period. It is considered one of the last Kofuns for regional chieftains in the Uzumasa-Sagano area, following the [[Jazuka Kofun]] and [[Maruyama Kofun]]. The mound is associated with the [[Hata clan]], a powerful immigrant lineage that developed the Sagano area{{Sfn|Hirose Kazuo|2013}}. Today, the kofun site is preserved within private property. == burial == [[File:Kabutozuka Kofun (Kyoto), 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Bird’s eye view of the stone chamber}}]] The burial facility is a double-sleeved [[corridor-style stone chamber]] opening to the south. The area of the chamber are as follows{{Sfn|Hirose Kazuo|2013}}: * Total length: 14.4 meters * Main chamber (*gen-shitsu*): length 5.2 meters, width 2.9 meters, current height 3.4 meters * Corridor (*sen-dō*): length 9.2 meters, width 1.7 meters, current height 1.8 meters The rear wall of the main chamber is assumed to consist of two stacked layers of stones (its details are obscured by the presence of a shrine). The side walls of the main chamber consist of two stones stacked in two layers, while the front wall is a single layer. The chamber entrance consists of a single stone, and the corridor walls are two layers. The ceiling stones comprise three stones in the main chamber and four in the corridor{{Sfn|Hirose Kazuo|2013}}. File:Kabutozuka Kofun (Kyoto), 3D-P.jpg| Kabutozuka Kofun (Kyoto), 3D-2.jpg|Expanded view of the stone chamber Kabutozuka Kofun (Kyoto), genshitsu-1.jpg|Main chamber (towards the rear wall) Kabutozuka Kofun (Kyoto), genshitsu-2.jpg|Main chamber (towards the entrance) Kabutozuka Kofun (Kyoto), sendou-2.jpg|Corridor (towards the entrance) Kabutozuka Kofun (Kyoto), sendou-1.jpg|Corridor (towards the main chamber) Kabutozuka Kofun (Kyoto), funkyu.jpg|The Kofun == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|(References used in article compilation)}} * {{Cite book|language=ja|editor=|author=|year=1979|chapter=甲塚|title=京都市の地名|series=[[Japanese Historical Place names Series]] 27|publisher=[[Heibonsha]]|isbn=978-4-582-49027-5|ref={{Harvid|Kabutozuka (Heibonsha)|1979}}}} * {{Cite book|author=[[Kazuo Hirose]]|year=2013|chapter=Questions Regarding Yamashiro's Jazuka Kofun|title=Research Reports of the National Museum of Japanese History|volume=Vol. 178|publisher=[[National Museum of Japanese History]]|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Hirose Kazuo|2013}}}} == Related Reading == {{small|(Related literature not used in this article)}} * {{Cite journal|language=ja|author=|title=Detailed Measurement Diagram of Kabutozuka Kofun, Ukyo Ward|date=1964|journal=Trench|publisher=Kyoto University Archaeology Research Association|volume=|number=11|pages=|url=|naid=|doi=|ref=}} * {{Cite book|language=ja|editor=[[Kyoto University]] Archaeology Research Association|author=|year=1971|chapter=Overview of Kofuns > Kabutozuka Kofun|title=The Kofun built of Sagano - Excavation Report of the Mitogagaike Group of Kofuns|publisher=Kyoto University Archaeology Research Association Publication Office|isbn=|ref=}} * {{Cite book|language=ja|editor=Kyoto City|author=|year=1983|chapter=The Kofun built > Ukyo Ward|title=Historical Materials of Kyoto's History|volume=Vol. 2, Archaeology|publisher=[[Heibonsha]]|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Historical Materials of Kyoto Vol. 2|1983}}}} == External Links == * {{Commonscat-inline|Kabutozuka Kofun (Kyoto)}} {{draft categories| }} [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kabutozuka Kofun (Kyoto)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kabutozuka Kofun (Kyoto)]][[Category:qq]] {{draft categories| [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Uzumasu-Sagano Kofuns]] [[Category:Ancient Kofuns in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Ukyo Ward]] [[Category:Circular Kofuns]] [[Category:Hata clan]] [[Category:Uzumasu]] [[Category:Kabutozuka Kofun (Kyoto)]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:New2]] [[Category:pages which might possibly be incomplete but are most likely complete]] [[Category:I think I cleared all the incomplete or blank pages earlier]] }} [[sn:Kabutozuka Kofun (Kyoto)]] [[ja:甲塚古墳 (京都市)]] [[ja:甲塚古墳]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Kabutozuka Kofun (Kyoto)}}

Entrance to the stone chamber |alternate_name = Kabutozuka/Ōtsuka{{Sfn|Kabutozuka (Heibonsha)|1979}} |affiliation = |location = Sagakabutozuka-cho, [[Ukyo Ward, Kyoto|Ukyo Ward]], [[Kyoto]], [[Kyoto Prefecture]] |coordinates = 35|coordinatesutes = 1|coordinatesonds = 10.80 |coordinates = 135|coordinatesutes = 41|coordinatesonds = 24.85 |omitted = JP-26 |type = [[Round Kofun|Round mound (empun)]] |map_size = Diameter: over 38 m
Height: over 5.5 m |burial = Two-sleeved [[yokoana-style stone chamber|yokoana-style stone chamber]] |burial goods_found = |associated_burial_mounds = |built = Early [[7th century]] |person_buried = (Presumed) member of the [[Hata clan (Japan)|Hata clan]] |imperial_mausoleum = |historical_site = None |tangible_cultural_properties = |notable_features = |map = Japan Kyoto city |type = Empun |map_dot_label = Kabutozuka Kofun |map_dot_label = bottom }} '''Kabutozuka Kofun''' (甲塚古墳) is an ancient Kofun (kofun) located in Sagakabuto-cho, [[Ukyo Ward]], [[Kyoto City]], [[Kyoto Prefecture]]. The mound has a circular type and is not Historic Site1 as a historical site. == Overview == The Kabutozuka Kofun is a standalone, large circular Kofun built on the Sagano Plateau in the western part of the [[Kyoto Basin]]. The mound's name originates from its helmet-like appearance. Currently, a small shrine is enshrined within the inner chamber of the stone burial facility{{Sfn|Kabutozuka (Heibonsha)|1979}}. While the mound has suffered from looting, no typeal excavations have been conducted. The mound is circular in type, with an estimated diameter of 38 meters or more and a height exceeding 5.5 meters{{Sfn|Hirose Kazuo|2013}}. The burial facility is a double-sleeved [[corridor-style stone chamber]] (*yokoana-shiki sekishitsu*), which opens to the south. It is a large chamber measuring 14.4 meters in total length, constructed with massive stones. The funerary burial goods are not known{{Sfn|Kabutozuka (Heibonsha)|1979}}. The construction is estimated to date to the early 7th century, during the late Kofun period. It is considered one of the last Kofuns for regional chieftains in the Uzumasa-Sagano area, following the [[Jazuka Kofun]] and [[Maruyama Kofun]]. The mound is associated with the [[Hata clan]], a powerful immigrant lineage that developed the Sagano area{{Sfn|Hirose Kazuo|2013}}. Today, the kofun site is preserved within private property. == burial == [[File:Kabutozuka Kofun (Kyoto), 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Bird’s eye view of the stone chamber}}]] The burial facility is a double-sleeved [[corridor-style stone chamber]] opening to the south. The area of the chamber are as follows{{Sfn|Hirose Kazuo|2013}}: * Total length: 14.4 meters * Main chamber (*gen-shitsu*): length 5.2 meters, width 2.9 meters, current height 3.4 meters * Corridor (*sen-dō*): length 9.2 meters, width 1.7 meters, current height 1.8 meters The rear wall of the main chamber is assumed to consist of two stacked layers of stones (its details are obscured by the presence of a shrine). The side walls of the main chamber consist of two stones stacked in two layers, while the front wall is a single layer. The chamber entrance consists of a single stone, and the corridor walls are two layers. The ceiling stones comprise three stones in the main chamber and four in the corridor{{Sfn|Hirose Kazuo|2013}}. File:Kabutozuka Kofun (Kyoto), 3D-P.jpg| Kabutozuka Kofun (Kyoto), 3D-2.jpg|Expanded view of the stone chamber Kabutozuka Kofun (Kyoto), genshitsu-1.jpg|Main chamber (towards the rear wall) Kabutozuka Kofun (Kyoto), genshitsu-2.jpg|Main chamber (towards the entrance) Kabutozuka Kofun (Kyoto), sendou-2.jpg|Corridor (towards the entrance) Kabutozuka Kofun (Kyoto), sendou-1.jpg|Corridor (towards the main chamber) Kabutozuka Kofun (Kyoto), funkyu.jpg|The Kofun == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|(References used in article compilation)}} * {{Cite book|language=ja|editor=|author=|year=1979|chapter=甲塚|title=京都市の地名|series=[[Japanese Historical Place names Series]] 27|publisher=[[Heibonsha]]|isbn=978-4-582-49027-5|ref={{Harvid|Kabutozuka (Heibonsha)|1979}}}} * {{Cite book|author=[[Kazuo Hirose]]|year=2013|chapter=Questions Regarding Yamashiro's Jazuka Kofun|title=Research Reports of the National Museum of Japanese History|volume=Vol. 178|publisher=[[National Museum of Japanese History]]|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Hirose Kazuo|2013}}}} == Related Reading == {{small|(Related literature not used in this article)}} * {{Cite journal|language=ja|author=|title=Detailed Measurement Diagram of Kabutozuka Kofun, Ukyo Ward|date=1964|journal=Trench|publisher=Kyoto University Archaeology Research Association|volume=|number=11|pages=|url=|naid=|doi=|ref=}} * {{Cite book|language=ja|editor=[[Kyoto University]] Archaeology Research Association|author=|year=1971|chapter=Overview of Kofuns > Kabutozuka Kofun|title=The Kofun built of Sagano - Excavation Report of the Mitogagaike Group of Kofuns|publisher=Kyoto University Archaeology Research Association Publication Office|isbn=|ref=}} * {{Cite book|language=ja|editor=Kyoto City|author=|year=1983|chapter=The Kofun built > Ukyo Ward|title=Historical Materials of Kyoto's History|volume=Vol. 2, Archaeology|publisher=[[Heibonsha]]|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Historical Materials of Kyoto Vol. 2|1983}}}} == External Links == * {{Commonscat-inline|Kabutozuka Kofun (Kyoto)}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| }} [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kabutozuka Kofun (Kyoto)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kabutozuka Kofun (Kyoto)]][[Category:qq]] {{draft categories| [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Uzumasu-Sagano Kofuns]] [[Category:Ancient Kofuns in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Ukyo Ward]] [[Category:Circular Kofuns]] [[Category:Hata clan]] [[Category:Uzumasu]] [[Category:Kabutozuka Kofun (Kyoto)]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:New2]] [[Category:pages which might possibly be incomplete but are most likely complete]] [[Category:I think I cleared all the incomplete or blank pages earlier]] [[Category:qq]]{{Kofun navbar}}[[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Kabutozuka Kofun (Kyoto)]] [[ja:甲塚古墳 (京都市)]] [[ja:甲塚古墳]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Kabutozuka Kofun (Kyoto)}}
